title: Worked for a day or two
body: We bought this specifically because it said you could leave it plugged in and it wouldn't turn off automatically. That part seems to work. We have it in the office and my wife connected her laptop to it. It worked great. She closes her laptop when she's done working, and after an hour or two comes back and the speaker is still on and reconnects to the laptop when you wake it from sleep.Then after a day or two, opening her laptop in the morning and the speaker won't play music from the laptop. The laptop shows a connection to the speaker and the speaker is the default output device. The music is playing but no sound on the speaker. The speaker's light is blue and steady, but kind of pulsing. She disconnects the bluetooth connection and reconnects to the speaker -- nothing. She disables bluetooth on the laptop and re-enables it -- nothing. Finally, I try pulling the charging plug out of the speaker and turning it off -- the blue light goes out. Then it won't turn on when I press or hold the power button again. Finally after multiple tries, the power button turns on the speaker and the blue light begins blinking. Now it connects and plays music.This same exact process occurred the next night after sitting plugged in and on overnight when my wife returned to her laptop in the morning after having successfully played music on the speaker the day before.We specifically wanted a speaker that could just sit, plugged in, and automatically connect to her laptop whenever she wanted to play music. Unfortunately, despite the product's description that it can do this, it seems to have some defect that causes it to "lock up" when sitting plugged in and disconnected for long periods of time (like overnight).When I originally opened this package, I now remember that the triangular cardboard packaging for the speaker itself (not the shipping box) was partially opened as if it had been repacked into this packaging, so I'm wondering if this one had been returned and simply sent back out again because it didn't show any signs of problems, which it doesn't initially. It does *appear* to work -- until it's left overnight plugged in and not connected to any bluetooth. Then it locks up. When it happens two nights in a row, it's defective. Sending this one back and will try a different brand.

title: After 3 months, won't charge, flimsy USB port is mangled.  Replacement, pretty much the same.
body: I bought this in July, and was initially very pleased with the sound, either cabled or bluetooth, the portability, and toughness/waterproof characteristics. So, after a few months, scratch the "toughness" aspect, because like others have noted, and with SO many other products out there, the USB port is mechanically flimsy and after repeated use fatigues and fails. Why Cambridge Soundworks, like so many other manufacturers, insist on employing USB when all it's used for is power, is beyond me. Great sound and battery life for the price, but if it's only going to last 3 months I would expect Cambridge Soundworks to take some heat for the number of these things that will be littering the landfills in short order.EDIT: From 1 Star, to 3. Based entirely on the response and support from Cambridge Soundworks. (see the comments to this review). Cambridge Soundworks acted quickly to this issue, and offered a replacement unit. I received it less than a week ago. 3-Stars from 1 is a good bump. But the replacement unit has the same somewhat flimsy USB port for charging the unit, so I'm withholding a better rating until I see how this one holds up. I also asked about disposal of the original but haven't rec'd. an answer to that question; if these thing are basically disposable that would be horrific, since I'm pretty sure that whatever these are made of has no business being in a landfill or the oceans.Second EDIT:The replacement that Cambridge Soundworks sent worked as well as the first, and the ONLY reason it's still working is because I RARELY disconnect the USB cable (not very convenient for a "portable" device, is it?) because of the ridiculously flimsy and easily wrecked micro USB connection to the speaker. The one on the replacement is getting very close to being useless, and I have to be VERY careful when plugging it in in order for the cable connector to seat and not bend the receiving port connection on the speaker. The bottom line is this thing just won't last. Even normal but careful use of the device will result in it being wrecked in far too short a use period. Back to one star. Sorry, Cambridge Soundworks.

title: Good sound for a small unit, but some VERY annoying design flaws and a FATAL security flaw! DO NOT BUY!
body: Good sound for a such a small unit, certainly much better than for example laptop speakers. That said, while it came with (short) cables, there was no charger so you need to supply your own (minimum 1A.) It also includes a speakerphone feature - I almost wish it didn't! The problem is that when your phone pairs, by default it goes into speakerphone mode, rings over the speaker, etc. As I use this at my office desk, that's HIGHLY undesirable, and there is no way to disable this feature on the device. Worse, the ringer tones are LOUD even when the phone and device is set to a low volume.As seen in the picture, the controls are on the rubber endcap. This is good and bad - the bad is that when the device is sitting as shown in the product picture, you need two hands to press a button or the unit just slides on the desk. Because of this, I run it on it's side so the controls are on the top. Also, as shown in the picture, the buttons are just raised printing - black on black. You can't see them unless the light is Just Right. The symbols should be painted white or something. The power button is on the edge right next to the port cover flap and is hard to press (two handed operation no matter what orientation.) The controls are: Power, Bluetooth pairing, Volume up and down, Play/Pause. There is no Next or Back which would be VERY useful to have (other devices have these.)The stars docked are due to the missing charger, the poor button placement / usage and visibility, and undesirable speakerphone mode that can NOT be disabled.Edit: I had to knock ANOTHER star off as this thing is ALWAYS in Bluetooth pairing mode meaning that ANYONE can pair with this device even if you don't want them to and there is NOTHING you can do to stop it. Had someone else somewhere in the building pair to it and start blasting crap. ALSO, since it does have a microphone, that means that anyone can SPY ON YOU without your knowledge!This product is going in the trash. VERY VERY disappointed! For SHAME Cambridge Soundworks!

title: Review of a few top sellers
body: So I bought this based on reviews, using iTunes on an iOs device, in all bought the Cambridge SoundWorks Oontz, Anker Soundcore, Anker Portable Bluetooth Speaker, and Klipsch Gig, to see which really had the best sound. Intend to keep the best of the 4. I already own the Bose Soundlink Mini II, but looking for another to add to house. Received everything as expected, carefully opened each and started to get them all powered up. I rate them from best to worst, based on sound and ease of use. I listen to music 75% of my day.1. Klipsch GIG- Decent design, excellent sound, easy power up and connections over bluetooth, don't care for the Knob on top, and the power switch being on the end of unit, also the charge port is there, so when charging the stand or belt seems in the way, but is manageable. Beats out the others as far as sound quality, provides great room filling sound, and when more than 10 feet away still sounds great.2. Anker Soundcore- Excellent design, sharp looking, soft rubber coating, easy to power up, easily connects, nice soft buttons on top to control source. I have to say this is my favorite design of the 4, sound quality is great, but if the speaker is not facing you directly you can't hear everything. Would be great for an office, the sound is still 3 times better than both of the last units. Just don't care for the one directional sound. If on a budget would be a great speaker.3. Anker Portable Bluetooth- Nice design, small square, don't like that sound directed straight up, powers up quick, connects quick, on/off switch instead of soft button like others, but easy to use. Sound definitely better than the OontZ, but not by much, to me sounds like my phone in a coke can.4. OontZ Angle 3- decent design, super light, powers up quick and connects easily, but sound is poor, and empty, and cheap, compared to others

title: Nearly perfect, but falls short with accessory cables
body: ****Update: Sadly the speaker stopped working with occasional use. I'd spend more money on a different one. Live and learn. ***Just received the Oontz Angle 3 today, so I'll come back and leave a follow up in a few months but my first impression is as follows.Packaging is typical plain box with not too much extra junk thrown in, thankfully.Inside the box is the speaker wrapped in plastic3.5mm Audio cableUSB charging cableThe speaker itself has a quality feel to its construction. The buttons are well marked and work easily. There is a plastic cover over the charging and audio jack that fits snugly enough no water would enter with any normal splash.The speaker is simple to connect with blue tooth and worked seamlessly. The sound is really impressive for such a small speaker. Nice tones and range. It gets plenty loud to fill a room and is clear as can be.The two cons are the charger and more importantly the audio cable. Are you kidding me. It's like 22" long. Not enough to hook up to a desktop pc and place it anywhere but on top of the pc itself. If it's on top of your desk that's fine, but if it sits under your desk it will not work. What an accessory design flaw! So, now I'll have to spend another 5 bucks to get a reasonably length cable. I'd have preferred paying a little more initially and gotten a more appropriate length cable. The charger is only 10" in length so it has to sit right next to your charger or pc.Other than that I'm thrilled with the product. I'd have given it the full 5 if not for the short cables. If you don't care about audio cable length I highly recommend it, if you care about cable length be sure to order a cable with your speaker.

title: JUNK X2!!!’ straight out of the box.-UPDATED11/19..UPDATED AGAIN! 11/21
body: 1 star=Out of the box it was static-y, especially when plugged in to power.I have a contact request to the seller, I’m assuming this will be handled and I will be editing this review. Or let’s hope!UPDATE——-3 starI was immediately contacted back by the customer service and I have returned the first unit and the other unit replacing this is on its way. I hope it sounds as good as everybody says, their customer service is an example to hold up to all other retailers.It is unbelievably awesome.I will update completely when the new item has been received.They also offer you 30% off to buy another one when you return the first.#2 UPDATE- 1 starReplacement product is WAY WORSE than original static issue. This unit has MORE static and it randomly shuts off my "Dot" and makes the unit restart in the middle of a song. Piece of crap.This is my summation: they tell you the units are damaged in "shipping" which is not true, but oit sounds believable.. they are cheap crappy units and this is how they handle all of the returns...acting as if this is a rare occurrence. It is a business decision, they really don't care about the quality of the product they are shipping...its all a numbers game, sell a ton, and eat the crappy ones that come back..30% discount does not mean crap if it is crap to start.I am putting a stop on my am ex for both units I ordered, if they want them, they can come and get them. Why should I use my labor for their crap?they need to rename their company to Cambridge SoundDoesntWorks!No wonder they have great customer service...they have to to stay alive. it is all dependent on selling more crap.

title: Buyer Beware - You Get What You Pay For!
body: I don't buy a lot as a prime member. Unfortunately what I thought was going to be a great product for listening to music before going to bed, wasn't. Perhaps the adage: "you get what you pay for" is true! I even bought the 3 yr product warranty. Hopefully the following message I sent directly to Cambridge SoundWorks will help me and then I'll be able to write a better product review than the following:I was pleasantly surprised how nice the Oontz angle3 sounded. However, I have a serious problem with this purchase and want to return it for credit, or at least a new one that actually works! I listened to music for a couple of hours at a time, for less than a dozen times since I bought it less than 90 days ago. When it stopped working yesterday I used the cell phone type power plug and it would play for just a few minutes then shut-off. I thought something was wrong with our Bluetooth, but other devices work fine. Then I plugged an audio cable into my Galaxy Note 4 direct to the OontZ Angle 3 (like I use to do when listening to my transistor radio as a kid) and absolutely nothing happens. When checking Amazon about returning product, I didn't see how I could do so. I did buy the 3 yr product warranty. Can we use that to get me a new OontZ that actually works? If not, guess I'll have to tell my story on social media about my experience with your product and find someone else to work with!How come the extra square trade product warranty for 3 yrs doesn't cover my purchase? What good is it buying an extra warranty if amazon won't honor it? My speaker didn't last a month! Buyer beware!

title: If you purchase this, be sure to buy an additional warranty.
body: I purchased this item in February 2017 and less than one year later, it will not charge. The manufacturer states on their website: Who is not covered:Those who have either purchased or received an OontZ product that was sold as new or refurbished from an unauthorized retailer including but not limited to websites such as Ebay.com, actions sites or sales by surplus or bulk resellers are not covered by the limited product warranty. For more information regarding the limitations on our warranty, please refer to the section “What is not covered” down below. In addition, the warranty is for 90 days only and here is more of What is not covered from their website:This warranty is limited and not applicable to damages, defects, malfunction or failure to meet performance specifications resulting from:1.Normal wear and tear2.Misuse of product, neglect, abuse, improper handling or accident3.Product service, testing, adjustment, installment, maintenance, and alteration including software changes or hardware changes made by someone other than SoundWorks, Inc.4.Non-compliance with instructions given by SoundWorks, Inc. including, but not limited to, improper use of an electric source or other operator error5.Damage or loss during shipment or transit6.External causes such as floods, storms, fires, earthquakes, sunlight exposure, acts of God, weather, moisture, sand, dirt, electrical surges, battery leakage, vibration, corrosive environments or damage caused by the connection to other productsIn conclusion, there is NO WARRANTY; at least that is how I understand it.

title: No better than your phone's speakers
body: This Cambridge Oontz angle 3 is simply an awful sounding bluetooth speaker. The bottom line is that regardless of price, this speaker sounds terrible and isn't even really an upgrade from most built in phone speakers. The sound is tinny, screechy, with no audible bass or midbass to speak of. Literally hard to stand listening to for more than a few minutes, the sound is that bad. It vibrates on the surface you put it on, but unless you're six inches away, you won't hear a lick of bass out of this thing. After getting suckered into buying this cheapo speaker, I don't understand why people think this sounds good and worse yet, why they think for $25 you'll get a speaker worth listening to. You simply will not, and I fell victim to this as well. I returned this speaker after a couple of weeks of my ears suffering. Life is too short to listen to crappy speakers like this. Do yourself, your ears, and those around you a favor and spend more to get a solid sounding speaker. Personally, I think around $100 is the minimum I'll spend to get a good sounding bluetooth speaker, anything less and you'll mostly get junk or refurbed units. Some good sounding bluetooth speakers actually worth listening to for around $100 include: Klipsch KMC1, Klipsch Gig, Klipsch Groove, Bose Soundlink Color, JBL Charge. JBL UE roll, JBL clip. Many of these speakers are open box or refurb for under $100, but still blow the pants off this Oontz speaker. The only speaker I have found listenable under $30 is the Philips Shoqbox mini, and even that is barely tollerable to me, but much better than this Cambridge speaker.

title: wow, I wasn't expecting this! To well, this sucks.
body: I bought this on a whim really because I had been looking for a Bluetooth speaker and it was a lightening deal, so I sprung on it. I got to say I'm VERY impressed with this little gadget. The design of this is great but the bass does vary depending what surface it's on so keep that in mind when you do play music with lots of bass. It sounds fantastic on my kitchen counter but once I move it to a more dense surface the bass isn't quite as strong but it still sounds great. Overall, like I said, i'm very impressed with the sound quality of this little triangular box! I prefer it over the speakers on my tv and it'll be a great addition to use when I skype with my husband overseas, that way the kids will be able to hear him better and I can hear his voice over their excitement. We have a decent sized house and it allows the sound to travel down into the hallway so the range is fantasic. If you're looking for a good speaker i'd recommend this one, especially if you're a newbie at these like I am, you'll be very surprised with the sound quality. I've only had this for a few days so if things change i'll come back with an update but as of now, very happy with the purchase and design of this product.UPDATE 4/30/16 - this speaker has now died on me. I got it in December, wrote the top review and now changed it. Its a great speaker, when it works, but like others have mentioned, it crapped out on me. No damage to it, charge won't hold and constantly disconnect even after its been charged. Will no longer recommend this one.

title: Disappointing
body: I'm quite disappointed after reading many of the positive reviews. I expected a lot better from Cambridge. But perhaps my hopes were too ridiculous considering the low price point. -- I am looking for a cool bluetooth speaker with good sound to use as a giveaway for a charity golf event. I saw this one and it's big brother on several lists of "best" bluetooth speakers and saw its top seller status here on Amazon. Then I went to the manufacturer's site and saw that they have a program for buying these in bulk with a custom logo for exactly my intended use. So I bought one here so I could determine the quality for myself, and I honestly had high expectations. Let me be clear that it doesn't sound BAD by any means. But it just doesn't sound complete. There is no bass. I have no idea what people are comparing it to who say it delivers bass.For reference purposes, my normal go-to bluetooth speaker is a JBL Charge 3, which I think delivers great, complete sound with good bass. No, I'm not trying to compare this product to the JBL which is a lot more expensive -- I just mentioned it so you'll understand my frame of reference for saying the Angle 3 doesn't have any bass. If you've heard the JBL Charge 3, the Angle 3 is not even in the ballpark. Based on the reviews, I was hoping maybe the Angle 3 would be half as good. Hence my disappointment.I might give the Angle 3 PLUS a shot since it is larger and purports to have better drivers, but I'm no longer holding my breath.

title: Why is this getting such good reviews?! Much better for the money!
body: I needed a small blue tooth speaker because the Echo Dot sound quality just wasn't cutting it and i would like to add music to whatever room I am working in that may not have a device. I have an Echo Dot 2nd and 3rd gen. The 2nd gen is tinny and the 3rd way too bass centric and boomy, but still better than this speaker. The sound quality doesn't blow me away, in fact, it is pretty bad. Not enough bass and not much of an improvement over my Echo Dot Mini 2nd Gen. Wow, this is so disappointing. The speaker paired easily with my phone and laptop, but had an issue pairing with the Alexa app via blue tooth. The controls are a pain to see in low light. As long as you keep your expectations super low you will be happy.----Update-----Lost another star after buying the Doss Touch speaker for $4 more. The Doss has MUCH better sound quality and paired to Echo Dot instantly. The Oontz seems to be having difficulties with Alexa. Probably going to return this and buy the upgraded version of the Doss 20W because I am much more impressed with that speaker as a whole. The sound quality on the Doss is more balanced and it has True Wireless Stereo so both can be used simultaneously. At first it was about being an upgrade to the Echo Dot sound quality, but now it's about better sound with every device. I was never happy with the sound on the Oontz to begin with and now it's completely unacceptable after trying a much better speaker within the same price range.

title: At least it won't roll off the desk....
body: I really wanted to like this. I've had great success with previous CS products and thought this was a no-brainer. Unfortunately this one's a dud...I've given it to my 7 year old to use with her tablet as she couldn't care less about sound quality.For me sound quality trumps volume or bass, and while the Angle 3 can get fairly loud for it's size it has neither of the other attributes. Highs are relatively clear but mids are beyond muddy and there is really no bass to speak of. Max volume exacerbates the problem and there is also some distortion unless the piece being played is a single instrument, IE classical piano or guitar. Rock/Metal just sounds plain awful. Also, while being advertised as "stereo," you'd never know it-the tiny speakers are just too close together to allow for any true separation.On the positive side, build quality seems good and pairing is simple and trouble-free.Edit......2 weeks after writing the above review bluetooth stopped working. The speaker wasn't mishandled or abused and in fact never left it's spot on my daughter's dresser. She wasn't happy about having to have it plugged in to her tablet but put up with it until a few days later the charge port failed. So, scratch what I said about build quality. The Oontz met it's demise at my next visit to the shooting range and my little girl is enjoying her far superior JBL product.

title: STOPPED WORKING ALL OF A SUDDEN
body: I'm the type of person that has to have music in everything I do. I can take this speaker with me wherever I go whether I'm washing dishes or out in the garage doing work. I had a Polaroid bluetooth speaker box that I bought from Big Lots for $15. It was ok, but the cell phone battery that powers it, ended up going out. I did my research and this speaker had the best reviews for the price range I was looking for in a good speaker. I subscribe to Pandora, and if this product allowed you to switch to the next song, it would have gotten a 5. It's plenty loud and smaller than what I expected. I was initially disappointed when I saw the size, but after turning it on, I was even more pleased to know that a speaker this small could put out this sound! I would definitely recommend this product. The true test will be how long it lasts without being plugged in.UPDATE AFTER ONLY 2 WEEKSI had gone with this speaker because of the review's. I was really happy when I got it, but that lasted for a very short time. Nothing changed in the way I used it or anything, it just stopped working. The Bluetooth light stays on, and no matter how long or which buttons are pressed, it does nothing. I looked for a way to have them send me another one, but the only option was to send this one back. I am going to order the Magic Box II and pray that I get one that works.

title: Another tiny speaker with the same Bluetooth connection hassles.
body: Another not very loud tiny bluetooth speaker that 1. (specific to this speaker) On/Off is vague at best-push power for on, push and hold for off, it's usually silently draining the charge because 2. It is a pain to connect, I watch a lot of short YouTube videos for information, so I want sound so I can walk over to the kitchen and still hear. Instead I spend 5 minutes every time fussing to get this speaker to connect. Often I'm done before this speaker has done anything. (Not too dissimilar from similar speakers, but more trouble than the older one I have) 3. Sound is always at max. I bought this because the reviews talked about how loud and good the sound is. My MacBook Pro has pathetically tiny speakers, this isn't much better. I will say that my iPhone 6s connects better to these devices than does my computer.The new millennium seems to have brought back the silent movie. Why is sound connecting and levels such a complete pain? I purchased this speaker and use Bluetooth, because my MacBook Pro has terrible speakers, and the headphone plug doesn't work--has never worked--most of the 5 years I've had it. (For five minutes prior to writing this it didn't connect, then it says its 'connected' but no sound. I've finished writing this and it's still not connected. How much time do you spend every week troubleshooting your amazing electronic devices?)

title: Exceeded expectations--Incredible sound!--But died in 2 months!
body: ----BEGIN UPDATE 3/14/2016----:The device no longer charges. Thus, rendering it useless. As luck has it, the last date that I could return this item was 2/24/2016...The performance was great, as I had initially described; however, I didn't expect the item to last me only 2 months! I am removing 4 stars for this as aforementioned; the device is now useless.----END UPDATE----I have a Bose SoundDock Series II station. I purchased it before I knew better, meaning the "no highs no lows, must be Bose". This OontZ Bluetooth speaker is incredibly loud and crisp in sound. I docked my iPod to the Bose immediately after streaming through the OontZ for comparison, and it was night and day. The Bose really does not have any variance in the sound and is just plain muffled. I purchased the Bose when it first came out (many many years ago).I am so impressed with this little device, that I carry it everywhere. The battery also lasts for a very long time--I had it cranked for at least 4 hours while finishing my basement. It also packs a powerful "punch". So much so, that it has fallen off of shelves from the "boomy" under-speaker.I initially just wanted a tiny portable speaker to use while in the shower, but I had no idea of the capability of this thing.

title: You get what you pay for
body: I was extensively searching for a best option for a bluetooth speaker, investigating which brand are the best, which models, viewing reviews at youtube, etc until I got to the point that according my judgement the best choice -economically and by performance- would be either the JBL Charge 2+ or the JBL Flip 3. I was just about to shop one of those until someone recommended me this speaker. That I won't regret it.. I was surprised by the accesible price and amazed by the good comments of the people in here so I gave it a try. I am really dissapointed mostly by the performance of this item..it has a lot of "rain noise" intermittently. At first I thought it was because of the volume because I started to tried it very loud then I noticed that the "interference" appeared at modest volume as well. So I started to do some test discarding music with too much bass, or high sounds...but so far I did not found another explanation but that my device just won't work as expected. Do not get me wrong, it works when you put it in a office at decent volume and just once in a while you hear the rain noise (deal with it)....but when I started to move it from here to there, or tried to use it at a meeting with my friends I have to be prepare to get dissapointed.It works, but not as expected. With the quality I expect.

title: Beware, AWFUL sound quality, brutally harsh treble, distorted bass.
body: I've been using 10+ year old, non-bluetooth, speakers with bluetooth adapters for years. After listening to a few small bluetooth speakers in person at a retail store (JBL, Beats, Bose, UltimateEars, etc,) I was impressed with how much better the sound was, especially from speakers that were 1/4 the size of what I have at home. I purchased the OontZ because of the incredible number of positive reviews. I wasn't expecting it to seriously compete with the $150-$200 offerings from big names like JBL or Bose. However, I did expect the Oontz to be a significant improvement over the decade old speakers I've been using.I'm at a loss for words to describe how disappointed I am.The sound quality of the OontZ Angle 3 is horrendous. The treble is so incredibly high that it was painful. Cutting all frequencies from 1kHz and above allowed the volume to be turned up to a normal level, at which point the low end starts distorting, which is only "fixed" by cutting everything below 100Hz. The only tolerable range, with 0dB adjustment, was ~150Hz to ~400Hz. I'm truly shocked at how bad this is.I hope this helps others avoid the same disappointment. I cannot understand how 73% of the reviewers (at the time of writing this review) give this 5 stars.

title: After 15 uses it won't easily bluetooth connect - after 30 uses it just won't work, sounds like it's connecting but won't play.
body: Cambridge Sound Works OontZ Angle 3 - I really liked this the first couple of months I had it. The sound wasn't outstanding but it worked perfect for watching Netflix movies from my Samsung Galaxy tablet. I just wanted a louder sound than my tablet provided. I've probably physically used it 30 times and the last 15 times I've had to reset my bluetooth several times on my tablet before my speaker would connect. I'd hear the noise it's connected -push the speaker volume up, down, up -nothing... reset the bluetooth on my tablet again, hear the disconnect then reconnect and then FINALLY it'd connect. Now all I hear is the connect and disconnect noise.. no amount of resetting will get it to connect.One thing I didn't like about this speaker is that you have no way of telling how much charge it has -ever. So usually when I went to use it I had to have it plugged into the wall charging, which lets face it, doesn't make it very portable. As many other reviewers write the buttons are cheap and you're never really sure they are working or if it's just that the unit is broke.Truly a waste of money. If you can spare a few bucks more a get a better quality speaker it's worth it cause in the end that's what I'm going to need to do.

title: Don't believe the positive reviews. Terrible!
body: As with many items I purchase, I look carefully at the customer reviews on Amazon. In this case, I was snookered! After using this speaker for about a month, I have come to the conclusion that there is no way almost 36,000 people would give this 4.5 stars; something is amiss. I should have followed the adage of "if it's too good to be true, it probably isn't". In this case, the reviews are not close to reality.I purchased this speaker hoping to save a few bucks over the cost of another speaker like the Wonderboom speaker another member of our family has. I was immediately disappointed in this OontZ Angle 3 for two reasons. Most importantly, the sound produced by the speaker is no better than the sound produced by my I phone 8. There is very little base sound and "tinny" overall sound is VERY disappointing. I don't consider myself a sound aficionado, nor do I consider myself a speaker snob; I just want a good full sound from a speaker. Also, this speaker's volume is only able to be controlled by the phone so if the phone is not immediately available, there is no way to lower, or increase, the volume.Save yourself don't waste your hard earned money on this lame excuse for a bluetooth speaker.

title: Extremely Dissapointing
body: Well, sadly -- this thing is terrible:((. I had high hopes as I've had an older model Oontz with great success, it only quit working after 3 years. So I instantly went to the same brand to get another and thought for sure the newest version would be even better as they improve and refine the product. It is not better than the three-year-old version. At all. This one will not charge to run wirelessly, Bluetooth only? Will not connect to any blue tooth device, if it does finally? It goes mute after 3 seconds? I have to keep it plugged into my computer to even play it, with earphone adapter as well to my tablet. Additionally the sound, you can barely hear it? My old one you could hear from any room in the house. This one? You have to be right beside it. Volume up is non-existent. For all the off the charts claims the manufacturer of this product makes in the product description (take a moment to re-read it, none of it is true from my experience), and the marked out 'original price' of $99.00 on 'sale' for $27.99 I am so disappointed. And I cannot return, as I was so certain this product would instantly be as great as my old Oontz I threw the packaging away. Save yourself the money, I DO NOT recommend this product.

title: Great for younger people. Terrible for older people.
body: I ordered this for my parents (older) so they can play music/YouTube videos from their iPad. I showed them how to use it (very simple to operate) and it will work perfectly for them in that sense. The size and the sound quality is good to.My biggest problem with this Bluetooth speaker is that it’s pure solid black (including all the buttons). The black buttons are very difficult to see because they blend in with the color of the device. You can’t see them unless you hold this device at a particular angle and under a bright light. What genius at the manufacture thought of this design? Stupid design. A younger person must have thought of it.The manufacturer could have easily made the buttons a different color (other than black) so that they can be seen easily. I had to put drops of white paint on top of the buttons so they can easily be seen now. I shouldn’t need to have done that. The manufacture should have designed it that way! How hard would that have been. Pathetic.If you are older, do not buy this. Choose another brand and just know that most of these tech companies these days design things for younger people and don’t give a crap about older people’s needs. Good luck.

title: "Potentially" Excellent product that FAILS within 1-2 months use.
body: I do not know how Amazon's math will handle my 'stars'. I gave the product 1 overall star. My intent was to indicate the product is unacceptable despite my having given the product 5 stars for each of Amazon's 3 pre-determined performance ratings.I initially purchased a device 12/2018. It failed a month or so later. The company immediately sent out a replacement. Now, after a similar time frame, the device has failed again. It has failed in the precise way as the first. Despite having a great sound, good battery time, good design...the company managed to spec crap when it came to the speaker's USB port. The connection between the male charging cord and female charging port is what has failed each time and in the precise same manner.I did not bother filing a warranty claim with the company since I'd just get another speaker that would fail in a month or two. I cannot emphasize how unfortunate the company's manufacturing decisions are regarding the USB connection. The speaker is excellent. If the company spent maybe two and a half cents more per speaker for a better USB mechanical connection, the product would indeed be as good as they stipulate vs. (currently) a waste of one's money.

title: Just a bad product, really.
body: My first speaker was DOA. Died right after I tried to charge it.The second one works fine. Pairs easily, good connectivity. It's a functional speaker, but...Holy gawd, the audio is AWFUL.Podcasts are fine. A single voice talking? Very understandable. I listen to Joe Rogan, it's fine, it works. But if you're listening to ANY music with ANY level of complexity beyond a single instrument or voice, it just mangles the whole thing into a scratchy mash.I'm telling you. If you're any level of audiophile, you will HAAAATE this speaker. If you don't care, you just want some noise while you work, or if you really only want to listen to podcasts... yah, this speaker will work just fine. But if you actually LIKE music and you want to hear the instruments, this speaker will totally offend your ears and rip the soul out of everything you try to listen to.I wanted something I could listen to music with. I didn't get that. I did not receive a listenable product, not at all -- I got a scratchy, mashy thing that I can maybe listen to podcasts with, and that's just about it.How did this trash product become an Amazon-recommended item?

title: If you really enjoy music, stay out of this
body: Bought this speaker as a replacement for a first gen Bose SoundLink Color. I know, Bose is Bose, and it has a unique sound and quality, so I should never compare that to this speaker. When I purchased this speaker I had in mind that I would never get the same sound, or even similar, from this speaker, but I decided to give it a try due to the high ratings. I really bought it knowing that my standards would not be met... But I simply do not understand how this speaker is so highly rated. When I was researching about this speaker I found that everyone spoke about the bass and clarity of sound... They were definitely talking about another product, not this one. Bass is horrible, and I miss instruments and even voices from the tracks that I regularly listen to. I would not even give this speaker out as a gift to a kid. Terrible!!! I have purchased non-expensive portable speakers on the past, like several from Anker, and I really enjoy listening to music with them. Big mistake to try another brand. I will get another Anker SoundCore for sure.For me, there is a big difference between listening to music and enjoying music. With this speaker, I can only listen to it.

title: Best speaker purchase on Amazon... or not...
body: [UPDATE 10/01/2020]The speaker I ordered April 2020 won’t turn on anymore. It started having connectivity issues (unpairing randomly, glitching and skipping in the middle of a song) about a week ago, then it started turning off with no warning like the battery was dead. I charge it for an hour at a time once it dies, but it started turning off as soon as I take it off the charger. So it’s effectively useless. I’m going to search elsewhere, which is a bummer considering my first experience was stellar.[ORIGINAL POST]I bought this speaker in August of 2016. The sound is great, carries well when it’s turned all the way up, and also great on the lower volume settings for ambient sound. You can change the sound by directing the speakers toward or away from yourself or a wall to project or mute the sound. It charges in an hour, and stays charged for a week of constant use (about 3-4 hours per day). It always connects with no issue to my iPhone or my friends’ androids. It is currently April of 2020, and my speaker has stopped maintaining a charge, so I’m buying a new one! This is a great speaker for the price, I’m very pleased.

title: No Bluetooth or Cabled Audio from any iOS Device - and No Tech Support
body: No audio via Bluetooth with iPhone (iOS 11.x). No audio via Bluetooth with iPad (iOS 10.x). No audio via physical audio cable from iPhone or iPad. Yes - speaker was powered on and volume was up. Yes - iPhone/iPad volumes were up. Yes - both iOS devices were actually playing music. Yes - both iOS devices connect and play fine through other Bluetooth devices. This OontZ was just dead on arrival. Tried resetting the speaker via micro-switch as per the manual. No audio. Went to OontZ web site to call for tech support. No phone number listed. Only a contact form on their web site. No phone support. No chat support. Just "send us a message from our contact form and wait until we maybe get back to you." I want tech support now. Immediate assistance. Returned product to Amazon and purchased a different brand. Optimistic that one will work just fine.UPDATE: Purchased the Anker speaker ([[ASIN:B016XTADG2 Anker SoundCore Bluetooth Speaker with 24-Hour Playtime, 66-Foot Bluetooth Range & Built-in Mic, Dual-Driver Portable Wireless Speaker with Low Harmonic Distortion and Superior Sound - Black]]) and it worked perfectly out of the box.

title: "SoundWorks...No it doesn't"
body: So looking forward to getting this and pairing with my Echo Dot. First, when I opened the box, NO cables, just the speaker and quick start guide. According to the guide, there was enough charge to play right out of the box and there was. So I set it up on my desktop (bluetooth), paired the device and sure enough it came on. Horrible sound...skips, sound cracks, static. Moved speaker around, same issue. Found a charging cable that worked...same issue. Then I decided to go to the sellers website for help but could find no phone number, email only. They requested my name, email address and order number...typical request, no problem. However, they also ask what device I was emailing from (i.e. phone, tablet, desktop, etc), then ask name and model of device I was emailing from (all required fields in order to submit my inquiry to customer service). WHY would they need that information when I was contacting them about a problem with THEIR product. Would not provide that information so unable to contact seller. Now I need to contact Amazon who, by the way, is AWESOME, and ask to return this item. Very disappointed.

title: UPDATED 7/12/16 Great little bluetooth speaker for the price
body: UPDATE 07/12/16: This worked great...for a while. Went to use it today and it kept turning off. We thought we didn't charge it all the way and left it charging overnight - still won't stay on. I guess you get what you pay for - we only used this about 5 or 6 times and now it's trash. Would no longer recommend due to how quickly this broke.For the price, this is a great little bluetooth speaker. It's portable, waterproof, and was louder than we expected for such a small speaker. We used it for an outdoor party an weren't disappointed. We haven't really put the waterproof quality to test, but plan to use it in the shower, so I'll update if this feature isn't all it's cracked up to be. One of the big selling points for me was that this speaker has a rechargeable battery that is charged with a microUSB cord - no need to replace batteries like many models at a similar price point. My only qualm is that depending on the device linked to the speaker, sometimes the volume buttons on the speaker don't work (had to increase the volume on the original device). Other than that it worked perfectly!

title: Don't buy! Waste of money
body: I bought two of these speakers back in May. About 3 months ago. I've only used them 3x since. Once for a party & twice to workout. I loved them. I thought the sound quality was pretty decent & it was easy to pair. Until today. Only one of them has sound. I've done everything. I've reset them. Unpaired them. Tried it one at a time. I've watched YouTube videos and googled it & went to different websites following the step by step instructions on how to reboot or get the sound back. Nothing has worked. The speaker is able to pair & if I press the pause/play button it will stop or start the music on the other speaker it is paired to, but it won't play music on itself. The sound no longer works. For an almost $30 speaker I expect more than 3 uses. It's probably had a total of 4 hour play time & no longer has sound. It is also completely charged so that's not the issue either. & it is past the return date. On the speaker website it also says it is not eligible for return past 30 days & it requires the original packaging. This honestly really sucks. Save your money, find another speaker. Don't buy from this brand.

title: Awesome Customer Service!  Updated review! Updated again....
body: I thought I got an awesome product for an awesome deal. It was for about 3 months. This thing only stays connected for about 4 seconds now. I use a Bluetooth speaker as my alarm in the morning. I have to listen to my phone speakers when I cook. Ugh.*UPDATE* Cambridge Soundworks contacted me directly and are sending a replacement! They said it may have been due to an issue during shipping and that what I experienced was an anomaly because they manufacture a superior product and had I written my review in the first 30 days initially, I would have agreed. The sound output was awesome! I work for a very large mens retailer and we pride ourselves on our customer service and too often don't experience the same level we give, given to us so hats off to Cambridge for being amazing! This truly is an awesome speaker and the output is amazing so I'm hyped to get it again . Update.Little late but I haven't used this thing in a long time, never took it off my bed stand and it worked for about 2 months, the only reason I updated this review was because I'm on the hunt for a new bluetooth speaker.

title: REAL Review
body: I’m giving this one star mostly to counter all of the obviously fake reviews. If I wasn’t trying to help it show a more accurate star rating, I would probably give it 2-3 stars.It connects easily to Bluetooth, and it is water resistant.I bought it to use in the shower, and it’s okay for that purpose. It holds a charge an average/acceptable length of time. It hasn’t crapped out listening to it in the shower on average once a week for two months.The sound quality is terrible, though. I mostly listen to Podcasts, which it’s fine for, but music sounds better if I just turn the volume up all the way on my iPhone 7 Plus and leave it on the other side of the shower door.Also, anywhere smaller than my tiny bathroom and it is barely audible, even for a podcast. There is no projection with this speaker. It wouldn’t work as my kitchen speaker, and it certainly wouldn’t work for a small event where people would be talking.Be discerning in reading the reviews for this brand - they obviously give away a whole lot of products in exchange for high star ratings.

title: Cambridge Soundworks lack of direct customer service contacts.
body: I piurchased a angle 3 it worked 1 rime and i have had no satisfaction with this product. i have tried to reset product,charge it, follow instructions given by third party given for Cambridge Soundworks, All phone number listed on Cambridge soundworks has no contact phone numbers. A phone contact on website is no good . My disappointment comes from lack of support from company, Making a product that sounds good but all this goes out of the window. Pairing worked with no steps needed at first but every minute ,hours and days have been unfair for any paying customer to pay 5 or twenty dollars to possibly get positive results. i gave one to a friend who may have issues when he opens gift angle 360. I feel bad that I gave gift that may not work please forward it to the company who hides in the bushes and leaves others companies to field the weight of a bad product. I thank you for your time and sorry about venting to company that sells product that leaves other people to cleaning up a mess. The pairing problem will effect all other companies who sell products made by Cambridge soundworks.

title: Poor workmanship - heed the complaints before deciding if you really want to buy this - I DO NOT recommend this product
body: The speaker had issues from the start. The mini plug on the cord fell off when I unplugged it soon after I purchased the speaker. I wanted a longer plug anyway so I replaced it instead of returning the product - big mistake on my part. The speaker constantly kept disconnecting and blinking that it was running out of power even though I kept it attached to my computer. It would work, though, if I wriggled the cord at the end connected to the speaker. But now it won't charge at all. So I opened the unit and, sure enough, the soldering is substandard and the receptacle for the mini plug wasn't properly connected. I'm not going to pay to have it corrected. This is the worst thing I've purchased in a long time. The pretend $99.99 must be shown to get people to throw away $27.99 on an item that should not even be offered for free. I suggest that Cambridge SoundWorks take a look at their quality control and raise the bar. I DO NOT recommend buying this product as I am not the only one with these problems, by far. They don't seem to be responsive to complaints and that says a lot about the compnay.

title: UPDATE: stopped charging after 3.5 months :(
body: Well my below review is still completely true, however after 3 and a half months the speaker can no longer hold a charge, or the charger cable has stopped working. It will only play for about 5 seconds before shutting off. This is a huge bummer since I like this speaker so much. I mostly leave the speaker in one place too, it hasn't been jostled about at all. Changing rating from 5 stars to 1 as this is the auxiliary speaker with the shortest life span I have experienced yet. Fail :(. Original review: WOW!!!!! So impressed. The internal speakers in my Mac laptop are blown & I've been using various external speakers for the last couple years, most of them lasting roughly 6 months and exhibiting many performance issues along the way. Not this little speaker!! So far anyway. I am seriously so impressed with the sound quality, volume level, ease of use/pairing/charging. Plus it's kinda cute, the shape is structurally smart (resistant to tipping over) and it has great little non-skid feet that really keep it in place. Only it's longevity remains to be seen...

title: Not a bad speaker but don't use it in your car on a hot day.
body: The sound quality and overall functionality of the device isn't bad. However, the build quality leaves a lot to be desired. I say this because the case of the speaker melted and warped. Yes, this really happened. The speaker, which I was using in place of my broken aux port, literally cannot withstand the direct sunlight on a hot day. The case of the speaker began melting and is now warped. There is now a noticeable difference in the quality of audio. Aditionally, the battery is maintaining far fewer hours of charge. That is of course, when it charges. Which, I'm sure, is do to the fact that the case melted and is now warped. Did I mention that the case melted and warped? That all being said, if you intend to use this in your car or next to a fire while camping (or a heat source and etc.), do not. In fact, if you aren't in the frigid indoors just save yourself the trouble and don't use it. I say this because your speaker will likely warp due to it melting.I will be adding a few photos to this review in a couple hours when the chance arises.

title: Fail to stay connected to a laptop for extended periods of time
body: The sound quality is great. Syncing one speaker to the other is cool and works (sort of). But these Bluetooth speakers do not stay connected to a laptop for an extended period of time. I bought them to use for workshops I lead that involve video and to play music for participants during their work time. I can get one speaker to connect consistently and found syncing the second speaker to the first worked well at first, but then became problematic the more I used both speakers. If connected and not used for more than 10-15 minutes, they disconnect completely or play audio in bursts interspaced with silence. I can't have tech that fails to perform in trainings I lead. It looks and sounds unprofessional. I wanted two speakers I could spread out in a room that would sync. Instead, I got tech I couldn't trust.I've got an older Braven speaker that costs more than these two speakers combined, but it also works flawlessly. I had to return the speakers and will most likely fork out the money to buy a better quality speaker that puts out more sound.

title: Update/Do NoT purchase/update
body: [[VIDEOID:de361a31581bd808a8525d6958e527c8]] 10/24/22 update:Received June 13th. July 13th no Amazon return allowed.On August 27th emailed Cambridge SoundWorks. Speaker is cutting out.They tell me do hard reset, done worked twice then same issue. They tell me to update the firmware, try, no success oontz app doesn't work. To this date still emailing back and forth. They say I can't get refund because it is outside the return window! Amazon says the seller offers 1 year warranty.....I am sooo disappointed I loved that little speaker and Cambridge SoundWorks sucks at customer service!This little speaker is so full of amazing sound! I bought it because I teach a water exercise class. They love it! It is advertised as a shower speaker so I did not need to worry about it getting wet. The fully charged battery lasts about 7 and a half hours, I wanted to be sure to time it so I know when to charge for the next class. I would definitely buy it again. It took literally seconds to connect to my phone, I have a Google pixel 4a.

title: Poor customer service, speaker has issues.
body: Got this back in October. For whatever reason Cambridge now has this listed 3 different times on Amazon. My original review is in one of the other listings.This seems to be the most populated one so I'm going to post my review here too.My original oontz angle 3 had charging issues. When I tried contacting Cambridge I heard nothing from them until I posted the bad review. I got a replacement sent and this one didn't have the same issues. But, after about 2 weeks it started to develop a distortion at any volume. I tried contacting them again and this was back in November and I never heard back from them. As of now the speaker is pretty much unusable because the distortion is so bad(I'm assuming a blown driver).I have the oontz angle plus and have had it for over a year and still running strong. This one just isn't as good. So sad. As good as the angle plus was, with this experience I think I'm done with Cambridge. I had such high hopes from them and expected the new speaker to be better than the last but it's not.

title: Dead battery made it useless
body: This is the second Oontz I bought (the first one being the Oontz plus, the older and bigger version). This speaker worked well initially, although I found the sound is not as crisp as the bigger version. About one month later, the plastic strip that covers the power button and USB port fell off. It made it looks kind of ugly. Aside from that, it made powering on/off the speaker very annoying because it relies you to press the plastic strip to power on/off it. Without the plastic strip, you have to use a small pin to push the little button inside a hole to turn on/off it.Worse thing, now the speaker's battery is completely dead. No matter how long you charge it through USB port, it does not have any power. Essentially, it is a dead speaker. I kind of noticed that the speaker's play time lasted shorter and shorter. But I did not really pay attention as I though I did not charge long enough. Now it is completely dead and useless.It is a very bad experience with this speaker and I would not recommend buying it.

title: DONT BUY.  CUTS OFF. POORLY DESIGNED.TINNY SOUND. NO ONE KNOWS ANYTHING,
body: This product is terrible. It cut off while watching videos. It deserves NO STARS. And speaker connector didn’t fit well so there was static. I complained and they sent a replacement. Still didn’t work...AND NOW NO SPEAKER CONNECTOR. The only way I had transmission was if I plugged it into my iPad and it acted as a dumb speaker. I thought the sound was tinny. Amazon reps were clueless and poor communicators. They didn’t give me proper credit. So frustrating .....DONT BUY. i got info from Cambridge and still didnt work. THEY BLAMED AN APPLE UPDATE OR SOMETHING. Also they say the battery has to be fully charged. But no way to measure charge. Five hours should be enough. I am now connecting videos ON MY iPad to a lovely portable Bose product I bought to use with my old iPod, which I’ve lost. That’s good sound.!But not wireless. I’m sadly disappointed in the product AND the Amazon reps. Yuk . The last rep was smart and knowledgeable but then we were cut off. I’ve spent hours trying to unravel this problem and on the phone.

title: Incredibly WEAK speakers..weaker than stock desktop PC or even laptop speakers. VERY disappointing.
body: Have used this for about 7 months now and just can't make peace with it...as inexpensive as it was it's just NOT WORTH IT. I'm sorry...but gotta wonder if the mfg changed the device in time since the amazon best seller status (which got me to buy it) but bluntly stated, its the weakest speaker I've ever owned and the irony is that Ive never bought good speakers. LOL. WEAK LOW max volume...no kidding, this is weaker than the economy speakers that came with my last three economy desktop computers (e-machines...used) and the volume is weaker than my laptop computer (9 year old Dell). Please see my other reviews to gauge my specificity and readiness to compliment a good product. I'm sorry to say this could only have been more disappointing unless it didn't turn on and even then it wouldn't be much quieter. LOL. I'm seriously thinking of hooking this up to a meter to quantify the low decibel output and video it as a warning to other users. If the mfg wants to contact me to send this unit back for examination I'd happily send it.

title: Unfortunately not the match for my Amazon Fire Stick TV
body: I am amending my feedback as, unfortunately this portable speaker stopped working after just 5 months - and with minimal amount of use. It stopped pairing with my Fire TV and my iPhone. Amazon referred me back to Cambridge however I have not received a response from them.My initial review:After purchasing three other bluetooth speakers to use with my Amazon Fire Stick TV I decided to try this Cambridge product. I tried two iHome Bluetooth speakers (and a third I can't remember the brand name) which kept shutting off while I was watching a show - with Battery fully charged. Each time I wanted to use the portable speakers instead of the television speaker's I would have to go into settings for the Fire TV Bluetooth to connect the speaker. However, this speaker stays connected and the Fire Stick recognizes it each time and sends the sound right to the speaker. The volume controls on the speaker work well and the pause button actually pauses the programming. I do not have to use the remote. Good quality speaker!

title: Stopped working after 18 months- doesnt pair with any bluetooth devices
body: Update after 18 months of minimal use:The speaker doesnt pair with any phones anymore. Tried Android and Apple phones. Even doesnt connect with PC.Hence derived that the bluetooth is broken in the speaker. Contacted support and was asked to send the speaker (shipping cost on me). It will be analyzed and then determined if replacement is needed. While I understand that the speaker is out of warranty hence the process of sending the speaker to the company at my expense however my concern is that the speaker broke down in 18 months of minimal use. I have had other bluetooth headphones much cheaper than this one and they are still working fine being more than 2 years old.Initial Review:Initially didnt like it as i felt the sound was too low and the bass wasnt good.But in a few days, realized its good enough. Its not very loud but clarity of the sound is very good for the price.Even the bass is pretty strong when the system is laid down with the speakers towards the bottom.

title: Poor Sound Quality
body: I purchased one of the first Oontz Angle speakers years ago. It was fairly underwhelming at the time, but decent for an entry level speaker of its size. It had some heft and good build quality. The Oontz Angle 3, while still of decent build quality, seems light and cheap. Even so, I was still curious about what the sound quality would be. It paired quickly with my phone with no issues. But the sound - wow, so disappointing! I wasn't expecting the sound of my JBL or Bose portables, but this speaker is just plain lousy. Absolutely no low end, tinny highs, and really no joy at all. I would sooner listen to music from my phone speakers - at least they are nicely tuned with stereo sound. I was really shocked that the latest incarnation of a wildly popular speaker would be such a step back. I bought it for less than a ten spot on Cyber Monday, so I'll just give it to one of the neighbor kids, but at the MSRP of 30 dollars, it would have gone right back. Unfortunately, I wouldn't recommend this speaker at any price.

title: Cambridge SoundWorks -- another formerly great company craps out
body: I hate to leave this kind of feedback for a Cambridge SoundWorks product, given that I have been a fan of the company and its products -- I've owned about a dozen of them over the years -- since Henry Kloss co-founded it back in the '80s. Back then, Kloss delighted in creating products that would outperform overhyped competitors such as Bose at a far lower price. Sadly, the Oontz shows how badly the company has lost its way since Kloss died. Not only would the speaker's anemic bass performance be anathema to Kloss, who voiced all of his speakers to produce robust bass, but the Oontz also falls far short of the performance of competitors such as the DKnight Magicbox, which blows the Oontz away to a degree all out of proportion to its $5 price differential. In the old days, Kloss would have designed a bluetooth speaker that held its own against the as-usual-outrageously priced $199 Bose Soundlink, and sold it for a quarter of that price. No more. Sad to see the formerly great Cambridge Soundworks lose its mojo.

title: Extremely Disappointed
body: This item doesn't work through a single wall or round a corner. Basically, you need to have your device on you (if it's a phone) and this speaker right in front of you, or set your computer on a table and have this speaker just across the (small to mid-size) room for it to work. If you're trying to use it through a wall in an adjacent room, but only 12ft away from your device, the sound skips around and is super choppy like a bad radio station. Even if there's an open doorway, this speaker cannot seem to play a clear sound when any walls exist, period. A person standing in front of or walking by a computer connected to this speaker will also cause the sound to break up. And forget using it upstairs while your device is directly below it downstairs (within 30ft); then, it doesn't work at all. This speaker is essentially worthless. It should be advertised as a sound amplifier only, to be used only when you are able to have both your device and this speaker right next to each other. Don't waste your money.

title: You get what you pay for
body: For anyone looking for good quality sound at this price point…you won’t find it and this isn’t the product for you. Coming from a background in building and competing in Car Audio installation and tuning…I was impressed by the sleek design, simplicity, and ease of use; however that is where it ended. Less than 5 seconds of listening and I packaged it back up and issued a ticket for return. The reviews on this unit can only be born of an opinion derived from no experience in high fidelity sound as most reviews say the unit has good sound quality…I would equate it to no better than using your phone’s internal speaker with added volume nothing more. If put on an oscilloscope I would be surprised to anything register under 9000hz. If you just want louder sound than your phone in an easy to use compact design…this is your product. If you’re looking for EXCELLENT sound quality and a lot more volume for a little more cash…go with the JLB Flip5…that’s what I went with and am thoroughly impressed.

title: No luck here very disappointed...
body: First I owned 3 Bluetooth speakers so far the last one was my favorite. I had it for about a year and a half the last few months the battery barely hold any charge but worked fine while it had external power supply.It was loud and sound was very decent but one day drop it in a pail full of water and that's the end of the story.To bad it has been discontinued so I decided to give the Angle 3 a try after all the great reviews and because it water resistant.So I tried first with my cellphone paired right away however sound was really distorted I had to restart Pandora or whatever the application is I was using, very annoying. So I tried with my laptop and it doesn't pair tried another computer and still don't want to pair none of them are a year old since I was going to format the laptop I tried with the new windows 10 and still not pairing.Went to local cell shop tried a Bluetooth dongle and even when the laptop sees the speaker the connection never happens,Maybe I got the Friday night special.

title: App is sketch and doesn’t work
body: I was interested in this speaker so I could use Alexa in the shower to get morning news, weather, etc while I’m getting ready for work. In order to use Alexa though, you have to first connect to their OontZ app. Their app cannot detect their own product though, even if following the included directions step by step (the app has 1.7 stars as of this review). I found a work around by pairing directly to the speaker then launching the app. This enabled the app to finally detect the speaker. To pair with Alexa it wanted me to enter my Amazon login username and password directly into their app. Now most apps I have used in the past that do this will auto forward you to the Amazon app so you know it’s safe and secure. Maybe I’m overly cautious, but entering my personal Amazon info into a 3rd party janky app that is sketchy to begin with (misspelled words etc) was a dealbreaker. Unless this is a stellar piece of hardware, I assume most of the product reviews are fake. Got me this time.

title: Loved this little thing, but now? Nothing but a cute lil' paperweight
body: When I recieved my Angle 3 Enhanced, initially I was as happy as one could be with my purchase. I LOVED this little thing, it sounded WAY better than I expected - not alot of bass thump, obviously, but - clear, musical, & loud - real loud. Suprising volume for it's size. This was perfect for my work - I am a route delivery driver, and with the speakerphone feature as well as a bluetooth in my ear, I was covered in & out of my vehicle. It would go to my earpiece out of my truck - and automatically switch back to the Angle III speakerphone when I was driving. And sounded great. Perfect.But out of the box the first day, I noticed right off the bat that was a real drag, and a problem I've noticed with many similar products - the lack of attention spent to the USB Charging input. It's one of those, "some units the cable fits properly - some units it doesn't" type deal. No matter how you inserted the cable, it's fit with the unit far below sub-par. It just didn't go in and fit securely, in fact, not at all. You kinda had to prop it up gently when it was in, not bump it, and watch it closely to make sure it stayed in there - because half the time it wouldnt. A couple of times the connection was so that instead of charging it - it drained it instead. Not to mention that the design of the rubber casing in relation to the width of the connector that was needed for that connector the go in and fit snuggly was not considered when designed. That, to me, is a mental error. It clearly didn't fit, nor did the connector on the unit hold up. Beyond dissapointing, to say the least. But, I loved the sound. It was perfect. I would just make do, and be very careful with that part of it. And just make sure it was fully charged when I left the house each day.Well, of course, I get just outside the warranty period - and the connector on the unit just desinigrated. The pins on the inside male part of the connector just collapsed or broke off, and the connector itself just pushed in & fell back into the unit. So now, the last full charge I had, is the last full charge I'll ever have. Once that charge cycle is used up, my bitchin' bluetooth speaker will be a paperweight. Or a doorstop. Gee, great. That's not what had in mind when I purchased it. The end of a great little speaker. Which could have been avoided.So - this is my stupid question to anyone in the know - can an Oontz III possibly in any way be charged by any type of wireless charging unit or plate? I know, it's a dumb question, but? I'd like to see it live on, but the charge is getting low, and the unit will be 100% useless to me. What a shame. Considering it's basically new.Any help from someone out there would be greatly appreciated....

title: 5/3/16 Not so great anymore
body: UPDATE(11/10/15)The speaker has developed a distortion/rattle on the right speaker. Not very noticable when on the loudest but once the songs quiet down its very noticable and its hard to ignore. Sounds like a blown speaker but not at the same time. Contacting cambridge about the issue. I will update shortly.UPDATE(10/27/15)I had Cambridge contact me soon after initially posting up the review. They have since replaced my speaker with another and the new one works flawlessly. It is LOUD and clear. No, volume issues. Down to the nitty gritty, this speaker is amazing for the price and size. Its bass is really, really strong for something this size. Most people are amazed by it. Yet i have seen many reviews saying its not enough. I have a trick for that. Most of the time i have the speaker in or on something that reverberates a lot like a cabinet or something hollow and this really amplifies the bass. Giving it a much richer and powerful feel (it really doesnt need it though). The battery at full blast seems to last about 3-5 Hours. Considering it is supposed to last 7 hours at 50% i would say thats not bad. Big thumbs up for cambridge for following up and apologizing for the lack of communication. It seems my email got lost in their systems somehow and they called me immediatly as soon as they saw my review. They called like at 5pm on a Sunday. Overall, best speaker for price, PERIOD.The speaker itself is amazing. Much louder than the Angle plus and just as clear. But i think i got a defective product. The speaker cant keep itself at full volume for long as it seems to overheat and then the volume gets turned down(on its own) im assuming to protect the internals. I dont have an option for a replacement so im returning for a refund. I tried contacting Cambridge through their website like 2 weeks ago but have yet to receive a reply on a warranty replacement. Also, they sent me 2 AUX cables instead of one AUX and one Micro USB. I want to give it 5 stars but the lack of communication and the faulty product has me thinking i might not buy it again or buy from Cambridge at all. Its a shame really. Their Oontz angle plus and Oontz XL are great speakers.Update 5/3/16The speaker has pretty much had it. The bad driver is so distorted it's unpleasant to listen to anymore and I never heard back from them after numerous emails. The original I ordered was a dud and took some time to get a hold of them and the replacement blew out a driver within a few weeks of use. Tried contacting Cambridge again and nothing. This really is a shame,I still have my oontz angle plus and it still runs strong.

title: You Have To Try This Speaker!!
body: **PLEASE SEE TWO UPDATESWell, what's not to like about this little, powerful, tuff son-of a-gun speaker??? I waited a long time to review this little dynamo and I just get more and more impressed with this wee guy!!! I live in the country and carry this speaker everywhere with Alexa pouring out tune after tune. Probably drives my neighbors (multiple acres away) with the extreme quality these little speakers (there are two) pour out!! I have dropped it more times than I can count, my dog has run off with it and shook it (all the while playing George Strait at top volume), then thankfully brought it back. Stuck in the window when it's raining, it never gives up-cat has swatted it around and I even take it into a humid post-shower bathroom. Alright, I never intended to abuse it, but it's my only music speaker, but not sadly, this little thing rocks! The battery stays charged for days if you turn it off at night and even if you don't and it dies-just plug it into the charger and it play immediately-and charges at the same time. I have plugged it into my laptop for MP3 tunes....phenomenal. I keep thinking, I should get another one, but this little thing plays so well, I have to turn it down just to realize someone has come over to visit!***Update: I guess it's Murphy's Law but I just wrote this review last night and this morning it will not work with Alexa (the volume will not increase). Thought it was Alexa, but tried it on the laptop and it worked for a few minutes then turned itself off. It is fully charged but will not stay on and will not play more than a whisper on Alexa....figures, only eight months old. Thought this was the exception. Bringing it back to only four stars from five. Sorry everyone.****Second Update: I ordered another speaker after this update. That speaker failed within a month. The EXACT same issue, would not stay on nor keep a charge. I contacted the customer service department and after explaining that I followed every instruction to the letter (again) they mailed me a replacement in three days. An excellent customer service department, but that does not make the product. So I received it early August-it is September and the new one has failed AGAIN!!!! I have HAD IT with this speaker. Three in ten months-all failed. I will tell the reader that I use(d) this speaker all day, but should that affect it??? No. I will be ordering a different brand this evening. Dropped review to two stars.

title: Okay, not great, not terrible
body: I recently ordered four of the highest-rated, low cost portable Bluetooth speakers for comparison. My intent was to buy the one I liked best for my personal use when travelling and to return the others. Following are my non-professional impressions.The products I purchased from Amazon, and the prices I actually paid, were:Sony SRSX11 $48DKnight MagicBox II $35Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 $30Anker Classic $37I played a variety of music on each of them, including Van Morrison, Miles Davis, U2 and Pink Floyd, constantly rotating between the speakers mid-song as I compared their sound.1. The Anker was by far the least impressive. It sounded tinny and flat with little or no bass. Very disappointing and was an easy reject, the first to go back into its box.2. The OontZ Angle sounded pretty good at first, and showed excellent midrange and treble notes. It also played at a nice loud volume. I liked it a lot but ultimately I noticed the bass response was lacking and it sounded a little thin compared to the remaining others, so it was next to go. Too bad, because I liked the styling of it, and the fact that, unlike the others, it had no annoying blue light to bother me in a dark room.The last two standing were a tough choice, but …3. The MagicBox was really good and almost won out. It had a full sound and great style, but in the end I found it didn’t have the best range from highs to lows that I heard in the Sony. Just not quite enough bass to make me happy. If it had been the only one I’d heard I would have been quite happy with it, and felt sad to send it back.4. The Sony was totally surprising given its very small size. I thought it would be easy to rule out before I heard it, but it kept impressing me with its excellent bass response and midrange tones. I’d say it has a cleaner sound overall. Maybe in this case, a little higher cost is worth the price. I’m keeping this one, knowing it’ll fill up a hotel room with great sound.Hope this helps, and happy listening!

title: Not an amazing value when it quits working after two months!
body: Old review updated: I really hate slamming a product I initially loved, but I have tried everything, and my speaker I loved so much just quit working after two months. I have tried to post an update once before, but I must not have completed it, because no one has contacted me, and I cannot believe I am expected to keep a product I bought on Amazon as a prime member when it did not even last 6 months. The trouble is that, even fully charged, when I turn the speaker on, it comes on for a minute, then dies. I have done all I can do, and so now I am back to using my trusty IHome bluetooth speaker (which I bought at Radio Shack two years ago and still works like a champ. It does not sound as good as the Oontz Speaker, but I do not have to replace it 6 times per year. Amazon or Cambridge SoundWorks should reach out to me now. I will keep you all posted.I have purchased several of these little Blue-tooth speakers to carry with me on picnics, place around the house for music while I am cleaning, and to carry outside for cook outs or poker night. I bought the best one from Radio Shack for $39, and that tennis ball sized I-Home speaker was the best until Oontz. I got the sweet red Oontz speaker a couple of weeks ago, and this evening-bag sized speaker beats all five of its predecessors in sound AND price. My husband and I listened to some of the glorious love songs of Whitney Houston Sat night over Rhapsody connected via Bluetooth to the Oontz. We never got up to full volume, but it sounded like that angel was singing in our den, with a choir surrounding us. Sunday we used it to watch a movie on my tablet. When the bad guys were surrounded I could hear the bullets whizzing past my ears. That is a STEREO. I plan to order a couple more in a few weeks. Once again, Amazon Prime came through with a fine product at an excellent price and two day shipping was free, as always. Thank you, Cambridge SoundWorks for giving us Boze Quality at a price we all can pay!

title: "3" is for the number of months it works before breaking
body: I'll say this: Cambridge Soundworks provides really good customer service. They've earned this 4.5-star rating, not because Oontz Angle 3 is the preeminent low-cost portable speaker option (note: it isn't), but by managing their ratings very carefully via superior customer service.I'm now on my third Oontz device in under a year. I bought my first one in March. When it broke in June, I posted an appropriate review here and the people at Cambridge Soundworks were kind enough to send a free replacement. When the replacement broke in August, they immediately sent an upgraded Oontz Angle 3 Plus -- again, at no charge -- in hopes that I'd have better experiences with the upgraded device.Like clockwork, after three months of usage my Oontz Angle 3 Plus started having all the same Bluetooth connectivity problems that my first two Oontz devices had.
