Each device worked well for three months (wondering, is that what the "3" stands for?), but eventually the audio starts to cut out and sound very choppy like a skipping compact disc. At first I thought it was related to low charge, but it isn't. Power-cycling the speaker and re-pairing the BT devices solves the problem for only for a little while, but the choppy audio eventually returns.Thank you, Oontz, for the great customer service and for all the free devices, but it's now time to move on.The SoundBot SB520 that I bought for my girlfriend two Christmases ago is still working fine and has never had an issue. The SoundBot SB571 that I bought for my mother last Christmas is also still working fine and has never given her an issue. The AmazonBasics Portable speaker that I've had at work for over a year sounds better than the Oontz Angle 3, and again, has never once given me a problem. While all having lower Amazon ratings, my first-hand experiences with each of these devices has been far more positive than my experiences with Oontz.

title: Louder yes, but the original one sounds better.
body: I have the original OontZ Angle which is great, just under powered. This came up on a lightning deal and I have been wanting to get another bluetooth speaker so I gave it a go. It's packaged nicely, easy to use and pair up. It's actually a bit smaller or appears to be a bit smaller than the original OontZ Angle. So I paired both of them up to see how they sound. The original one has a real nice sound, it's just needs a boost in power. The new one has more power and a passive bass radiator so it should sound much better. Well, it doesn't. Side by side, there is an obvious difference in loudness with the Angle 3 being quite loud for something that small, not Bose or UE loud, but loud enough. I'd say 3 times louder than the original, but boy does it lack in full range sound. You would think the bass radiator would help, but it doesn't seem to. Again, side by side, the original one has a nicer overall sound than the new one. I asked 3 kids and my wife tell me which one sounded better. They all said the red one (original one is red) sounded better, but the black one (new one) is much louder. If your close to it, you hear the bass, but we were about 10 ft from them, both on a kitchen counter and about 6 inches from the back-splash. Sorry, the original sounds better. The new one overpowers with higher tones when the volume is up. If you lower it so that it's at about what the original one max volume is, then they sound the same. But you're buying this because the original one isn't loud enough. Going to return it. Maybe I got a defective one, I don't know. I really love my original one and had higher hopes for this one. If the XL[[ASIN:B010OYASRG [New] OontZ Angle 3 Bluetooth Portable Speaker: 10W+ Louder Volume More Bass Water Resistant IPX5 Wireless Shower Speaker (BLACK), Give The Gift They Want to Receive by Cambridge SoundWorks]] one goes on a lighting deal, I'll try it.

title: ... through the list of reviews and you'll see plenty like mine: powered it on
body: Scroll through the list of reviews and you'll see plenty like mine: powered it on, was really excited, connected easily, and it worked. For about 10 minutes. Now, no matter what device we pair it with, it is just unusable. I'm guessing quality control isn't the best, since most people have glowing reviews, but there are quite a few with defective units. When you connect and start playing audio, it sounds good for about a minute. Then, the signal starts breaking up. It drops out intermittently, the audio will cut out and then skip forward quickly, it will "hang" and get that choppy/static sound (like when we used to listen to CDs and it would get a scratch and hang on a part for a moment before skipping ahead). The sound (when it comes through) is sometimes really good, but usually just garbled. We didn't even get to test out its water resistance. I got it as a lightning deal for my wife for Christmas; we had our early Christmas present opening and it only worked for a few minutes. Every device we connect it to does the same thing, leading me to assume it's some internal bluetooth receiver issue (in the past when my bluetooth devices have failed, they do the same thing, dropping out and being patchy, and eventually they stop transmitting/receiving). And even though Amazon has good customer service, they won't allow me to send it back for a replacement. They will only allow me to send it back for a refund to my credit card or an equivalent value amazon credit. And since it was a lightning deal, if I purchase the speaker again as a replacement, I have to pay MORE to take a gamble and see if a new one works better, or take the refund and try to buy a different speaker. If Cambridge SoundWorks audits these reviews, PLEASE take heed of the slew of recent customers with this same exact issue.

title: Too boomy for my purpose and annoying buttons.
body: I have used 2 of the older version of this for 4 or 5 years and they worked pretty well for me but they both finally wore out (I use them in my car and at my work every day listening to audio books from the library so they had a lot of use). I got this new one assuming it would work as well. Unfortunately it does not. The sound is so boomy and lacking in high end, it makes voices unintelligible a lot of the time. Unfortunately the app I have to use offers no EQ to try and make it sound better on the high end and neither does my tablet. If it is a woman doing the reading it's okish but if it's a man doing the reading, it can be very hard to understand what he is saying a lot of the time. I suppose these would work pretty well with music but my older OontZ did both voice and music much better. Plus some bright designer decided to make the symbols on the end for Power, Volume +-, and Bluetooth set up in black on black so it is nearly impossible to see them without having to pick it up and rotate it around at different angles to get the light to hit it just right so you can see what you're trying to push. The older style was much easier to adjust. And my 3rd and final dislike about this model is the Auto shut off. It would be a good thing if you tended to forget to shut it off, but I tend to leave it unattended with no books playing for longish periods of time and it keeps shutting off way too soon in my opinion. And it takes it most of a minute to come back on and reconnect with my tablet. Again, my older OontZ's did not do this. However, this unit does have a lot more power (volume) than the old ones and if it could produce better high's it would be great for listening to books on. Overall, I would say this could be OK for music purposes but not any speech purposes.

title: Work Great until they don't....
body: I've had my Oontz Angles for a while now, 3 years almost. Earlier this year both of them on the SAME EXACT DAY stopped working. They still connect via Bluetooth, they both still charge properly and turn on. Neither of them, however, will produce any sound. I have connected them to six different phones and a laptop. I find it very curious that both stopped working on the same day but hey, I moved on to a higher end product with hopes it will last longer.While the Oontz Angle was working it produced what I would call adequate sound, though one could make it loud a lot was lost in clarity. The speakers, if turned up, would bounce around and fall off of things which could be annoying or amusing depending on ones mood and the presence of children. The only issues other than the untimely death of my products were that when traveling in a vehicle without a stereo we would use the Oontz Angle to provide much needed audio entertainment. Nearly every passing semi emitted some frequency that would disconnect the Bluetooth from the device temporarily. As it turns out there are a LOT of semi's on the highway so I would not recommend using this on the road, however at home it is a perfectly adequate device as long as it is functioning.In conclusion, I would say buyer beware, but at the price this is listed compared to similar quality devices it's value is there, but you will be increasing your electronic waste, including batteries as there is quite clearly some kind of internal clock/timer/defect in the units that ultimately renders them to the trash heap. My two cents would be to save up and save the planet by buying a quality product instead of buying disposable ones, but hey, live and learn.

title: Good Speaker, No AC Adapter
body: Update: after about 2 years, the battery life started to deminish. It went from about 2 weeks between charges with daily use for 30-60 min. Then down to 1 week, then quickly reduced. Now maybe 20 min on a full charge. Given the gentle indoor only use, I hoped for more than 2 years of battery function. Score reduced based on the short product life.Original review: So far I have been happy with the product. The sound is pretty good for a small speaker and the volume has gotten loud enough for my listening preferences. I have been impressed with the battery life. It seemed to have a full charge out of the box, as I immediately paired it up and used it for 3-4 hours without issue. I have not used it unplugged for more than 4 hours, so I cannot say how long it would take to fully deplete the battery. When not plugged in and not playing, it will auto shutoff after a while (I have not timed, but several times I have paused my music and realized it was off when I went to restart, maybe 10-15 minutes). If plugged in it will stay on until you turn it off. I have used it with an iPhone and Amazon Prime Music and the functionality is fine. I have not used the speakerphone functionality, so I cannot speak to the microphone capabilities.The only reason I give this a 4 rather than 5 is for the charger. While not specifically discussed in the description, I expect an item with an internal rechargeable battery to come with a complete charger and this does not. It only comes with a 10" micro USB cable, so you will need a spare USB AC adapter if you plan to plug it in to an outlet (and probably also need a longer cord). Luckily I still had an extra charger from a couple phones ago.

title: These speakers were crappy. From the reviews
body: These speakers were crappy. From the reviews, I actually thought these would be decent. They weren't. And who in the heck thought this had decent, or even acceptable bass. Really? Compared to what? Their grandpa's old hand-held AM radio?Don't waste your money on these things. Even the bluetooth was terrible, if I bent over to pick something up and my phone went lower than the speakers, they cut out (very weird).Lucky for me, my dad just happened to have an extra Bose stereo and it was the kind you can connect up to WiFi, so I totally scored there. He wasn't even using it. How does someone just have an Bose stereo just hanging around that's not even being used?? Still can't believe my luck with that one!Anyway, I do realize that it really wouldn't be fair to compare these $30 things to a $600 Bose. Still though - honestly this wasn't even really worth $30. It was hardly even any better than just listening to the music coming out of my phone itself. I'll give it 2 stars since it was slightly better than just using a phone and also was easy to set up. I guess it could be cool for taking to the beach or camping or whatever. But definitely not a good house speaker, even if two are connected (which is what I did). I live in a tiny house, by the way, I really only needed this for a couple of rooms and immediately outside in my backyard. Didn't even work for that.Very annoying that these got such good reviews and makes me suspicious of the reviews. Or at the very least, wondering if most of the reviewers are tone-deaf...?

title: Whistle While You Work - Updated (battery won't hold charge after 2 months daily use)
body: *Update: speaker turns off, won't hold charge and dies 30 seconds after being unplugged*Might have a bad unit, but that don't excuse Cambridge SoundWorks or Amazon for not backing their product.I am a professional driver, think Purple Promise, and being on the road in any type of vehicle in our fleet the Oontz Angle 3 delivers crisp clear sound all the way to the back of our trucks.Pros:Durable - I've dropped it at least 10x now and aside from a few scratches, it looks and sounds brand newBattery Life - I've pushed it to 7hrs straight playing time without plugging it in. LOUD.Bass - for a little thing it packs a good bass punchConnects easily as soon as I turn my phones BT on.Price - I got mine for $24 through Prime. Can't beat the price.Cons:Bluetooth - range drops in and out depending on the battery charge. Expected, but still a hassle.Weight - it is a pro and a con, due to its heavy bass it has a tendency to "jump" or move across the dashboard. The rubber grips help, but I think a little more weight may help. Easily fixed.Overall, I gave it 5 stars because of the price point and quality of sound. That's all I ask for in a portable speaker and the Oontz 3 delivers. I pull in to our station and everyone thinks I have a huge speaker in my truck or it costs $100+. I have recommended this product to nearly every driver who likes to whistle while they work.Great product. Highly recommended.

title: Doesn't work with audible books or podcast (known problem doesn't have fix for yet).
body: Had the original OontZ Angle that battery stopped staying charged from so much use. I love to listen to audio books and music as I get ready in morning, and take around house doing errands. I decided to buy two of this newest model so I could have one in bathroom and one in kitchen. Plays music just fine and has decent sound for the price, BUT, after a month of using for audio books, it would skip the first part of a word when started a new paragraph. For example, "chapter" would sound like "apter". and so on. Super annoying. But with that book, it only happened on one of the speakers so I went to return it and bought another. But then the 2nd one started doing it with a new book. I then replayed the previous book and it was now also skipping. i didn't understand why. When I contacted Audible, they explained it wasn't the book, it was the bluetooth connection as if I listened on the phone, it was fine. Contacted Oontz support and they are aware of the problem.They offered to send me a new speaker that they flashed with hardware fix so said ok. They sent me 3 as I was willing to have a 3rd one if it worked and was willing to test it out (and I actually hadn't shipped the first one back as of that point). Got 3 of them, but all three had the exact same problem. But even worse results with the whole first word of sentence being cut off. Impossible to listen to a book that way. So, if only using for music it would be fine, but not for podcasts or books.

title: Angle 3 Shower: How is it this bad???
body: First off, this is a review for the Angle 3 Shower waterproof model, not the base Angle 3. I love the base model and believe you'd be hard-pressed to find anything better for $25 aside from buying something used. I will be returning the waterproof model and replacing it with the base.I've had 2 Angle 3's so far, and both have either gotten lost or stolen, so when it came time to replace it (again) I decided to upgrade to the Shower waterproof model. I used it for 5 minutes and knew I'd be sending it back. First off: the integration of Alexa and the new Bluetooth chipset that had to be used as a result completely neutered the versatility of the old one. Other users have reported not being able to connect to it, and on my Pixel 5, it resulted in a complete loss of volume control on the phone for the speaker. It was either muted or all the way up, it wasn't synchronized with the volume buttons on the speaker at all.Secondly, the speaker's signal processing is hilariously bad. It seems intentionally designed to get rid of any dynamic range and make everything as loud as possible, and if you listen to any song with a punchy mix there is so much compressor on everything that the volume level jumps around and makes it really hard to enjoy. The processing on it is a football announcer's wet dream.I like the waterproofing, I like the interface, I like the carrying strap and I can't speak to the Alexa functionality. But it's definitely back to ol' reliable for me.

title: Bad/Cheap quality battery
body: sound is great, loud, splash proof, and everything else the good reviews say are for the most part all true. But maybe I got a bad apple, so it won't get 1-star because I do believe I could be on the small percentage of casualties.I've had this speaker for about 6 months, use it about 1-4 times a month, and it's never gotten wet.One day it just quit taking/holding a charge. Plug it in and it shows charging, after a few hours of being plugged in it still wont turn on. Being out of warranty I went ahead and broke it down and starting testing the battery on my bench and determined the battery cell was just bad. Not the PCB that's wrapped up inside the skin of the battery (probably controls over charging, over heating, and whatnot) but the cell itself just up and quit.Luckily it was a 18650 size 3v battery which are available with leads and controllers already.This is the other annoying part and why it gets 2 stars. it's only the cheapest, lowest capacity 18650 around. 2000 mAh and NiMh. In today's world, why not something bigger or maybe even lithium? I would pay the extra $4 in cost difference in the end for a unit that could advertise higher quality components.So I scooped up a new cheap battery, a 4000 mAh 18650 with leads and board. Wired it up and now I've got twice the usable play time.If it would have lasted a whole year I would have probably considered it a 3-4 star unit. But 6 months of light use just doesn't cut it.

title: Compact, portable, but lacking
body: I decided to do a little research on BlueTooth speakers in order to have one to carry with any of my three laptops. I tried a number of speakers: the Oontz Angle 3, the JBL Charge 2+, the UE Boom 2, and the Bose Soundlink Mini II. In all cases, I learned I needed to seriously dial down my expectations as compared to my THX-Certified desktop speaker systems with their floor-mounted sub-woofers.First let me say that I have been a fan of Cambridge Soundworks since their early days. I still use their original 4-piece Ensemble speaker system in my home entertainment center (plus center channel, additional powered sub-woofer, and two of their ceiling mounted rear surround speakers). So I have no desire to be critical of Cambridge Soundworks products.In fairness, the Oontz does not belong in a comparison with the other three mentioned speakers, since there is a price disparity ranging from $130 to $170 as compared to the price of the Oontz. Since this is not a review of the other three products, I will just say that given its price point and extremely small 5" size, the Angle 3 sounded like a toy compared to the others, and I returned it to Amazon. For those that care, I found the UE Boom 2 inferior (to my ears) to the JBL Charge 2+ and the Soundlink Mini II. Which of those two one might prefer depends on one's ears and musical tastes. The Oontz is solidly made and highly portable, but could not compete with its larger and more expensive competition.

title: Fine for listening to voice--music, not so much
body: If this product were intended only for use as a hands free solution for android phones (I don't have an iphone so can't speak to that) for car use, I would give it 4 or 5 stars. and I may keep it for that use. It's small, black, unobtrusive, has grippy rubber feet and end caps so it won't slide around, and is shaped to fit nicely on top of my car's dashboard against the bottom of my windshield, so I can speak toward its mic while driving and keeping my eyes on the road (using it, I phoned someone who thought my voice sounded clear). For music, not so much. With all the fanboy reviews here raving about bass & midrange response etc etc, I thought I'd give it a try as an extension for my PC speaker system, but it is what it is, worth what it costs, just a tiny speaker with a lot of hype. The bluetooth feature works fine. The + and - volume buttons I found less convenient. The on-off button is hard to find, and the mfr has put a sticker on the unit, pointing it out. Bottom Line--fine for voice communications, and that's about it.UPDATE---tried using it in the car as a phone hands-free receiver, people on the other end said I sounded like I was talking down a tunnel, had to repeat myself. No Good!! So sorry I was involved in other things, should have sent this back, now I'm stuck with it. Downgraded it to 2 stars, but that doesn't matter much with thousands of fanboys on these reviews thumbsing it up....I don't get it.

title: Somewhat inconvenient...
body: I've been using this to replace my desktop speakers to clean all the cord clutter, but I'm not sure yet if it was worth it. The fact that the OontZ isn't ready to go as soon as my computer is is pretty annoying. I have to reconnect it every time I turn it on, and sometimes it doesnt work so i have to restart my pc and try again. I think it's supposed to automatically connect to where it was last paired as soon as you turn it on, as long as it's in range, but mine has to be manually paired every single time. It's a huge pain in the butt. I also wish there was a knob for turning volume up and down instead of buttons. It's just kind of a hassle having to pick the thing up every time just to locate the + and - buttons. It's all black so you cant just instinctively know, reach over and change it. Just while writing this review, I switched it from 3 to 2 stars because I'm just thinking about this stuff that makes this unit more inconvenient and annoying! The cords it comes with are way too short, so if I want to use the aux cord instead of bluetooth (counterproductive to why I bought this in the first place) I have to keep it right on the edge of the desk, and really the only way to listen to this as a desktop speaker is straight in front, since you don't get a left and right distinction. It's a great price, but if you're replacing perfectly good speakers just to go cordless, expect to find some frustration with the change.

title: Low Sound
body: I have actually purchased two of these items and have problems with both. Here is the situation:I owned an iPhone 7 Plus and used the blu-tooth connection to power this speaker. For well over a year it worked fine. Suddenly the volume dropped extremely low. I first thought that it was the speaker so, after trying several things, I ordered another one. The same problem existed. Then I just assumed it was the iPhone. I recently bought an iPhone 11 Pro Max and, to my dismay, the same problem existed with both of the speakers I had purchased.I made an effort to contact the manufacturer and the only way to do this is to give them your purchase information, which I did. I never heard back from them. As you might expect, I tried the various troubleshooting suggestions that were available and none worked.So, today I have ordered the Mix Hero T102 Plus. It should arrive next week. It has similar features as the others but appears to have higher output. So, we'll see...But I want to caution anyone buying Oontz Angle 3 because you might find that you will have the same sound problems I encountered. The truly strange part about this is that the first speaker worked perfectly for a very long time. I literally have no idea why this problem occurred. I have to say that even getting information for trying to resolve it was not at all impressive.

title: Owned 2 of these, both had problems
body: I have owned 2 of these and I use them 24 hrs / day, all day, every day, to broadcast white noise. Bass is somewhat important to me, as is smooth sound.PRO #1: Smooth sound.PRO #2: Good customer service, they sent me a 2nd device when my 1st one broke after I stepped on the wire.NEUTRAL #1: The bass is neither great nor terrible; it's average, a little flimsy.CON #1: The mini USB connector was loose on both of mine, meaning the supplied cable barely even stays attached. This is somewhat of a nuisance, especially when moving the speaker even just a few feet or accidentally moving the wire. On my first one, the connector actually got mangled to the point of unusability after I stepped on it. The wire is angled out awkwardly and makes it easy to break if you use this on the floor. (I have to, my bed is on the floor.) I wondered how it broke so easily, until I studied my 2nd one and saw the same shoddy connector looseness. You must use this cable and mini-usb connector very delicately.CON #2: After about 1 month, the bass started to cut in and out, and after about 1.5 months, it seems to be almost completely dead. (This is my 2nd device.. 1st one died when I stepped on the wire.) Now that that's happened, the already mild to moderately low max volume is very low and mild. I'm now interested in replacing it.

title: disappointed by the sound quality for the second time!
body: I've had an earlier generation model of the Angle speakers from Cambridge SoundWorks. That older model was a bit larger and had track forward/reverse buttons in addition to power/play/stop/volume buttons. I still have that older OontZ Angle speaker (sorry I can't recall what the model name is) and the bass punch and overall sound quality is still nearly the best I've heard for any speaker that size... but the battery is degrading fast. I purchased this new model hoping to replace the older speaker. I charged it up and was connected easily to my iPhone. but then I turned it on and was immediately struck by how inadequate the sound quality was. bass Frequency reproduction was terrible and weak even for a speaker of this size and power; and the mid and high Frequency reproduction was unimpressive. I was so disappointed that I boxed it up and sent it back.when I was holding the speaker it seemed familiar to me so I went to my past purchase history and found that this wasn't my first try with this model. I had purchased the same speaker a couple of years ago, again because I liked it's older big brother so much, and and realized that I had the same disappointing experience with this newer speaker then. I had returned that unimpressive speaker then as well. I'm afraid I've purchased my last speaker from Cambridge. Soundworks.

title: Dissapointing Product
body: Buyer Beware! Updated Review.... Purchased this speaker in 3/2016. For the price I thought it sounded decent and was easy to set up. I purchased it so my wife didn't have to listen to music through her phone speakers. It seemed to work well for that.Now the downside; The first speaker I purchased failed within two weeks. It started dropping the blue tooth signal with a couple of minutes and would not reconnect. I tried several times to contact CSW. With no response. I finally contacted Amazon and there was an immediate response and action taken. CSW sent us a new speaker. Now the second speaker has lasted until December 12, 2016. This speaker never left the house and only had moderate use at low to medium volumes. It was never pushed to it's limits and was never dropped or abused in anyway. The replacement speaker is doing the exact same thing the fist speaker did. It lasted a whole 8 months before failing. Now there is No warranty so in to the electronics recycling bin it goes.If this was a $100.00 + speaker I would probably a little more bent but I still take issue with companies selling junk to the public. This products barely qualifies as little more than a throw away speaker that will end up in a landfill. The 90 day warranty alone should tell you that CSW has no confidence in this product either.

title: Sound quality just OK, power is good
body: My speaker had a defect (the sound cut in & out when I listened to my audiobooks) so I returned it, but my review is based on what I think of the speaker if it had not had this defect. I would actually give this speaker 2.5 stars, but you can't do half-stars & I feel most ppl have over-rated this product, so I went w/ 2 rater than 3. I read many glowing reviews, and based on that I did expect better sound quality than this puts out, but for just pure power, the 10-watts really is good and loud for such a compact speaker. I would personally describe the quality as "acceptable", and the power as "impressive for the small size". I do like the triangle shape, I imagine it helps cut down on bulk if you travel. I did not like that the rubber used on both ends just attracts fine particles, lint, etc., and if you try to rub it clean with a dry finger, you'll just be wasting your time. I had to clean it twice with a damp cloth in just a day, but then I'm a little picky when it comes to things like that. All-in-all, this is probably a great speaker for a kid or teen, OK for an adult if you just want to jam in the shower, but for me this just fell short in the sound quality dept., which is frustrating because I don't see many small speakers such as this in a water-resistant model at 10-watts in this price range. Bummer.

title: 2nd Bluetooth speaker to stop working due to CHARGING...
body: WHILE this worked it was terrific but it stopped taking a charge; got to turn on-blue and red lights blink-blue and red lights turn off within 2-5 seconds. It worked on a car dashboard as a TERRIFIC work-around, bypassing a radio (in a 1983 Mercedes w/380,000 miles on the original radio, etc.)--terrific. I'm even now buying a much cheaper one to replace it for VOICE AT LEAST. VOICE QUALITY is FINE. MUSIC quality is better with the more expensive (not this one) of 2 I've owned.THE SAME CHARGING-STOPPED--unit-is-dead end happened with the 1st of 2 bluetooth speakers--NOT this brand but bought from Amazon. Both suffering the same fate after about 3-6-9? months--stopped taking a charge.I DID JUST read online suggestion that charging of OTHER THAN A WALL PLUG ... might not be strong enough (assuming the unit had drained battery completely)--suggestion: leave unit charging OVERNIGHT, plugged into 110 volt / wall adapter. ...I'll come back and revise this review if that awakens this battery.Again......if you have an old car or for other reasons think of THIS solution to work around radio.....AND consequently get benefit of EVERYTHING you can get ... a TON of good audio ... offline versus today's corporately-owned / screwed up commercial radio ... that DOES work. Good luck.

title: There are WAY better choices out there for portable Bluetooth speakers.
body: I bought this speaker because I thought I had lost my other Bluetooth speaker (Photive HYDRA), but then I found the Photive so it allowed me to do a side-by-side comparison. Unfortunately, the Oontz doesn't compare favorably.Size and Waterproofing: The Oontz has a slightly smaller overall size compared to the Photive, but the Photive is completely waterproof while the Oontz is only rated for "splashes".Sound Quality: The Oontz has poorer overall sound quality than the Photive, with an unbalanced shrill tone when volume is up and virtualy no bass at any volume. That said, the Oontz does have a "clear" sound at low to medium levels, just not balanced.Bass Quality: Is nonexistent with the Oontz when compared to the Photive.Volume: The Oontz has a higher output of sound at the highest volume level, but the sound it produces at that level sucks compared to the Photive.Pairing: The Oontz had no problems pairing with any of my Bluetooth devices.Battery Length Between Charges: The Oontz seems to have a longer battery life than the Photive at higher volumes. At least 7-8 hours compared to 5-6 with the Photive.In all, I think there are better (and cheaper) choices out there for Bluetooth speakers and I can't recommend the Oontz.

title: Large Cupholder size but forceful like a glovebox sized speaker
body: Update Oct. 20,16--------3 months to the day, unit stopped playing on the last day of 90 day warranty. Nervous if they will give me a hard time. Contacted customer service on the weekend got a response right away asking for my Amazon purchase number. 2nd message they were sending me a replacement. Fantastic, very happy. Got the replacement within a week. Tested it. Played, but with distortion now at higher phone volumes. Well at least it works. Red battery light starts blinking, "o yea got to charge it up". Plug in the little black microUSB cord that came with(nice touch) to a Samsung 7 USB charger. 6 hours later it has stopped blinking and grab it to go for sound outside. Won't turn on. "What?", this is how the old one acted after 3 months now it's happening right away. Try different things then plug it in and light goes on and unit powers up and plays music. Disconnect power and nothing. Ugh! I'll update after I hear back from Oontz though they threatened me that only one replacement under warranty. I'll see.Original review--------July 25, 2016Loud and clear music. External phone and volume controls built in. Pairing up to 30 feet. Fits cupholder in my van with the broken radio perfectly, so now excellent hands free music.

title: ... for the past two months and think it's a great product. I've used it in the shower or ...
body: Update 3 months post purchase. Now I cannot seem to keep a charge on the speaker. I'll leave it on the usb charger for several hours and it will only charge half way. I also recently took it on a road trip and it played approximately 3 hours with no problem before dying. Since dying I attempted to charge it and the sound wouldn't transfer on via Bluetooth or aux cord. Sucky thing is that it's more than 3 months beyond the warranty so I can't return it.I've had it for the past two months and think it's a great product. I've used it in the shower or generally around the house. The sounds is very loud and bright. You can monitor the sound both with the dials or through the source of the sound (e.g. phone or computer). I've used it both with my phone and with my laptop, and it does great at amplifying the sounds when I'm streaming a movie. The charge stays forever. I use it several times a week and have only charged it 2-3 times since I've purchased it.Cons- You can't toggle back and forth for different music. Only pause, and volume.- The bluetooth range doesn't do well when I'm moving it from my downstairs to upstairs without having my phone close. The sounds starts to get choppy. (I'm not quite sure the spacial distance.)

title: Developed and awful hum after barely 5 mo of intermittent use!
body: I actually purchased two of these updated models because I already owned an older model and loved it. It's Amazon, so my new speakers arrive in two days. I placed each one in a different room (one in the study and 1 in the living room) to use when I needed them. I use the one in my study at least a few times a week and had not problem with it. I love it!HOWEVER, I rarely used the one in my living room. When the speaker is on Bluetooth it works fine, but if I want to plug the USB in to allow it to charge while in use or use an aux cord, it makes a horrible humming noise. I tried swapping out the cords to see if they were the issue. Nope. When I plugged the other speaker (from my study) into my laptop with the same cords it worked flawless. I definitely received a defective unit but since I rarely used it (maybe 10 times total in 5 months) and never with the usb plugged in or an aux cord, it went undetected this whole time. Needless to say, I missed the return/exchange period. It really sucks because the other one works amazing. Lesson learned: Test all of the possible ways to use your device when it arrives, especially if it's something you may not use often. I won't by another one though because 50/50 is too large of a gamble for me.

title: Awesome sound, killer bass, way cheap price.  Too good to be true?  Duh.
body: The battery in my ancient Bose Soundlink Mini II will no longer hold a charge so it was time to buy a really expensive new battery or just replace the speaker. I bought the Oontz Angle 3 because of the "rich bass" and "crystal clear stereo sound" hype, the overwhelming number of excellent reviews, the small footprint and the crazy low price.The Angle 3 arrived fully charged so I plugged it into a music source (my desktop computer) and fired up some tunes. "Rich bass?" Puhleez. The sound was harsh, tinny and decidedly bass-free. And if there was any "crystal clear stereo" separation going on it completely escaped me. I actually grabbed the instructions to find out if I needed to enable something, download the latest drivers, anything...this couldn't be the same product.In retrospect I guess I wanted something for next to nothing and I got what I paid for. This Oontz will get loud and presumably run off battery for a long time, as well as hold a BlueTooth connection over a long distance. And the price is hard to beat. If that's what you're shopping for this may work for you. I don't care about any of that; I want a very compact speaker system replacement for my desktop PC that packs a punch. This is not that item.

title: Miss
body: Sound quality is better than average; in particular, it's clearer than most such devices, so long as you position it correctly -- it's surprisingly directional. It's also smaller than it looks in the pictures. However, the volume buttons don't control the volume level on the device; they only scale the volume level the device was set to (e.g., to get it to max volume, you need to set both the speaker and your phone to max; if your phone is set to a lower setting, you won't be able to convince the speaker to get any louder). Get into the shower without setting the volume on your phone to max, and you won't be able to hear a thing. Leave your phone in your bag to keep it safe during your trip to the beach? You'll have to get it out to adjust the volume. Rather a deal killer for me, and something readily available on any number of other cheap shower speakers. The shape and directionality of the speakers is also a bit of an issue; it works well on a table top (pool party?), but poorly in sand and there's really just no good way to place this thing in a shower. Finally, the bluetooth range is pathetic at roughly 10', far worse than even the $5 junk devices I've tried. All easily correctable issues -- I expected more from Cambridge SoundWorks.

title: Great for 2 months then nothing!
body: I ordered this speaker on November 30, 2016. On January 20, 2017, it no longer works. It worked this morning but now it will not establish a connection with my computer. I've tried resetting it, turning off and on bluetooth, re-pairing with my computer but nothing. The blue light just keeps flashing. My other Bluetooth devices (keyboard and mouse) are happy so I know it's not me or my computer. This really stinks. At least I'm still in the warranty period. I'll update the review if the replacement lasts longer.Update: Increasing by one star because of prompt response from customer service and fast delivery of new speaker. Will update again in a few months.Update 2 (Feb 11, 2017): The replacement unit has developed a loose USB port so the unit will not recharge. The red power light flickers on and off if the speaker is not placed just so. The first one actually developed the same problem so maybe this is why it failed - not enough power. I have another USB cable that forms a tight connection that I can try. I have other USB connected products with which I have never had this problem before. So, the question is, does the Oontz eat up USB cables or does Cambridge Soundworks ship the unit with a poor quality cable?

title: Unexpected Feedback with Bluetooth on.
body: Just got it today and ordered it yesterday, so shipping was FAST!! Right out of the package I fired it up, as most have a slight charge already and wanted to hear it's range. It has a sturdy feel, a bit rubbery because of the water proofing, but sits nice at an angle waiting to be heard.I noticed immediately when I turned on there was some buzzing of feedback of some kind. The controls are on the side for Bluetooth, volume and play/pause. The power button is located on the back, above the nice and tightly concealed charging port. I noticed the light on for the Bluetooth, and paired my phone. It connected immediately, and for a moment the feedback went away, but came back right away. I turned on some Mumford and Sons (I will wait) and the music sounded great, until it got quiet in the song then the feedback reared it's ugly head.I turned off the Bluetooth via the button on the side of the speaker, and the feedback went away immediately. Turned it back on and it came back. I'm guessing there might be a shielding problem with the Bluetooth receiver, but that's just a guess and I plan on sending it back in for a replacement.Stay tuned and I'll post an update when I get the new speaker.

title: Sound is good but bluetooth is spotty
body: I've had this device for a couple of months now sitting on my desk at work. The sound is decent for the price point and it's compact and looks nice on the desk but, over the last two/three weeks, it's bluetooth connectivity has been spotty. Sometimes it connects, sometimes it doesn't. There are no other devices paired to this device. Only my laptop. There's two "speakers" that you can choose from in Windows - the Headset (hands-free) and the Headphones (stereo). Sometimes it connects to the Headset Hands-Free, sometimes to the Headphones Stereo, sometimes not at all. You can switch between them and sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't. In almost all cases now as well, the volume comes on at full blast and I have to scramble to turn the volume down regardless of any Windows volume settings or settings on the Oontz device itself. Half the time I can't control the volume with the Windows volume controls and/or keyboard buttons either. It's very frustrating especially when it worked pretty much as expected for the first month. This device has gotten a lot of good reviews so maybe mine is the outlier. Giving it two stars since it does work just frustrating dealing with connectivity and volume issues.

title: Crystal clear sound? NO.
body: I recently purchased a few amazing, nature-based, 3D, atmospheric sound recordings that are breathtakingly clear. When you listen to them, you can hear myriad distinct sounds, sound ranges/depths/variations and distances that recreate the experience of actually being somewhere (like in a wind-blown forest, with the sounds of wildlife, and plantlife/leaves rustling, etc).So...I figured how awesome would it be to pair a couple of these speakers to recreate this environmental effect in a room in my home?! Long story shorter, it's why I purchased 2 of these speakers and they flopped miserably. Not only is the left channel/right channel distinction relatively meaningless (where sound quality is concerned) when these speakers are pair, but also the sound clarity is comparatively awful. Those amazing, atmospheric audio tracks I mentioned above? They sound like varied volume white noise coming from these speakers--and I experimented with multiple volumes and quite a few different speaker distances and locations around the room.So...back to Amazon these go, as the raved about and repetitively mentioned "crystal clear stereo sound" obviously doesn't apply--at very least not in my experience.

title: Read the app reviews (android iOS)
body: The size of it is nice. The weight of it feels light and the bass reflects it. (Meaning poor bass quality.) Highs and mids are good.Pairing was easy enough, but I don't understand why it displays 2 different BT devices. One has the word "App" in it. Tried pairing to both. Only connects to the one without the word App in it.When paired, I hear a high pitch hiss which none of my other BT speakers ever have done. Perhaps this one is defective? Tried rebooting my phone, unpairing, re-pairing, etc.A BIG red flag for me are the reviews of the Oontz app. Currently 1.7 stars for iOS and 2.3 stars for Android.The Android version hasn't been updated in over 6 months and the developer site, muzohifi.com is down.The app itself requires to know my physical location and that's both a No-Go from a security perspective as well as an unnecessary battery drain. Perhaps the company can explain why it's needed? You have to grant this permission in order to connect through the app.Either way, the lacking bass, the abandoned software with questionable motives, and the strange low hiss has me returning this one and shopping for another one with a recognizable name brand.

title: Works great . . . until it doesn't work at all
body: I ordered one of these a few years ago and I liked it so much I got another one for my wife. Sound quality was excellent, battery life was very good, charging was no problem.Bluetooth pairing could be pretty tricky, though. Sometimes the speaker would not see the device to pair with it, so I would have to cycle back and forth to get the devices to see each other. That could be frustrating.I used them on the patio during a birthday party last year for my youngest daughter (note: dry weather, no water near them). Within a week, both speakers mysteriously died. One of them appeared to accept a charge, but neither speaker would switch on, and they did not respond to connecting a device by audio cable.The speakers were excellent while they lasted. I've been a fan of Cambridge Sound Works for years--I have an old set of their surround speakers for a PC and they still work!--but this experience has me questioning the brand for the first time.This is a four-star product . . . when it works. As good as it can be when it's 100%, unfortunately I cannot recommend it, because I know it's not going to last more than a year or two. I will not be ordering another one.

title: There is absolutely nothing special about this speaker.
body: I just got this speaker and not sure if I can complain for it being $25? but it has this constant hissing sound when playing via Spotify at higher volumes. There is absolutely nothing special about this speaker. I have purchased several shower speakers in the past and they sound the same. I am guessing these are mass produced in china. I just wish the reviews were more accurate these days. A 5/5 rating should be a product that blows your mind away, regardless of price. I have a tough time believing so many customers were blown away by the quality and sound by this product they had to give it a 4+ star rating. At best it is 2.5-3 stars for its price.If you are looking for a gym/garage speaker I would suggest getting something more expensive. My brother in law has the Altec Lansing h20 bluetooth speaker and it blows this one out of the water. He has the original model too, so his speaker is at least 3 years old. I found his speaker being sold for $30 which is basically the same price for his one. Do yourself a favor and shop around before just buying this one as there are better brands (JBL, Boss, Beats, etc.) with older models that will be onsale for the same price as this one.

title: Small speaker with big impact
body: EDIT: AUGUST 23, 2016: After just over a month, the speaker stopped being able to charge, so it's useless right now. I am going to try to get a replacement, but I'm bummed this didn't last. I must be in the minority, cause the reviews are great.ORIGINAL REVIEW:I bought this specifically when we were having a BBQ and to play music while people were at the house. I'm not quite sure how long the battery will last... we had it playing for about 4 hours and then it started to sound "staticky," my guess is because the battery was getting low. The Bluetooth range isn't super far... I had my computer in one room and the speaker in the next room, and again, it started to sound staticky. Bring them closer together, and the sound is fine again.I've also brought this into our garage to pair with my phone when I'm running on the treadmill and I don't want to bother with my headphones. I love it for that.The sound is pretty good, and it's easy to pair with your devices. I'm happy with my purchase. I wish the battery life was a little better and the Bluetooth range was longer, but for a $30 speaker, I think it's a good deal.

title: UPDATE to Great value - Would NOT purchase if I'd known.
body: Very easy to pair. Good battery life. And the sound is much better than what you'd expect for a speaker of this size. Now, it's definitely not a BOZE or a JBL, but for the cost this is a great little product. Good for a picnic table, or a garage. Or even at your desk in the office.UPDATE:I purchased this product for my garage, and as long as the weather was good it was a great little speaker.However. Since it was purchased for a garage, I leave it in the garage. I live in the Northeast, and apparently the battery couldn't take cold. I brought it into the house to use in the basement, and it was dead. I plugged it in to charge for about 12 hours, but it will not take a charge. The battery is good and dead. It will work if it's plugged in, but no longer on just battery.It's a shame. When I first started to use it, the battery held a good long charge. And all of the other things I said on my first review still are true, but clearly it's not designed to be left in the garage, or even stored in the camper during the winter.I would not recommend buying unless it's for indoor or warm weather use, and you plan to store it indoors.

title: Unfortunately quality is getting worse.  On my third OontZ Angle Portable Bluetooth Speaker.
body: I first purchased a OontZ Angle Portable Bluetooth Speaker in 2016 and it was a great product. I've tried several different bluetooth speakers and was very happy with it in comparison to others - Anker, Soundverse, Aukey. For the price - the sound quality was better than most, the price point was great, it worked well for 2 years and considering the price was worth it. It did die from overheated connection when charging, luckily I noticed the spark. The second one I purchased in 2018 lasted less than a year but after return policy. It wouldn't charge/play/connect. I then tried Aukey and Anker. Anker was the worst, the smaller version did not work at all and couldn't return in time. The more expensive Anker Soudcloud Flare stopped working in less than a year, couldn't return either. The smaller Aukey also stopped working within months and had terrible audio, of course after return time. So now I'm on my third OontZ Angle Portable Bluetooth Speaker and not pleased as it connects OK to Linux laptop but cannot keep connection to Chromebook. and frankly, tired of receiving substandard products, will not be purchasing OontZ Angle products again.

title: Not louder than a dishwasher
body: I got this to replace my five year old JBL Flip that finally died, based off reviews that promised equal volume and quality. The Oontz has neither. I might have realized that if I'd known that the Oontz is about 2/3rds the size of a JBL, but I guess I didn't look that closely. Nevertheless, if I'm standing fifteen feet away from my dishwasher, and this Oontz is blasting its loudest fifteen feet away on the other side, I can't hear the Oontz. I can't hear it over my shower. I can't hear it through an open doorway if I go in another room for a second. I can't hear it if it's pressed against cloth, so I can't put it in my pocket. And it doesn't have any clip, so I'm stuck holding the thing in my hand.I can't imagine this being any fun at a pool party or beach with other actual people around.Aside from that, the sound quality, while surprisingly loud and clear from such a small speaker, is quite flat. You don't get the same dynamics as a JBL at all.It also loses bluetooth signal really easily, so I have to always have my phone directly in sight of it.I'm returning for something with more power.

title: Doesn't have the longevity
body: Having owned this speaker now for 2 years I feel like I can give a good honest review for it.I want to start off by saying I enjoyed the bass level of it for being small while it lasted, but the design of this speaker has some serious issues.The first being that it charges used mini USB. This has been a major pain in the ass not being able to use any of my other USB C cables to charge this. The charge cable that comes with it lasted for like 6 months and its ridiculous short.The battery life is actually a joke. They claim 12 hours play time... playing what kind of music will you get 12 hours? In the beginning I would get a good 6-8 hours of play time from it, but after a few months it wouldn't hold a charge longer than 2-3 hours. Whatever the quality of battery they use, it's not good.Last, the speaker is shot now. It won't charge or turn on. Just stopped working.I know this is a $25 speaker, but I like buying things that last more. Just pay an extra $10-20 on a different brand that will actually last you. My 2 cents from a frugal cheapass.

title: Nice, but not long-lasting
body: UPDATE: The speaker stopped working after 4 years. I was using it one day, and it's now unresponsive. I couldn't turn it off and had to wait for the battery to drain on its own. It was nice while it lasted, but I was hoping for it to last a bit longer than that.OLD POST: I purchased this speaker recently, and I would recommend it to some you guys.Pros:-The audio is very clear and has nice bass. When I want to listen to music in the shower, the audio is loud enough for me to hear it even with the sound of my fan in the bathroom and the water coming from the faucet.-As soon as the speaker on, it syncs almost immediately, so I like that, I had issues with the sync time in some of the older speakers I bought on here,-Maybe it's just me, but I really like the design, It's better than the boring, rectangular shapes I see other speakers have.Cons:-The buttons on the side of the speaker have to be pressed a bit harder than you normally would to adjust the volume, but I use my phone to adjust the volume, so it wasn't too much of an issue for me..

title: poorly design case. Sound is great.
body: The speaker case is poorly designed. The ends are a black "rubber like" material, and the on/off button is concealed under an end band. It is so difficult to see the button that the maker has to put a little label with an arrow to give you a clue where to look. Even after you find the button it is so tiny that, press hard as I could, I was not able to depress the button. My wife has small fingers and she also could not press the button through the "rubber" end cover. We had to resort to using the eraser on the end of a pencil in order to depress the switch. The volume control buttons and the BT button are also hidden under the rubber end piece. You need a good light to be able to locate them. This may be great to make it water resistant, but that is no great help if you can't locate/use the controls. We would probably have lived with all this, but we found that every time we turned it on it turned itself off after about 5 minutes, even when stationary and close to the source. Then, we had to go through the whole pencil process again. Sound was great, case is poor, BT is problematic.

title: Go- to affordable speaker **read edit**
body: I previously owned an oontz angle 2. I was blown away by the sound quality, particularly the bass which a $25 speaker was able to produce. At high volume, it would often walk itself around my counter or off my desk. Unfortunately it stopped working after about 11 months of use. While this was disappointing, I felt like I still got my money's worth out of that purchase, so when looking for a replacement, I decided Oontz would be worth a second try. I opted to go for the waterproof version this time. While the sound is not quite as clear and consistent as the 2nd gen, the bass is just as powerful and I feel the tradeoff for the peace of mind knowing it will be fine if it gets splashed or rained on makes up for it. I hope this one lasts longer than my previous one, but for $25 I'd consider this speaker to be more than worth that chance.Update** the vibrato that the speaker produces has become annoyingly noticeable now. Im not sure id it's gotten worse or now I'm listening for it more.It's not awful, but enough to make me wish I'd bought something different.

title: excellent speaker
body: I use this speaker while going to sleep. I keep it next to my pillow so the volume doesn't have to boom across the room from my computer. Quite durable as I've knocked it on the floor a couple times while sleeping, but still works perfectly. Only remotely negative thing I could report is that sometimes the speaker automatically works/connects when powered on, sometimes the setting has to be flipped on my computer. Overall I would highly recommend this.I'm changing my review .. this thing has become such a headache .. its like figuring out hellraisers box to get this thing to connect. if you're wanting this to be added to a desktop computer .. don't bother. just slam your balls in a drawer every night before you go to sleep and you'll get the experience. I'm not joking .. in the last several months it never connects easily and today .. it single handedly stopped all video streaming services from working. Like i said .. desktop pc .. look elsewhere .. phone or laptop idk .. but if it uses windows 10 .. i cant imagine it'll be much different. closeting this thing.

title: if you are looking for full sound and that amazing bass everyone on here keeps talking about
body: Look, if you are looking for full sound and that amazing bass everyone on here keeps talking about, I would look at another product. But if you are looking for just a cheapo speaker to play music through, then this fits the bill. This thing is the size of a soda can, and we bought it based off thousands of reviews and how people were saying it's so good for the product. Long story short, I am returning it (just got it today) Out of the box after you pair it to the phone, the highs and mids are extremely bright. It is loud but, ear screeching loud. In order to get the sound balanced right, I had to download an equalizer and boost the lows and bring down the mids in order for it to sound good. At this point, it sounded pretty good for a 20 dollar speaker, however, when you use an equalizer app, it will bring your overall volume down, no matter how you mess with it. So if you don;t mind using an equlaizer app and have half of the volume being stripped away, then maybe this 20 dollar speaker is for you, but if you are expecting anything more, than look elsewhere.

title: Underwhelming quality.
body: This speaker had high ratings from a large pool of reviews so I chose to go for it. I wanted something inexpensive so I didn't have unrealistically high expectations for sound quality or overall build. That being said even for an inexpensive speaker I was underwhelmed. In regards to sound, it was ok. It sounded like a $28 speaker. The bass is almost undetectable, which given the price I wasn't surprised by, but the product description implies some level of quality bass. The description also boasts water resistance but on the unit I got the rubber flap that covers the charging and auxiliary ports would stay shut, which negates any sort of water resistance it may otherwise have. The Bluetooth connection was finicky and took several attempts to connect each time and often would drop out all together under normal conditions. Often the sound would skip and cut in and out. Lastly the power button would often get stuck in the pressed position which wouldn't allow me to turn it in or off. I tap on the unit repeatedly to get the button to get itself unstuck.

title: Getting a little less than you pay for
body: Seeing the great reviews for this product, I decided to give it a try instead of buying a more expensive speaker. I was willing to settle for less premium sound in exchange for a cheaper product. In reality, I was looking for a product that had enough volume to play outside when riding bikes with the kids (sitting in a child trailer), or when working outside, or when working inside. The sound quality itself is as expected for a lower quality speaker, but it is within expectations. However, the volume of the speaker is far below what I needed, or even expected. If using this outside, the volume is not enough to keep from being drowned out by outside noise which makes the speaker not usable for a great deal of what a Bluetooth speaker should be used for. The speaker is, however, sufficient for indoors, even with moderate noise (turned at high volume). The sound is just absorbed and lost outdoors. I am trusting Amazon reviews far less these days. While this isn't a horrible product, I would categorize this as below average for the price.

title: Big sound; well-designed; great value. Hiss/distortion/static at low volume, SOLVED.
body: Update 3/1/2017This is an update to my glowing review of two weeks ago. I have since found significant sonic defects in this speaker, and fortunately a workaround for them.My positive assessment still stands for use at medium to high volumes. In those situations, the sound is very good. The Angle 3's problems show up at low volumes, for example, listening to classical music with the speaker on a bedside table. Here there is an audible hiss, and static/distortion when connected by Bluetooth.There are two problems:1) A constant hiss that overlays all sound when using either Bluetooth or the audio cable. The hiss is loud enough to hear clearly over quiet passages and silences. It occurs even when the cable's inputs are shorted to ground, and is proportional to the speaker's own volume setting. Therefore it is generated by the audio amplifier section of the speaker itself, a design defect.2) When connected by Bluetooth, two more problems appear. First, when the BT source signal is low, either because the content is quiet or the source volume is set low, speaker sound gets distortion and static. I won't fault the speaker, since the root cause might be the BT protocol itself, but you should know that this happens.The second issue is more troublesome. Apparently there is some kind of silence detection that switches off the speaker's audio amplifier whenever the source becomes silent. Unfortunately, when listening to a quiet passage with silences, the effect is note…hiss…total silence…note…hiss…total silence. Even more unfortunately, the speaker's circuits make a little click when switching off and a little pffft when switching on. So you hear: note…hiss…tick…total silence…pffft…note, etc. The effect is a very annoying spoiler of the music itself.Here's the good news. After experimenting, I found a fix, one that's perhaps obvious in hindsight. To listen quietly, use the speaker's controls to turn down its volume to the lowest setting. Turn up the source volume to a high setting. The result is that the speaker's amp is turned down and with it the internal hiss. With Bluetooth, the high source volume eliminates the static and distortion. It also stops triggering the silence detection, so you no longer get the hiss…tick…pfft artifacts.Of course, to listen at normal room or party volume you will need to increase the speaker's own volume setting. The simple takeaway is to keep the source at the highest volume practical, and instead use the speaker's controls to set overall volume, so that the speaker's own volume stays as low as possible.Star rating adjusted from 5 to 3 because…1) Audio amplifier section introduces audible hiss at low input volume.2) Silence detection/amp switch-off makes the sound worse. It would be much better to leave amp on allowing the hiss to be constant.3) Cambridge Soundworks failed to acknowledge these design defects and to publish the solution for them, causing a sub-par experience for some of their customers, and wasting my time.Original review (2/15/2017) follows...------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------This has turned out to be one of the best gadgets I have ever bought. High volume, low distortion, perfect stereo sound and easy connection via Bluetooth or via audio cable. We place it in front of a large computer monitor for sound with movies, and use it as a portable speaker connected to a phone by Bluetooth.Some random observations:- I chose this model because it is advertised as water-resistant. A very humid summer in the Yucatan destroyed my previous speakers by rotting the speaker gasket. It remains to be seen whether this one will survive.- Small speaker design must have advanced the past few years - better Class-D amplifiers and enclosure design. This tiny speaker outperforms expensive ones I bought only three years ago. It is loud, emits bass, and is distortion-free except at the very highest volume.- Bluetooth connection to an Android phone is perfect sonically and happens automatically after one simple setup.- Speaker controls are simple, logical, and elegant.- It hits the sweet spot that balances performance, size, simplicity, and cost.- Cons: battery will eventually wear out and is not replaceable. Have used regularly for a month, and will update this review if problems show up.I am completely satisfied, and will happily buy more for gifts, and when this one breaks. Let's hope the company does not replace it with an "improved" model that is worse than its predecessor, creating an eBay market for the older model at huge markups. See Original JBL Duet, Sansa Clip+, 2012 MacBook Pro, among many other examples. Cambridge SoundWorks, please do not discontinue this speaker unless the next one is objectively better in the experience of actual customers. User testing and feedback is your greatest ally against profit squeezing, corporate momentum, and designers' egos.

title: Pro's: Small and Loud Decent Bass  Con's Harsh sound as volume gets loud Not warm at all,has distortion Needs Eq to sound best.
body: This is an email I sent to Cambrige in regards to my concerns about device I am posting it because it is essentially a review***BEGINNING OF EMAIL***As you can see from [the order] I ordered both the angle 2 and 3 [OA2 and OA3] . The OA2 i got for my neighbor. I am having an issues with the angle 3 to give background information I am a Dj, and audio producer so I am well versed in the many variables of sound quality .I am getting distortion from the angle 3. I first noticed when compare the sound quality between the OA2 and OA3. The OA2 has a much more accurate [sound] which is also a very warm natural playback while the OA3 an exaggerated top end, its almost as if there is an internal hi freq boost from about 3.1k to 14k. to increase perceived loudness. I have compensated this by lowering the frequency in these bands in a custom eq i set on my phone which greatly improves the accuracy of the playback and lessens the harshness but does not do anything for the distortion. Its almost as there is harmonic distortion being created by the amplifer. I'm presuming its from the amp that is causing the distortion, as I noticed this under all conditions but more so when played louder and/or under certain conditions: 1] female vocals [Eryca Badu Jill Scot] this is very noticeable when comparing to the OA2. the OA2 is warm and smooth while the OA3 has a raspy scratchy distortion over the vocals this is noticeable even when the volume is relatively low. The other time it is very noticeable is A bass guitar playing around 100hz-300hz[including the guitar harmonics its probably about 300hz-900hz [well with in range of this device] These notes creates a very noticeable buzzing overdrive distortion sound when playing from a medium volume and up[The song was Flashing lights by BBNG] this song is the most noticeable because the bass guitar is a solo at the beginning of the track so you can pin point that this is not from any other instrument in the track . Its even more noticeable because on this track the only variable is the volume : if your playing it medium low there relativity no distortion but when playing it medium to medium high the distortion increases rapidly. the distortion is defintily an issue. To rule some out variables all critical testing has been with high quality 320kbps tracks and have been tested on both the OA3 and OA2. . . and this fault only occurs on the OA3 plus for reference I routinely play these same tracks from the same source on my mains which are are set of Professional Mackie C300's which are of studio reference quality . Im kind of torn between the OA2 and OA3,OA2 Pro:s super warm, accurate smooth flat sound response excellent [no eq needed], battery life Cons Not as loud and its biggerOA3 Pro's: small and loud [extra head room for low tracks] Con's Not warm at all, has artificially boosted high end frequency[not a flat accurate response], further all the high end makes it sound very harsh ,plus horrible distortion as the volume gets louder.Of all these these comments I have made here, most of them seem to be by design for a superficial perceived increase in volume except for this distortion which seems to an amplifier issue and/or potentially a driver issue. Im looking forward to doing a youtube review on this itemNote when reading through the reviews there other people who report these same faults/issues [distortion/unnatural high end frequency] which makes me wonder is this all units or just certain ones.Many ThanksMike RiveraAny assistance would be greatly appreciated***END OF EMAIL***I WOULD HAVE GIVEN THIS A 5 if it weren't for the superficial exaggerated high end and the distortion, besides these 2 things I really really love it. Hopefully Cambridge will resolve this issue. To every body considering this device if your not an audiophile you probably will not notice what i am talking about BUT if you have an ear for quality these defects are annoyingly noticeable! Especially when comparing it to the older cheaper Oontz angle plus [2]. which is only 22 dollars and has a 15 hour battery lifeNOTE IF you want to EQ this for better sound , you want drop 3.1khz by-5db 8khz by -3db and 14k by -3db when you do this it sounds almost as warm as the OA2 except it still has the distortion on certain tracks . the Distortion may be a defect so I am awaiting cambridges response ,

title: Has "boxed in" sound w/ good bass, CHEAP SPEAKER GRILL!! NO low battery warning OR battery level indicator!!!
body: The speaker grill popped off when dropped and now it IS A CRAPFEST getting it back on right. So sick of these cheaply constructed products. Sigh... Taped it back... It's OK now....DROPPING a star rating!The Cambridge Soundworks Oontz Angle 3 gave me mixed feelings. The Inatek BP2001 speakers, had a big sound (albeit hollow) and seemed to have a wider range. These Oontz Angle 3's sound fat and boxed in. For one, I was put off by the size. The pictures make it look bigger, but they are puny compared to the BP2001s. But the day sounds is... Wow. You can FEEL the bass (physically, but not through the air as you would real good bass divers) drivers because there are no other drivers.. no tweeters. The Inateks had two drivers on both sides (bass and tweeter) & since the space between the speakers were wider the stereo depth of field was bigger (when watching movies), albeit not much bass. because the Oontz have a small footprint the stereo field is gone. But these sound bigger depending on how you position then. But placing it on the angle with the fan/air grill up, the driver out, and the port side down - with the speakers on the angle tip - your will sound louder and bigger. But the size does sound bigger in bigger rooms. But damn! that bass though, fat but small and lumpy, not thumpy.They have VOL +/- buttons which is cool when your device isn't near. The Inatek lacked this and you needed the device in order to change volume. Plus these have a smarter charge indicator. The Inatek charge indicator was puzzling. However none had an indicator to show you the charge level. I do like the slot for the tablet on the Inatek which justifies its wide footprint.HOWEVER the most ANNOYING thing is: IT JUST SHUTS OFF when the battery dies. NO LOW BATTERY WARNING AT ALL!! No beep! Nothing! Pretty rude, I think.(Update. 09. 11. 16=OK, The light blinks repeatedly when it about to die...BUT how are you supposed to KNOW that UNLESS you're actually LOOKING at it??!!.. So still pretty duurrrrr)I'll just have to get used to the big, fat but somewhat small boxy sound. But for the same price these are "better"; since I dropped the BP2001 many tines and the plate came off (easily placed back). These FELT better built. And so tiny LOL (=^_^=). BUT NO. THESE speaker's grill is cheap snap on that is hard to place the on without glueing. (ಥ_ಥ)Did I mention the Oontz Angle 3 has NO low battery warning OR battery level indicator!!!

title: Extremely Average Sound Quality
body: I'm starting to think the average person doesn't know what good sound quality is.LOUD DOES NOT EQUATE TO GOOD SOUND.With that said, allow me to present the pros and cons...Pros:-Good, loud volume-Battery life is really good so far. Hopefully it stays good. I've only had to charge it twice in a month, once right out of the box (it had some charge to it already when I got it) and the yesterday when I played it all afternoon in the garage.-Bluetooth connectivity is nearly instantaneous, which is awesome. It has good range. Doesn't lose the connection at all.-Used it to talk on the phone and listen to virtual meetings and nothing dropped. The voice volume was crystal clear, and the microphone picked up my voice really well.-Durable little unit. Rubber sides, so it can more than likely take a fall and fare well, but it probably wouldn't fall with those rubber ends.-Triangular prism shape makes it easy to old and balance itself.-Haven't and do not plan to test the water resistant capabilities.Sounds awesome so far, huh?Cons: Music sounds bland playing through this speaker. Yes, is loud and clear, but it is not a full, rich sound. Mostly mids and highs. Bass is probably less than average, but you get what you pay for, right? Bass is virtually nonexistent and cheap sounding. I would pay a little more for a JBL speaker, as I have used one and it had AMAZING sound quality (louder, richer, fuller, broader). There are other $25 options in amazing that have better sound quality, too. I was hoping to save some money, but sadly it didn't work out. I was hoping the speakers just needed some time to stretch out and settle, as all speakers do, and it helped a little, but a little on top of bad isn't much.I like this speaker, as I mainly use it in the garage when I'm working on a project or in the backyard when I'm grilling or playing cornhole, but when I want a full sound without the loud volume when I'm fishing or hanging with friends, this speaker fails me.I know there are more pros than cons, but the average, underwhelming sound is a HUGE one for me, as listening to music is what bought this for. I like this speaker, but I am faaaar from loving it.

title: Clear Sound at an Inexpensive Price
body: The Oontz Angle 3 is the best selling bluetooth speaker on Amazon and after trying others in the same price range, one can can see why. The sound is loud, clear and well balanced requiring no eq. On some songs the treble can be a bit forward sounding but for the most part it is stable. The bass is passable but nothing special. The sound quality of the Angle 3 is better than most of the competitors I have tried in this price range.Out-of-the-box the Angle 3 came with 30% charge enabling me to test it immediately. Battery life is reasonable, shows up in iOS and is in check with the specifications printed on manufacturer’s website.The Angle sports a triangular design with two drivers on the front configured in stereo and a passive radiator located at the bottom allowing the Angle 3 to resonate bass frequencies off the surface its placed on.The Angle 3 has two rubber pads on the bottom near both ends. I listen mostly to rock however if you play a track with excessive bass like “Billie Eilish - you should see me with a crown” at max volume the Angle 3 will move about on the table. This could have been prevented if SoundWorks installed weight strips in the Angle 3. This isn’t an issue for most music played through these but it’s still something to consider.Pairing the Angle 3 to my iPhone was simple and the connection is solid, even at great distances. The Angle 3 features a play/pause, volume controls. The volume controls are independent of iOS so you will have to set both at applicable levels.Films are a joy to watch on-the-go with the Angle 3 as audio plays back in sync with video. There is barely any noticeable latency when paired with my iPhone. Very important.Color: I love white electronics and would have appreciated SoundWorks providing the option of purchasing the Angle 3 in solid white as opposed to the black/white combination. As the option wasn’t available, I purchased the solid black version.The Angle 3 is a great little bluetooth speaker. It deliverers clear and balanced audio at an in-expensive price point. It could benefit from more bass, however considering the construction, competition and price point this is not a bad buy.

title: An okay substitute for your PC's internal speakers
body: I purchased this speaker to listen to iTunes while working on my new ThinkPad P50 laptop, whose internal speakers are useless.The first thing that struck me about the OontZ was how small it is -- under 5" wide. This means the tiny speakers within don't have a lot of room to move, which places limitations on volume and quality of sound. There's nothing "next generation" about this thing. On a scale of 10 for sound quality from a compact system I'd give it a 5.I tried without success to pair it by Bluetooth with the PC, but I confess I didn't spend a lot of time on the process because the speaker comes with a fool-proof cable that connects the headphone jack with the unit.Ironically, the manufacturer goes on about the extra volume obtainable with this model, but the reality is that cranking up the volume leads to sound degradation and bass distortion. This speaker is best played at mid-volume, which is adequate for use behind your PC's monitor. It would not provide enough volume for outdoor use: for example, as a monitor for your iPhone's music at a picnic. It's just too' small.The manufacturer boasts of the speaker's portability being ideal for hikers. If in the silence of a pristine forest I encountered a hiker playing Justin Beiber on an external speaker while he trudged along, I'd hang him by his ankles from the nearest tree using his packsack staps, upload a Lawrence Welk album to his smartphone, and play it on autorepeat through this speaker, just out of arm's reach.I don't need a waterproof speaker, nor do I require a microphone -- my iPhone has a Speakerphone option. I use the speaker's USB power cord so have no need for its battery. Instead of loading this product with all these gizmos, the manufacturer would have been better off investing their money in something a little bigger, with a little more power, and a "fatter" sound. What this speaker does acceptably well (barely) is replace your PC's internal speakers. In my opinion it has no business being outdoors. Get a good set of Bluetooth earbuds instead and reduce the noise pollution at your favorite beach.
