title: The speaker is good BUT there is NO volume indicator other than the ...
body: 1. The speaker is good BUT there is NO volume indicator other than the "+" and "-" symbols. 2. When you increase or lower the volume, it does NOT change the volume indicator on your system (my mac). This is confusing. For example, if my Mac's volume is on say a 2 of 10 level BUT the Oontz is at a 10 of 10 level, it will have a LOW volume. The REAL volume is a function of BOTH the mac's volume setting AND the Oontz's setting BUT the Oontz does NOT have an indicator so you do NOT know what level it is on. 3. When I turned on the mic on Skype, and was playing a youtube song the output was VERY VERY muffled. i.e., This unit is NOT compatible with Skype on a mac. The ONLY way to get it to work is to turn the mic ON and OFF whenever you are NOT using skype which is a real pain. 4. When I tested the Oontz mic (yes, it has one) on a Skype Test call - the result was a horrible quality. In short, the mic STINKS. It's a 2 of 10 at best. So do NOT buy this as a speaker phone or you will be VERY sorry. 5. Speaking of which, if you use this as a speakerphone, duh, the mic WILL pick up the OUTGOING spoken words and WILL - by definition - CREATE feedback. Why? B/c you really need to use this mic with a headset, NOT with outgoing sound. Translation? The ONLY way to get this to work properly on a mac / skype - is to use the crappy Oonz mic with a headset so there is no feedback. *** Summary *** Though the speaker itself is great, the mic stinks and the fact there is NO volume indicator AND it does NOT change the Mac's volume - well, you really have to just GUESS how to adjust the volume between the mac settings and the oontz. The way I did this was to set the mac's volume to about 8 of 10 and then play a test audio / youtube song video and raise the Oontz volume via the buttons until the Oontz sound maxed out. Then I back it down 25% or so (by ear) and THEN I ONLY use(d) the Mac volume controls AFTER I know where the Oontz is set. SoundWorks should put a volume indicator LED on this unit. Good speaker, bad controls, bad mic, bad speakphone.

title: Nice, but ...
body: This is an excellent speaker for sound clarity. I bought it along with a Bluetooth Taotronics TT-BA01 transmitter (dongle). Once the two were paired, which was easy, I was able to simply plug the transmitter into the 3.5mm ports of any of my TV's or Kindle Fire tablet. In each of these uses the volume and sound quality are excellent. There is some decent base, but of course these are small speakers so it is limited. My primary goal was to better hear and understand voices during news broadcasts, movies, etc., This speaker and transmitter gets this job done nicely. A significant benefit, of this and any other Bluetooth speaker, is that it can be positioned more closely to you than the placement of a TV or other device; this permits a lower volume setting for even less distortion.I give this speaker 3 stars, not 5, because I do have a few gripes:- My Taotronics transmitter says to ensure that speakers to be paired with it must have an A2DP Bluetooth Profile. Nowhere on the Angle 3, or on its box, or in its Quick Start Guide (the only paperwork that came with it) is the speaker's Bluetooth Profile specified. After looking high and low for the Angle 3's Bluetooth Profile I decided to just call Cambridge Soundworks and ask them. Guess what, no phone number provided, neither direct dial or toll-free - nada. In frustration I decided to just charge up the speaker and try to pair it to the transmitter. It worked. So I assume the Angle 3's Profile is correct; I still don't know.- Most of the speaker's specifications go unstated, such things as frequency range, volume capability, Bluetooth Profile(s), temperature limits, compliances, etc.- There is no statement of any warranty, although another Amazon reviewer stated a minuscule 90 days. So, if mine fails, I hope it does it sooner rather than later.You have to wonder about a company [Cambridge Soundworks] that does not provide major product specifications, has an unstated, minuscule warranty, and no customer support phone number.

title: Great customer service does NOT make this a great small/mid-size speaker
body: **Original Review (Update Below): TWO STARS**Original Title: "Cheap and static-like sound"This speaker is inexpensive, is easy to use, and has a solid build.However, it is not that great for music.I had hoped that all of the great reviews would mean I could expect sound quality to match the better-end of this size of speakers (thinking the Jambox Mini) or even the generic small bluetooth speakers, but I was severely dissappointed.The unit played highs alright, mids so-so, and base was terrible and washed out.I hope this was due to a bad unit, but I am not sure.This is a certain return, and I will not skimp to save a few bucks for a speaker again.**2016-12-11 Update: THREE STARS**New Title: "Great customer service does not make this a great small/mid-size speaker"OontZ Customer Support reached out and asked if they could send a replacement unit for me to try.So I really wanted to think this speaker was decent, and gave it a second chance.I tried playing music with and without the charging cable in and active to see if there was any affect on grounding or available power.Voice:I started with the Little Leagues, voice from a podcast.The speaking was clear and level, and low voices didn't sound too tinny despite lacking bass.I did a call with a female friend, and her voice was muddied and not clear at all (and that's compared to just a phone's speaker!).Music:Then I tried music of a few different genres: Pop, Electronic and Hip-hop.After a few songs in each genre, I can say that my experience is only slightly improved.The static-like sound I was hearing before is much less noticeable, but the bass remains a hollow.If you want a very-inexpensive speaker for dancing in the studio, working out, or something that does not desire amazing fidelity, then this could be your guy. If you are looking to really enjoy your music, spend money and get something that is better.

title: Sounds good but does not work with my Phones
body: I like the speaker and it sounds good and at loud volumes there is very little distortion. It connects very easily to both my phones and tablet. A nice feature is you can answer the phone by pushing the Bluetooth button on the speaker and it will pause the audio source. Callers on the other end say they had no trouble hearing me and it was clear without echo or static. It is splash proof only if you make sure you keep the rubber door on the charging port closed.Now for the dislikes. The main problem I have is it sounds good connect to my Surface tablet but when connected to my Cell phones, a Lumia 950 and a 950XL it cuts out repeatedly. This does not matter if I have the speaker next to or at any distance from the phones. I can overlook the other faults of this speaker but the cell phone issue make it worthless for my primary use. Maybe I got a defective unit but I am at a lost to the reason it works very well when connected to my computer and not the phones. Because of this glitch I am returning the speaker. I do not recommend this speaker if you have Lumia phones at this time. My other gripe is the rubber on the ends of the speaker are a dust magnet which you can clearly see in the photos. There is no way to keep the speaker looking good. I have another waterproof speaker and it does not exhibit the problem of dust collection. I had high hopes for the OontZ Angle since I have been using an OontZ Curve also made by Cambridge Sound Works for over 2 years without a single problem. In my humble opinion the OontZ Curve sounds a little better than OontZ Angle but not being splash proof it does not meet my needs at this time. I have been looking for a smaller splash proof speaker to take with me while traveling and I will have to continue my search.If this review helped you or not in you buying decision please let me know. Thanks for reading

title: Meh.
body: This device is fairly well designed, with mediocre sound. It's much smaller than I had imagined; it's about the size of a can of soda (except triangular). That's not an issue, but it does help to explain the sound quality...it's not great. I'm comparing the sound quality to both a "Doss 12W Soundbox", and a "DBPOWER BX-500" (which I don't think is available on amazon any longer). The Doss sounds much, much better in comparison. This 'Oontz' sounds similar to the BX-500 (which was only 5 Watts). Even when turned to max volume, the bass response is just not there. It sounds more like a decent set of built-in smartphone speakers. Tinny, with excessive treble and too much echoing. It definitely sounds like you're pushing a cheap set of speakers beyond their ability. Sound quality aside, this device suffers from poor button labeling and placement. The buttons themselves work perfectly well, but they are placed in the triangular side piece, and are not painted or highlighted in anyway, and with the given the triangle shape become nearly impossible to see in low light conditions. I haven't tested this device for its advertised water resistance, but because of this feature, the input panel/ power button is severely compromised. Said panel is completely covered with a rubber flap, which is also attached to the power button. If you have an AUX cable attached, the flap is so extremely close to the actual button that I feel the rubber covering would eventually detach from the entire input panel (hard to describe, but it's definitely a noticeable flaw), and with the flap open, would completely negate the water resistance. If this appeals to you for its water resistance, it doesn't sound nearly as good as even a cheap pair of earbuds. If you use it poolside: it'll sound like crap. So in short: I didn't like it, and returned it within 48 hours.

title: I really, really wanted it to work.
body: 1st things 1st: The speaker is an awesome product....when it works properly.I really wanted it to work because I love the way it sounds. The third generation has better sound quality and you can hear the difference from the previous versions.I bought my 1st Oontz angle a few years back. I am a delivery driver for a laundry company, so the speaker sat up front in the truck where the engine is and I never had a problem hearing the music from any Bluetooth source. It lasted me 10 months before it succumbed to the broken USB charger curse all these devices seem to end up with. As it was under Mfr warranty, got me a replacement from the manufacturer. No problem. That one lasted me another 6 to 8 months before it happened again. So I got a year and a half from my original $30 investment. Not too shabby.Since it was such an awesome little speaker, I ordered a new one. Plugged it in, lasted me a week and stopped charging all together. Figured it was a fluke and requested a replacement. Amazon sent it, I sent the defective one back and I went on my merry way back to my work truck. This one lasted me as long as the charge it came with. Died within hours and refused to charge. I thought it'd be BS for two units to have the same issue, so I went and ordered new USB cables. Got them, same problem. Took it home and plugged it to the wall with a brand spanking new charger, an old charger, a known working cable and a brand new one as well. Every combination you can think of, I tried it to make sure. It simply did not charge. As I write this, it's in a box on its way back to Amazon returns and now I'm trying to decide if I should just spend the money for a more expensive one.Bottom line: if you get one that works, Keep it! You will love it! but if it dies and refuses to charge, it's not you.

title: Mediocre sound quality.
body: This speaker was purchased over others because there was an Amazon recommendation next to it. It just does not really sound good. I have tried it in multiple settings, such as in the car, on office desk, hooked up to Amazon Dot in the kitchen. It is OK for music and terrible for spoken word. For music it boosts the sound volume of my other devices and adds base. The trouble is the mid range of the speaker. If you listen to any talk radio or books, do not buy this speaker. It makes the voice of the speaker sound like he is singing/speaking on the chipmunks album. I have never heard anything like it. I tried downloading some equalizer apps, but that did not work either. It is actually very bad to listen to this speaker and now I guess I will have to gift it to someone or just let it sit collecting dust, because I cannot stand to listen to it. I am now also jaded to Amazon's choice or Amazon's pick or whatever they call it. It is obviously just a brazen sales ploy instead of an actual recommendation like consumer reports or other rating sites. I suspect from my experience with this speaker that they just recommended it to get rid of the stock. I waffled between two stars 'I don't like it' and one star 'I hate it'. I hate it. One star. I have to make an addendum to this review. Since I was not able to figure out how to return this item, I have used it subsequently in a very limited fashion. The chipmunk sound that I described has not recurred, so I suspect and Internet or provider error. The sound quality is not great, but this is a very small speaker. I am not thrilled with the sound but I’m also not extremely unhappy with the device as I was previously. Rating has been changed from one star to three stars

title: The Cambridge sound quality I came to expect wasn't there but it's still better than off brand USB speakers
body: I'm huge fan of the Oontz Angle 2 so I jumped on the louder Angle 3 when it came out. Sadly, the charging port failed in less than a month and I returned it to Amazon. Regarding it's performance the Angle 3 was louder with a lot more bass but not clearer than the Angle 2. The Cambridge sound quality I came to expect wasn't there but it's still better than off brand USB speakers. The shortened battery life was a real pain. I could run the Angle 2 for 10-12 hours at maximum volume while I had to constantly keep up with Angle 3's meager 6 hour battery. The failing charge port didn't help things since I found myself charging it all the time. I wouldn't charge my Angle 2 for few weeks and not because the battery actually died (which might happen once every few months). The speakerphone in the Angle 3 was another failure point. I got no complaints using the Angle 2 but the Angle 3 was practically unusable since people on the other end couldn't understand what I was saying.I get the impression the Angle 3 was a cheaper design meant to replace the higher quality Angle 2. The specs look a little better but don't be fooled. It's not the grade of speaker which made Oontz stand out in the first place but it's still a better buy than most generics.After returning the failed Angle 3 I opted to get a deeply discounted (albeit 3 times Angle 3's price) UE Boom. I rarely have to charge it and it's louder than a Angle 3 with a speakerphone is solid as the Angle 2's. Manufactures should stop treating long battery life as a throwaway feature in portable electronics. If we didn't mind being tethered to a wall we wouldn't settle for these low wattage speakers.

title: Good, but Far From Great
body: For the money, I didn't really expect a lot from this little speaker. Which, in the overall scheme of things, is good because I wasn't disappointed when it failed to deliver the sound quality as advertised. Granted, every manufacturer of these things promises "room-filling sound with clear, crisp highs and lows, and booty-shaking bass," and no one actually delivers on this promise. It would just be nice if, for a change, one actually did.Overall, I am not unhappy with this purchase. It does what I needed it to do: connect to my iPhone to stream music to it in small rooms and once in a while outdoors on my small deck. However, if you plan to host a party or something and use this as the sound system, you're in for a major disappointment.The line-of-sight connectivity is better than most. But walk around a corner, into the next room, or behind something and keep your phone in your pocket, and you're going to start experiencing connectivity issues. Battery life is decent for this little speaker - I've easily gotten 8 hours of playback time at medium volume before needing to recharge. I also like the fact that I can take this with me into the shower. I used to have another water-resistant speaker that suctioned to the wall, but found that after a few 10-minute showers with music playing, I had to recharge the other one - which was quite irritating.Overall, this speaker is okay - again, considering the purchase price, it delivers a bit more than most in this price range does. However, don't believe the hype when it comes to the sound quality.

title: Afghanistan/Harsh weather use
body: The price is very fitting for the device quality. I purchased it for use on a deployment as an option to play music while in trucks or doing maintenance.It performs well enough in terms of volume, but it tends to vibrate alot more than higher quality speakers which can cause a rattling sound along with your music.The sound quality is good at low and medium volumes. Toward the high volume side of things there's a noticeable distortion, and speaker quality comes to mind.It's not anywhere near as loud or clear as smaller more expensive jbl's most our guys have. But it beats out every other off brand speaker so far.Video sync seems to be very very slightly off on a few different phones. If you're watching someone's mouth move it's noticeable but for most movies or music videos you can't tell.Battery life for now is pretty accurate. I've taken it out twice in a row and it hasn't died yet. It's pretty light compared to how loud it can get.Improvements that can be made: some type of lanyard or ring to hang from something. I know it's angled and that's it's gimmick, but if you want to use it and not risk losing it or having it fall where to you can't reach it, some simple corded loop would be helpful. Maybe being able to pair more than one device would be a useful feature as most major speakers do that now.Tldr; if you are short on money or plan to use and forget, it's a great speaker. If you can afford better and plan to use it more than a few months, get a higher quality product.

title: Loses bluetooth connection when battery charge is low...
body: Here is why I upgraded my rating from 1-Star to a 3-Star: As it turned out, after charging this speaker, it no longer had the problem of losing blue tooth connection. Apparently, there is a condition after using the speaker for a long while that it should be charged again, but the red led doesn't lite up telling me it needs to be charged. But it really does need to be charged, because I'm loosing blue tooth connection after a few minutes of use. Now I know to recharge the speaker when 1) the red led comes on and I can't turn the speaker ON, or 2) I can turn it ON, but I lose blue tooth connectivity after a few minutes of use.For a great $20 speaker, I can live with this annoyance. Here is what I like about the speaker:- It runs many hours with it on full volume before I start it starts loosing blue tooth connection.- At full volume, the sound is LOUD so I can use it while doing chores inside or outside the home.- It has a greater blue tooth range than any other device I currently own- I especially like that it will automatically connect with my phone whenever I turn this speaker on. For example, if the speaker is off, and I hear my phone ringing, I can turn the speaker on, press the * button (by the blue led), and it connects to my phone immediately and I can take the call through the speaker!Previous comment for 1-Star rating: Worked great for one month...Now, almost a couple of days after 30 day Amazon free return has expired, it constantly loses blue tooth connection a few minutes after turning it on.

title: Good if you have a use for it.
body: update: I added 1 stars because the very muddy distant sound quality it had was when paired with phone. but connected to PC the sound quality is perfect (except no bass which you should expect from the size of the device.) So that is worth noting is what you're using as a source seems majorly responsible for the audio processing.update 2: I removed 1 star because this device has problem connecting to multiple pc's in a house. you have to first disconnect it in the settings of the LAST PC to use it, from the last PC every time. That's an inconvenience for sure because you need to plan to take that step whenever you want to use a different PC and the speaker.I work at home and was kind of sick of my desk speakers being too close at times, since it could be distracting. I also wanted something portable to wash the dishes with or do whatever, without worrying about putting my phone in weird situations.This works well if you have some use reason like that.I will say that the audio to me sounds quite muddy. However, like I said I wanted this not for a peak music experience but to be serviceable. Seems to be that.It is annoying that it has to be charged but I suppose I could buy a long cord wall charger for it eventually, as some areas I use it would make more sense just to plug.Overall, it's not expensive, particularly on sale and you might have a use for it. Just don't presume the main use is when you want the music to sound absolutely amazing. Get something dedicated for that.

title: Overrated, better options available.
body: I purchased three Bluetooth speakers in an attempt to find the best quality device for the price: the Cambridge Oontz, Photive Hydra, and the Sunshine M&B. Out of the three, the Oontz is very average at best. The sound quality is significantly better than the Sunshine M&B and roughly equivalent to the Photive Hydra, but there are certain issues that very much detract from this device for my use.First, the grill is made up of very tiny holes, almost as fine as a mesh. In dry conditions, this is okay, but in any kind of wet environment be it rainy weather, splashing water, or even the shower; water has a tendency to accumulate on the surface, blocking the mesh and significantly muffling the device to the point there is virtually no treble. Compared to the Photive Hydra, the angle at which the device is situated isn't optimal for directing the sound to one's ears. Also, the controls are not as well thought out in my opinion in relation to the Hydra. Lastly, the Bluetooth range is very, very limited and pales in comparison to the Hydra; sometimes not even working reliably when in the same room as the sound source depending on what direction it's oriented. Under the same circumstances, I can take the Hydra outside on my patio or even a couple of rooms away with zero disruption or interruption.I'm not upset that I purchased this, and I will use it on occasion if I misplace my Hydra or need a second speaker, but it doesn't compare well at all in my opinion and in hindsight, I should have bought a second Hydra.

title: Charging not well thought out, limiting the usefullness of this product
body: You can't disable power saving mode, which means volume turns down automatically when 15% charge remains and the volume remains turned down even if you plug the speaker into a USB charger or laptop. And there's no way to know you are nearing 15% charge on the way down! Not sure if it needs to charge all the way back to 100% for the power saving mode to turn back off, but I plugged it into the charger 30 minutes ago and it still won't graduate out of power saving mode. The instructions seem to indicate that if you plug it into an Amazon Echo then it will go out of power saving mode, but I don't own an Echo and am not using this speaker for that. So if you to use the speaker on a daily basis you will need to plug it into the charger after every use, which eliminates much of the advantages of bluetooth. This product will not enable you to free your space of wires, because you have to plug it in after every use.The port for jacks is badly designed, with insufficient clearance around the jack, so that some 3.5 cables are not able to seat fully in the jack. If the plastic housing of your 3.5 mm plug is narrow (like with wired Apple ear buds) they will fit in the jack. But if the plastic housing of our 3.55 mm plug is a little more stout, it won't be able to seat fully in this jack.Good sound quality and volume for this price point, though the low frequencies are absent.Bluetooth range is good. It reaches all my rooms one story down from the player, and even a small area two stories down.

title: High Volume, Low Range
body: I just received my Oontz Angle 3 yesterday. It has plenty of bass and seemingly enough range to create a decent sound stage. For some reason, when I first powered it on, my unit outright refused to turn off. Eventually I found out in the manual that, by inserting a paperclip into hole beneath one of the water-resistant flaps, I could reset the device.In spite of that, overall it works quite well. It has plenty of volume, so it should be a good choice for parties of 5 people or more. Unlike other Bluetooth speakers, you can only pair one device at a time, but there is a quick de-pair button which allows you to quickly sync another device. Regarding the frequency range, I initially thought it sounded pretty good, until I found this review on YouTube: [...] Given that this device actually costs ~$100 and cheaper speakers exist that offer better frequency ranges, the amount of 5-star reviews on this product mystify me. Perhaps it is a matter of lack of comparison, perhaps it is the appeal to market prices (seeing as it is currently discounted to ~$30, where normally it costs around ~$100, so you feel like you saved almost $70 and therefore are willing to forgive the product's shortcomings), or maybe it's just a good product and I don't know any better /s. But, for all the criticism I've dealt to this speaker, it does a decent job for what it is--assuming the price is right. For $30, it is quite good. For $100 though, there are much better options.

title: Good, not great...
body: Got it for my car...which predates bluetooth or even aux inputs and didn't want to deal with getting a new stereo. The size is good and the triangular shape gives me plenty of options for mounting. It's made pretty well. Haven't tested battery life since I intend to have it connected to the USB power all the time, which does bring me to one of two cons. The USB connector comes directly out of one of the triangle sides on one end. It just makes the unit with the USB connected less "positionable", if you get my meaning. Would have been better if it came directly out of one of the ends...or maybe need to find a USB male end that's got a 90 degree bend.The other issue has more impact...the sound. Overall, it's clear, not muddy. But there is hardly any bass. I've no idea what a "passive subwoofer" is in the context of this unit, but it doesn't sound like there's any speaker that's putting out frequencies below 100 Hz (just a guess not an audiophile). So, if you like treble...In the same context, I was hoping for a unit that provides a more immersive sound...unfortunately the Oontz sounds like a point source. Very directional. Gonna have to download some equalizer/virtual surround technology to my phone and see if it can help.It's fine for the price that I paid...but it's not meeting my expectations for what I wanted it for.

title: Good sound quality. Very mediocre battery life.
body: Charging this thing is annoying. The light stays red the entire time you're charging and it gives no indication that it is done charging. I can leave it on the charger for a full day and the light will just stay pulsing red. So I never know when it is actually done charging, or how long it actually takes to fully charge because there is not indicator.Battery life is pretty poor for a bluetooth speaker. This thing is a little over a year old and it gets around 8 hours of play before the battery dies.. It also doesn't give a warning that the battery is getting low, it just dies. By comparison, my cheap $20 anker BT headphones get nearly the same battery life. Also, my old Photive Hyrda speaker that I had for 3 years maintained it's battery life very well and would consistently get 18 hours of battery life even though it was rated to only get up to 8 hours.Maybe mine is a dud, because of all the good reviews. But the battery is definitely very poor. Also, I play music with the volume at around 50-70% so the battery life should be at least as good, or even better than they advertise. (Since they rate their battery life using the device at 2/3 volume (66%))As far as the waterproofing, it's very good. I use it in the shower all the time and have taken it kayaking and to the beach. Never had an issue with that. The sound is very good for it's size too.

title: Good sound, but charging port broke off.
body: Good sound in a small package...I purchased this unit after researching and testing a handful of other Bluetooth speakers (Taotronics, Omaker, Anker) to give as Christmas gifts.I ended up keeping the Oontz and the Taotronics speakers, and bought two more Oontz speakers as my parents' gifts.Good for small rooms, especially if you can use a cabinet or wall to reflect the sound and amplify the bass. I used mine in the shower and for driving before I added Bluetooth to my car, and sometimes to supplement as a better speaker than my Macbook when watching movies.The microphone functionality is forgettable... I wouldn't try using this to make a speaker phone call or to summon Siri.Unfortunately the USB charging port broke off of the motherboard (under normal use), so I am no longer able to charge it. This happened about 10 months after purchase. I would recommend since the price is decent and the sound is good, but I wish the warranty was longer than 90 days.My parents speakers are still going strong.I am now upgrading to the Anker Soundcore XL which is arriving today... can't wait![[ASIN:B01DZ56MX0 Anker SoundCore Sport XL Portable Bluetooth Speaker with 16W Audio Output and 2 Subwoofers, IP67 Waterproof & Dustproof, Shockproof, 66ft Bluetooth Range, 15H Playtime, Built-in Mic, USB Charging Port]]

title: Upgraded? More like Downgraded
body: It really pains me to say this, but it's true. I've been a solid cheerleader for everything Oontz since I discovered them almost 5 years ago. I have the 1st Gen Angle 3, from 4 years ago and still going strong and the Ultra which is great as well for 3 years. So I decided to order an upgraded Angle 3 because it's upgraded with more sound, I don't understand how that is even possible because it's still the same 10 watts of output but I gave it a go. Sadly, the main thing I noticed is how tinny it sounds over my original one with the same level of loudness, so no improvement there just regrets. Also, the bass is half of what it used to be. I can take my original and put it up against a hollow wall or an interior door and hold it there and it massively increases the bass tone to where people think it's a much higher wattage speaker. Honestly impressive. I did the same thing with the upgraded Angle 3 and it's barely there with a sound that has almost zero punch. I'm really not happy. If it ain't broken, don't fix it unless you really are improving upon greatness. Also, you can do super Bluetooth but after a few seconds the upgraded one starts lagging a half second behind the original. Now I can understand that this may be something with my phone so I'm not making a huge deal about it. This has just left a bad and bitter taste and has me seriously regretting my purchase.

title: Major functionality issues with this speaker when using it for TV streaming from your computer
body: I formerly owned a cheaper Anker product and it spoiled me. The battery went bad on it so I had to get another Bluetooth speaker and I chose this Oontz. While this speaker has better clarity and volume, there are major issues with it that is annoying:1. Unlike the Anker, there is no verbal announcement that the battery is low2. The blue ON light is on the side, so you can't see it as an indicator if the battery is getting low or the speaker is dead3. The red charging light is in the back, which is of no use to give an indication of the charging state4. While the downward soundwoofer is nice, on loud sounds it causes my desktop to shake and vibrateBecause of #1-3 above, this speaker is a mess! You have no idea if the battery is getting low until you lose sound completely. Whatever indicator lights a speaker has should be on the front so it's visible. So for the few days I used this speaker for TV streaming, I'd run it till no sound came out of it, then I had to plug it in. On the Anker, if the battery was getting low it would tell you that, then you could hook up the charging cable to charge it back up and don't lose a beat.Hopefully Oontz is reading this and will get their act together and redesign this product in the future. I am giving it 3 stars due to these issues; for the sound quality it gets 5 stars.

title: Ok for $25, but ten bucks more makes you much happier
body: Please see my 5 speaker review/comparison titled “The Blue Tooth Speaker Marathon" under the OOntz Angle 3 Ultra speaker reviews.....This speaker (Oontz Angle 3) was part of a 5 speaker shootout where it came in 4-5. I think if I had only purchased this one speaker, and could only spend $25, I would have been satisfied with it, and that would be that. However, in our 5 speaker testing, it was out-classed by just about every other speaker even though the winners cost about $10 more. Money well spent IMHO.As far as the speaker itself, it seems relatively well built, but the least so among the 5 speakers I tested. I tested this speaker across a very broad spectrum of musical types, where I found it to have good detail and clarity, but weak bass and a disappointing mid-range. Piano pieces especially suffered the most where the music sounded rather ‘tinkley” and without weight. I actually found the frequency range of the Anker SoundCore better, but that speaker suffered from a muffled sound that became unlistenable. The other 3 speakers (Oontz Angle 3 Ultra, VAVAVoom, Tribit ) were just so much better all the way around. Connecting via Bluetooth to an Amazon Echo, and 2 different tablets was easy, and the AUX connection proved just as satisfactory.I returned this speaker because I was only keeping the best 2 out of the 5.

title: Not user friendly, ANKOR is better
body: I bought both the Oontz and the Ankor, and in using them, the Ankor is much, much better. WHY? Because, on the Oontz--though they have a little sticker where the power button is--you're constantly turning the thing round and round to find the on button. With the Ankor, the power button sits on the top left, and you can just reach over and feel it and turn it on. Also, the Oontz DOES NOT REMEMBER DEVICES, so every time I use it with my iPhone, then use it with my iPad, I have to go through settings again and set up the bluetooth. That might not sound like much, but in actual usage, here I am, flipping the thing round and round to turn it on, then I'm waiting for it to sync, then it's not syncing, then I have to push the sync button, then it makes me go through settings again, then wait again for it to sync, and YIKES! Three full minutes of minutiae! And the Ankor remembers all my devices. PLUS, in the car, I can use the Ankor and just feel for the on button without taking my eyes off the road. UPSHOT: Ankor makes a better device in the actual day-to-day usage...good things: it's compact (so is Ankor), the sound is nice (Ankor, ditto), but it's just a bit too cumbersome to be great. Hope that helps! Here is the Ankor I bought for 27.99 https://www.amazon.com/dp/B016XTADG2?psc=1&ref=ppx_yo2_dt_b_product_details

title: Short warranty, good customer support
body: **Update to the original review 2/2/17**I contacted OontZ's customer support based on a response to my review. Even though it is past the warranty period (which is surprisingly short), they agreed that both units failing should not have happened, and they are sending replacement units. I'll update again after receiving the replacement units and using for a while to check for long term reliability.**Original Review**I bought 2 of these last Christmas for my kids. At first, they worked well, and had decent sound quality. However, after about 4-5 months, neither of the units would charge. I tried multiple ways--from a computer, wall chargers, etc.If you check Cambridge Soundworks website for their warranty, you will find it is 90 days from the date of purchase!Wow! 3 month warranty. Within 6 months of use, both of the units are useless, except as a paperweight. I can believe that 1 unit dying might be a random defect, but 2 malfunctioning in such a short period of time indicates poor quality control. Check out the other 1 star reviews on this site--the ratings are all the same--great when it works, but it stops charging right after the warranty ends.Alternatively, I have a Braven BRV-1 that I have owned for 2 years that is still working great.

title: How could 12k+ reviews be wrong? Low standards.
body: For a $30 speaker the OontZ Angle 3 sounds "okay." Had I spent any more it would have received two stars. Had I spent the supposedly original MSRP of $99 it may have received one star. In 2016 there are simply too many great speakers out there and great deals to be had. Maybe I was expecting too much due to the overwhelming positive reviews. Bass response is average, I would not describe this speaker as having great bass. Nor is it as loud as I had hoped. For streaming Spotify at home I have switched back to my smart TV for more volume. I realize this is a $30 speaker and not billed as a Dr. Dre Pill beater. But with so many great reviews I was expecting more.I am satisfied with the size and fit and finish of the Angle. The materials feel good and the speaker is functional. It has good portability and feels like it would be reasonably durable. Bluetooth connectivity has been reliable. One gripe is that my charging port is near impossible for me to open without a key or something to pry it open. Perhaps this helps achieve the "water resistant" status but a little tab or something to grab on to would have been nice.If you just need a functioning speaker in the $30 range this may fit the bill. If you are looking to get that wow factor at a lower price point, look elsewhere.

title: Great, but charging port is finicky
body: Really nice little BT speaker. Using it as a desktop computer speaker. Sound is great for low normal volumes (regular everyday computer work). Really like the triangle shape so the speakers actually face me at my desk. I also like how there's no auditory indicator sounds. My previous BT speaker would always yell, "Low battery", "Power On", Power Off", "Connected", etc. and it was so annoying. Another reason why I prefer this instead of others are that it has an auto shut off after 15 min of no use.Using it daily, even for gaming (when I don't want to wear a headset), I don't usually have to recharge it for 3 days. It also takes about 4-5 hours to fully charge the battery. There are definitely better $20-$30 BT speakers on the market (such as the Tribit XSound Go), but for my basic needs it works.One major issue is that the Micro USB charging port (as many others have stated in reviews) is very finicky. Had to return the first one I had because it kept thinking it was still charging (when unplugged) and wouldn't turn off. Manual/Website claims there's a reset switch inside the audio jack port, but never found it. My current one also developed the same problem as the first, but luckily I was able to turn it off, not use it for 48 hours and it's been working perfectly for the past month.

title: Great quality sound, Sound delay with Bluetooth
body: I personally love this speaker for Music Only. When connecting to Bluetooth, there's a half-second delay between the speaker and my phone, so things will happen on the screen then I'll hear them a half second later.There's a variety of things that could be causing it; the connection in my room isn't great, but since that's where I'm using it most often, it greatly affects my enjoyment of its use, especially when playing games or watching videos. My phone is not that great, I bought it refurbished, and I'm already seeing issues with it due to overuse. It could be that the two devices aren't very compatible, I didn't see my phone listed (Moto G Power 2021) on the "Compatibility list" on the product's page.When I do use a cable, that issue mostly goes away, but the delay is still present. There's also a super uncomfortable static-y noise that could have something to do with the cable itself.If you do have great WiFi though, please give this speaker a shot. It lasted nearly 1 and a half 8 hour shifts of off and on playing. The sound quality is excellent, and the base is enough to shake the whole thing when it's on hard surfaces. It's incredibly affordable for the quality as well. I highly recommend it especially if you just need a good speaker for music.

title: this is the first time amazon reviews have led me on a wild goose chase...
body: Full disclosure: I bought this speaker to *hopefully* take the place of a bose soundlink mini (original) that I've been hauling back and forth between work and home. I wanted a speaker to just leave at the office and not worry about it walking off, so I ordered this one based on the glowing reviews on amazon. This was NOT what I was looking for. And yes, I get it, I'm comparing it to a ~$200 bose speaker, but the sound is not even remotely close. Not sorta, not kinda, just not at all. The reviews that say it has good bass, or "decent" bass, or anything positive about the bass at all? I don't know what to tell you, I have no idea what they're talking about. It's not a terrible speaker I suppose, but it's far from good, even at the $30 price point. I'll tell you what this sounds like: have you ever taken your cell phone, played music through the speakerphone, and put your cell phone inside a coffee cup or something like that to get a little bit of "amplification"? BOOM, that's what this sounds like. If portability is important to you and you've never heard decent speakers in your entire life, go for it. If you're looking for decent speakers for the office or home around $30, and portability is NOT important, I'd recommend the Logitech Z313's.

title: Not good sound for a 3.5mm stereo jack connection
body: I had these on my desk for about a day. In the configuration I was looking for, they did not work out. I wanted to plug them into the headphone output of my computer monitor via a stereo cable and then connect a second speaker to the first one for stereo sound. They do give a solid warning that any configuration using the dual speaker setup would not be a good experience with video, so I won't fault them on that. And once I got all the different device volume controls at good settings, they did sound okay for the price. Also, they are much clearer when connected bluetooth only. That I can confirm. So if you plan to use these for sound only and connect only via bluetooth, they are probably okay. In the configuration I was using, they had a huge delay in sound starting up and they constantly made clicking sounds. I have three devices coming into the same monitor through DP and HDMI, so I really needed a wired solution most of the time. I just through it would be cool to be able to have more uses for the speakers. This probably isn't the scenario they planned on. In the end, I settled on a wired only 2.1 speaker set. Even though I was not using a typical setup, I stand by my 3 star rating in that they do support the scenario I wanted, just not very well.

title: Good but not the best.
body: I was a little hesitant purchasing this speaker based on the price point but ended up purchasing it based on the high amount of good reviews. So here is what I think after a month with this speaker.QualityFirst of all the quality feels very good the power button has a nice click and the material feels solid built. DesignThe location for the power cord to me is oddly placed as I would prefer the location to be on the side or lower on the back side of the speaker.SoundThe sounds quality for this size of a speaker is pretty good with little to no distortion on the higher volumes.Now for the reason why I gave this speaker a 3 star rating. It works and it works well but I like to watch videos and the blutooth has a small delay from the device to the speaker so when watching a video the sound dosnt match what is happening on the screen. Most people wouldnt notice this when using Pandora or Spotify but if you watch Youtube you will notice. I tested multiple devices and the same result. Sadly the more premium speakers have a little better timing and for that I give it a 3 star.Overall a great entry level speaker for the price but it wont please many audio video enthusiasts.

title: A troubled start.
body: [[VIDEOID:d4d62857e845ac3b7fa0bb64df00e1d3]]Only worked for 10 days. Then the Bluetooth connection became unstable. The playback became a joke.Update on Nov. 11, 2016: I decided to try again to get this speaker to work. After pair/ unpiar twice with the Echo Dot, it is now working as well as it did in the first 10 days after I started using it.I have had several speakers made by Cambridge Soundworks in the past, and they were all terrific and priced very reasonably. This is why I was really shocked when this very speaker started to have problems just 10 days after being used.I am not sure if the problem I had with this speaker was due to the bluetooth device on the speaker, or on the Echo Dot, but I am certainly very glad that it is working fine now for several days.This speaker offers really good bass and the shape makes placing it an easy task. I have the Echo dot in the kitchen, but every night I fall asleep listening to my favorite music from Echo Dot on this speaker in the bedroom.Added two more stars to my evaluation.By the way, the people at Cambridge Soundworks were very quick to offer me a new speaker as replacement. I didn't take their offer, but was very glad with their swift response.

title: Worked great for a week or so
body: Worked great for a week or so, then I didn't use the speaker for a few months. Went to use it today and it seems to have magically developed the much reported "charging issue". When I plug in the charge cable, the red charge light pulses as expected, if i move the speaker at all (or the cable), the charge light flickers/goes out and the speaker powers off. As if the charge connector is failing and you have to position the cable "just right" for it to charge. I patched the issue by using tape to tape the charge cable to the speaker body at a certain angle where it charges - looks real classy.Too bad as its good sound when working. Glad I got it cheap.UPDATE: Changing to 3 out of 5 stars (from 1 out of 5). After leaving this review the company contacted me with an offer of a replacement. The offer was for a new unit for payment of shipping costs of $8. I took the offer. One star off for initial issue and one star off for having to pay $8 for replacing a poorly constructed speaker that was not my fault. The issue is well-documented and so I feel the company should swallow shipping costs. I bought the speaker PRIME (free shipping) yet still l had to pay shipping for a working speaker in the end.

title: Very Good Speaker
body: So I love this speaker, I've had it for about 3 1/2 months now, and I didnt want to write a review just based of first impressions1. The sound - The sound coming out of this speaker is amazing, the highs come out perfectly and the mids are very well projected and the bass is amazing for the size of this little speaker, I can throw this on my dresser and if it's at about 80-90% volume it will vibrate my entire dresser and the speaker will move on its own its amazing. Absolutley NO DISTORTION.2. Build Quality - The moment I pulled this speaker from its box prison I was amazed. The entire speaker except the back plate has a very soft touch to it, I love this, it doesnt attract lint. I did recently have a problem with the grill looking like its over sized for it, and it finally fell off yesterday. I was rather bothered by this and have tried to out it back on multiple tries and havent been able to fix it. Oontz, help me out here !3. Overal Impressions - Very impressed for the output of this speaker battery life can get me a whole week of play back at almost max volume for about 1hr playback a day.--I will be posting pictures of my grill, and the speaker.--Will be updated monthly --updated 1-19-16

title: Bought to pair with Echo Dot v3 but the Echo Dot sound is better IMO
body: There are a lot of things to like about this speaker but I wasn't overwhelmed by the sound. I don't know exactly how to describe it but it just doesn't sound as full or as rich as the Echo Dot3. It sounds to me like somewhere between an old clock radio and the Echo dot. I would be ok with that as it seems to have good connection range, it can be used with the power cord plugged in (VERY short USB cable 2 feet), connected easily, power and volume buttons clear, set-up to work either standing or laying down... a lot of things to like.I did try to search prior to purchasing it but I didn't realize that when you pair it with the Echo dot the Echo mutes itself; so it's one or the other and NOT both (fault of Echo not the speaker). I simply wanted to extend the sound of what I was playing on the Echo dot. I didn't need another Echo dot (smart speaker). Amazon sets it up so if you buy a subscription you pay a higher music subscription price for multiple devices even if you are just using the other devices as "dumb" speakers (repeaters).I want to return it because the sound isn't that great and, not its fault, it's not useful for what I bought it for which was not obvious to me prior to the purchase.

title: Pretty decent blue tooth speaker
body: Updating my review after owning the unit for almost 3 years: Overall it has served me well for the last 3 years. When I first got it, I was actually very impressed with how long I could go in between charges. I might only have charged it once every month. But to be fair I was only using it for about ~5 minutes a week. I use it mainly as part of my work as a private music teacher to for example play a song that I am working on with a student. The main negative for me was that carrying it in my backpack constantly caused it to turn on by itself! The on button is too sensitive in my opinion. My backpack would be in my car trunk, and randomly it will just turn on (it makes a sound when it turns on). This happens literally almost every time I am transporting it in my back pack. I think it auto-shuts off after a few minutes of being idle, but that's still a few minutes of unnecessary battery drain when it turns itself on in my car trunk. But unfortunately, after 3 years the battery will no longer hold a charge, (so it's effectively useless now) which is the main reason for this review update. I don't know if that is normal for all BT speakers of this type or not, but I thought it was something you all should know!

title: Fine for what I use it for.
body: I purchased this speaker so I could listen to audio books from my phone, outside. The sound is fine if the speaker is facing the exact direction that I am working. The area of the sound is not as large as I had assumed from the reviews I had read. So my final decision on this is, it works fine for me as long as I move the speaker with me as I work, and for the price, I will not return it. It is certainly better than my phone's volume, or having to wear headphones. I have no problems with the length of the battery life for general use. If you are looking for a speaker to listen to music at a party, this is not the speaker for you.[[ASIN:B010OYASRG Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 Next Generation Ultra Portable Wireless Bluetooth Speaker : Louder Volume 10W+, More Bass, Water Resistant, Perfect Speaker for Golf, Beach, Shower & Home (Black)]]One of the biggest problems with this speaker is the charging cord. It is VERY short. It makes it difficult to charge it, anywhere. You need the perfect location which means you can't really bring it outside to charge if you need long term use of the speaker. I like to listen to baseball (GO RED SOX) while I garden and this is a major issue to deal with!

title: Decent for the price
body: Purchased (08/28/20) to use in place of plug-in type subwoofer/speakers for desktop PC computer. PRO: Sound quality is relatively decent (especially for the price) for a small, water resistant, wireless portable unit. CON: As compared to the original Bose Mini Soundlink (not Mini II), it doesn't have anywhere the same rich base quality and lacks just enough base that it reminds me of the old (tinty) AM/FM clock radios from 10-20 years ago. PRO: Unit comes with a USB/micro adapter for charging that can be left plugged in while using to keep the battery fully charged. PRO: Bluetooth connection is extremely easy as tested on an iPhone, iMac laptop and desktop PC. CON: bluetooth signal is quite sensitive to other boothtooth signals either from nearby devices it's been connected to previously or whatever. Meaning, it's connected to my desktop via bluetooth, but the signal can randomly kick out either because I used my iPhone's auto transcript for texting anywhere near it or all on it's own for an un-obvious reason. A quick push of the bluetooth connect button on the speaker rectifies the connection, but when it reconnects it comes on at 100% volume, causing you to (again) lower the sound level back down.

title: The bass is really poor and the soundstage is what you'd expect from a ...
body: Just OK. With the reviews I read, I expected much more. The bass is really poor and the soundstage is what you'd expect from a $25 speaker. Interestingly, though, there is a surprising amount of detail, particularly in the mid to high frequencies. I'm hearing some things I haven't heard before, so kudos on that. Otherwise? Meh.Also, if using a computer as a wired source and charging from the USB port, there is a great deal of interference between the power cable and the audio cable causing hiss. Also, the bluetooth connection is much, much louder than the wired connection and actually sounds better. The micro USB charging port on the speaker feels real iffy. Hard to insert and remove the charging cable. I hope it holds up.Sound= just okSet up was a breezePaired easily and immediately with my MacBook pro and my iPhone 6S plusWorks with wire or bluetoothLightPortableSpeakerphone function is great, though there seems to be a little delay.All in all, a happy little speaker with a few foibles. I would have given a higher rating, given the price, but it is already VERY overrated for sound quality.Cheers,AW

title: It's possessed
body: I gave 3 stars because at first I loved it, it worked with my iphone and my asus chromebook. I've worked in my youth fixing speakers at a stereo shop. This speaker I got was great, could carry it to different rooms in my house, I did notice it would have issues if I left my iphone in my kitchen where I had my router, then it would cut out until I found the sweet spot.But then my asus could find it but couldn't transfer sound to it, it's like oh I have a bluetooth speaker but I'm not going to broadcast. I blame Asus and chrome for this issue. But lately it is getting touchy while charging, sometimes it won't charge at all, no red light, I've tried multiple charge cords and plugins but it doesn't make a difference. Then I'll give up and try again later attaching the power cord and the red light will blip on and the speaker will kick on with out the red power light going on. Other times it will charge like normal like it's new out of the box. I don't understand , I've tried charging from battery, no go. A different wall connection, no go. I fussed with it for 15 min trying to get something out of it , nothing. Wait 10 minutes fuss for 2 minutes and it works. I've concluded It's possessed.

title: Good Sound Quality; Poor Charging
body: I bought this to pair with an echo dot. It got good reviews and was reasonably priced. It's very compact and the sound quality is super. It was easy to pair.... and had I known would have been even easier because "Alexa" told me afterwards I could have just asked "her" to do it.Now the reasons for only 3 stars: It takes a long time to charge and the charge doesn't last very long. I wouldn't mind this if I could just leave it plugged into a nearby outlet, but the cord that came with it is only about 10" long. And doesn't having to keep it plugged into the wall defeat the purpose of having Bluetooth? The cord didn't come with an outlet plug. Luckily I can use my iPhone attachment. But the only place I can charge it is on the kitchen counter because of the extremely short length of the cord.The good news is that is that I just tried to use my kindle charger cord and plug and it seems to be working to charge the speaker.I would suggest that buyers be prepared to have to use components of other electronic devices. Technically, it's usable as-is, but it sure isn't convenient to be constantly having to charge with what came in the box.

title: Average speaker; there are better options
body: I decided after few minutes of use that, i will be returning the Oontz Angle 3.Sound: The sound quality is absolutely FLAT. It sounds like the different highs and lows are compressed into one channel. The bass shakes the speaker but cannot be heard. There is no way that speaker could be a 5 or even 4 star rated product and I question the substance and validity of other reviews on here.Construction: The speaker's body is constructed of metal and rubber. It's aesthetic and has a functional form factor.Battery: I did not have it long enough to test.Use: The buttons are easy to find and give tactile feedback. I did not get to test the bluetooth thoroughly but the volume does not sync between phone and speaker. I was not able to test water resistance.Conclusion: This speaker sound quality is mediocre when compared to other competitors at this price range. The JBL Go2 and Anker Soundcore Mini provide much better sound with drawbacks of their own. The Anker has a maximum volume glitch and JBL has low charge life but I would choose either over the Oontz because poor sound is not acceptable when it's your primary purpose.

title: Worth the money but it's nothing astounding.
body: I find it's a very narrow, but good speaker. The frequency range is more in favor of the middle and high ranges and it has very little bass response compared to the older Angle 3's, but yes it is louder, clearer and less likely to move itself around while it is playing music as a result of less low frequencies. If you use this to listen to Rock or Metal or even Nuage, Synthwave, ambient or meditative music you might like it more than the old Angle 3. If you listen to Hip Hop or other largely bass oriented music there is a decent chance that half the song you're listening to is not audible. It's pretty much just a pair of ultra high-grade laptop speakers in a little water resistant triangular prism, compared to the older models which had a little more thump and low end to them, to the point where they would actually slide around on the table and sometimes vibrate noisily against other objects. I definitely think both of these Angle 3 products should be more separately and specifically marketed as such, and both available, as they are really made for two different crowds of music listeners, but you wouldn't know it until you had already bought both of them.

title: Didn't last but four months.
body: After 4 months it stopped working. To late to return. Now what...Return policy should be at least a year on any electronics.Giving this product one star.After I posted review earlier today I decided to dig deeper. I Google searched Oontz Angle. I discover a company named Cambridge Soundworks that manufactures the Oontz Angle. I contacted customer service via email. Filled a short questionnaire added a short sentence letting them know my problem and sent it off.Within an hour of sending my email I received a email back from Jennifer in customer service department. Needless to say I was shocked at such a fast response. The email stated a sincere apology and explaining the quality of their products and they are very concerned of why and how this happened. The conclusion in the email stated that it must have happened through transportation. They are replacing the broken speaker with a upgraded speaker and including a carry case.I'm very impressed with the customer service I received from Cambridge Soundworks. I'm looking forword to receiving my new upgraded Oontz Angle 3 with the case.New rating three stars

title: Not good as aspeaker phone.
body: I did not buy this for the audio speaker performance.I bought it for use as a table top speaker phone. My review is for that function only.The caller is deep and clear (a little too deep, would prefer much less bass - not an adjustable option)The microphone is a tiny port on the top of the unit. Everyone I called remarked when I switched audio source on my iPhone from speaker to OontZ that my voice went from clear to tinny and like I was in a well or big room. When I got on top of the unit and spoke within a few inches of mic, it got better, but not the point of a table mounted speaker phone to have three people almost kissing each other to be understood clearly at the other end.Shame, the unit with a little more engineering thought to the microphone could have been a winner, but if the iPhone speaker phone mode is better, then why use this, granted the caller sounds better on the Oontz. If every other sentence from the caller is "what was that?", well, no go for me.If the speaker phone is not your primary usage, it is probably a nice little unit for the price.Thanks for reading.

title: Good volume, no bass -- not the upgrade I expected
body: I got this [[ASIN:B010OYASRG Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 Next Generation Ultra Portable Wireless Bluetooth Speaker : Louder Volume 10W+, More Bass, Water Resistant, Perfect Speaker for Golf, Beach, Shower & Home (Black)]] to replace the now discontinued [[ASIN:B00WB91AYS OontZ Angle 2 PLUS Portable Wireless Water Resistant Bluetooth Speaker, (BLACK), by Cambridge SoundWorks]]. I thought,"version 2 was great! version 3 will be even better!" I was mistaken. This is not an update to the Oontz Angle PLUS that I purchased before. It is smaller -- about 2/3 the size. While this unit gets loud for it's size (maybe even louder than the PLUS), the bass is completely lacking. I give it 3 stars for cool and functional form factor, ease of use, volume & portability; but I will likely return this unit and continue searching for a portable speaker in this price range with more balanced sound. The [[ASIN:B016ZDDSPY Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3XL : The Ultra Powerful and Portable Wireless Large Bluetooth Speaker]] looks like it might satisfy my acoustic requirements, but at triple the price -- no thanks. My search continues...

title: OontZ Angle 3 vs Anker SoundCore - UPDATE: vs SoundCore 2
body: Man, this is tough to review. All 3 do a commendable job given their size and price, and based on that alone I'd give them all 5 stars. Given the sound quality itself, however, I'd say they sounded good but not great, which is why I ultimately gave each of them 4 stars - MAYBE 5 stars for the SoundCore 2. You're definitely trading some sound quality for portability.All 3 seemed well built and solid. The SoundCores were a notch heavier, and while all 3 have an Aux input jack, the SoundCores don't include the cable. The OontZ does. All 3 include a USB-based charging cable but none include a plug-in wall adapter. I thought the OontZ had better/clearer instructions, but they all easily connected to Alexa. Haven't tried anything else yet. The OontZ and SoundCore2 are "splash" waterproof, the SoundCore is not. The SoundCores play a short multi-note audio tone when powered on, and when connecting to a bluetooth source, with no apparent way to turn it off, but it was subdued and I didn't find it objectionable. The OontZ is silent. All 3 have raised buttons for power, volume, bluetooth, etc, but I DON'T like that they're all the same color as the case. You either have to shine a light to see them, or fumble around by feel. Annoying and unnecessary. I don't care that making them white or gray or whatever "ruins the clean lines" - it's a portable speaker, not a piece of furniture.Now, I'm not an audiophile, but I do understand that you're never going to get subwoofer-like bass and top quality sound from small speakers like these. For this comparison I listened to the same songs played from the same source (Alexa), at the same volume, 30 degrees off center and from about 8' away. No equalizing - just as-is straight out of Alexa.OontZ Angle 3:Louder than the SoundCores. Highs and midranges were distinctly brighter and clearer as well - actually quite impressive. Even my self-proclaimed "tone deaf" wife could easily hear the difference. Unfortunately, while that end of the spectrum sounded good, it also kinda drowned out whatever bass was present. You could say there was a bass "presence", but it was more of a "whap" than a "thump". Overall it made the sound seem a little unbalanced toward the high end.Anker SoundCore:Highs and midrange were more subdued, which is probably why it seemed quieter. Compared the OontZ this speaker sounded a little like it was laying face down in a pillow. Even when Alexa "talked" you could tell her voice was more muffled. That, however, allowed more of the bass to shine thru and made for a little more balanced overall sound. When a song hit a bass note it was a little more pronounced and deeper than the OontZ. Now (depending on your "ear"), if you just walked into a room with the SoundCore playing you may not notice the more subdued highs, but right after hearing the Oontz it was quite noticeable.So bottom line, if you dig highs & midrange and don't care as much about bass, the Oontz is definitely your speaker. If you like a more balanced sound at the cost of brighter highs, the SoundCore is the way to go.Me? I'd go with the OontZ by the slightest of margins. While the SoundCore may have a slight edge in bass, the OontZ's has a bigger edge in highs - making it the better overall speaker (to me).UPDATE 10/31/2018:The SoundCore 2 arrived. Noticeably richer sound than the original SoundCore - mainly due to a fuller bass. Won't rival a sub-woofer but it was impressive for such a small speaker. Highs seemed slightly more pronounced as well (vs the original SC), but it was hard to tell if that was real or it just seemed that way next to the richer bass. Overall the fullest, richest sound of the 3 speakers, and right now my top pick. Being $10 more you'd expect it to sound at least a little better, and it does.It also adds "splash" waterproofing, with a convenient tab you can pull to open the rubber cover that protects the jacks. The OontZ requires some digging to lift enough of the cover to grab.

title: splashproof, good and loud, nice sound, really compact
body: I've said this in other reviews, but for years, I've been chasing the perfect "cruise tunes" speaker. I need a decent speaker to listen to my tunes while I'm on a cruise, on the balcony. To work for this scenario, it needs to be small, rechargeable, bluetooth (no wires needed to jack into it), and cheap enough that if it gets splashed by a rogue wave or falls off a table and breaks, I'm upset but not devastated (so, no Bose stuff here).I stayed in the less than $50 range, although if I thought a $100 one would sound better, I'd consider it. That said, I've bought over 10 of these over the years, some with the 3.5 mm jack (which I no longer want to use) and some with bluetooth. I returned a few, which from the get-go I really did not like. I have several sitting in the closet, never to be used again, much to my wife's dismay. But for this review, I did a head-to-head comparison of my current 3 "cruise tunes" units, to find a winner.The sound was sent from my iPhone 6 using Pandora and the cydia tweak EqualizerEverywhere, which allows me to tweak 10 bands any way I like. I did the double-upside-down-V, where I boost bass (mid-bass being boosted the most) and treble (mid-treble boosted the most), which to me, sounds the best for rock or jazz. The speakers are the Urge Basics (original, non-NFC) Sound Brick (http://www.amazon.com/Basics-UG-SNDBRCKBLK-Soundbrick-Portable-Bluetooth/dp/B00BC36BW0/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1443218646&sr=8-2&keywords=urge+basics+soundbrick+bluetooth+stereo+speaker), which ironically is more expensive than the new NFC one, the Aukey Apllo (http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00NICDJA0?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00), now discontinued, and the Oontz Angle 3.First, the Urge. At 100%, the treble is good, but bass is very weak, and the sound is decent, but not screaming. When the ship was moving and breeze was blowing, it just was not loud enough, so this year I started looking for other units. This one just is too tinny sounding for me. I bought this one 2 or 3 years ago from Tanga for $29.99, I think. This unit is 3 watts per channel, 6 watts total.Next, the Aukey. I got it as a steal from Amazon (with a coupon) for that same $29.99. This unit is 5 watts per channel, 10 watts total. Volume is a lot louder than the Urge, and the bass really thumps for such a compact unit. The bass is fabulous. But, mid and treble really seem to lack, and the loud bass overpowers them. I still prefer the sound of this one to the Urge, as it gets a lot louder and sounds better in general, but I kept looking to get better mid and highs.Now the Oontz Angle 3. Again, by some coincidence, the same $29.99 (again, a coupon). This unit is 5 watts per channel, 10 watts total. Turned up all the way, I think this one screams the best (gets the loudest). It really cranks. Bass is not as good as the Aukey by any means, but the treble and mids are much better. Overall, I think it needs a bit better bass, but the other 2 are quite good.I'm thinking I'll never find the perfect balance of all 3 with my cheapskate and portability requirements. Of the 3, though, the Oontz is the best for overall use, I think. If you look at the pictures, it's also the most compact to toss in a bag to transport, and the lightest. The Urge is only slightly longer and heavier. The Aukey is a lot bigger, and seems as heavy as a paver brick (but again, love that bass). I also bought the custom Oontz case to put it in, so this will be my new cruise tunes player. For the current price of $29.99, I recommend it as good buy. I should also note, it's the only one of the 3 speakers with good water/splash resistance, so that rogue wave will not hurt it.

title: Defective Item and NO Customer Support from Cambridge Soundworks (with UPDATES)
body: This item worked great when I initially used it with my iPhone for about 15 minutes listening to podcasts. When I tried to use it a few days later, the connection was extremely intermittent with the voice continuously breaking up - the voice was a constant "stutter." I tried:1. Playing music instead of my podcasts2. Turning Bluetooth on and off (multiple times)3. Turning the Oontz Angle 3 on and off (multiple times)4. Rebooting the iPhone (twice)5. Telling my iPhone to "Forget this device" in the Bluetooth settings and re-pairing with the OontZ Angle 3 (multiple times)Unfortunately, the playback was still beyond horrible.I then tried pairing my wife's iPhone 6 to the OontZ Angle 3 with the same playback results - my Oontz speaker was unusable. When I tried using it the next day, it was even worse - every 3-5 seconds, a very brief sound comes from the speaker while playing music - otherwise, no noise at all.After I paused the music, a few brief sounds still came from the speaker after several seconds, indicating some sort of delay in processing the sound.I bought this speaker due to very favorable reviews on Amazon, so I obviously have a defective item. I filled out a form on the Cambridge Soundworks website to explain the problem, including my Amazon order number and other information they requested. That is the ONLY way you can contact them regarding defective merchandise. They never responded = lousy company. Amazon was unable to replace the item because it was supplied by Cambridge Soundworks. However, Amazon is GREAT because they simply refunded my money. It's time to find myself another speaker.UPDATE 1/11/16:I'm not sure whether Amazon alerted Cambridge Soundworks to my negative review, or if they monitor reviews on their own. In any case, very shortly after posting my initial review, I received multiple e-mails from Cambridge Soundworks, very concerned about my bad experience and more than willing to make things right. They immediately sent me a replacement speaker that works perfectly. They also sent me a mailing label so that I could return the defective unit, at no cost, for their analysis. Although I am always pleased when a product works well right out of the box, but I understand that things go wrong. Although Cambridge Soundworks never responded to my initial request for help, they more than made up for it with their prompt response and resolution for correcting the problem once they were engaged. I use the speaker mostly for listening to podcasts or audiobooks, and it is perfect for that use - clear sound and good volume in a small package. For the price, the music quality is fine, but you can't reasonably expect a $28 speaker to produce the same audio quality that you can get from a $150 speaker. For the price, this is an excellent speaker. Also, when you get their attention, Cambridge Soundworks is a very good company to work with.UPDATE 1/19/16:OK - I have now used the speaker every day during the past week and it is working perfectly. So, I changed my initial 1-star review to a 4-star review, with the slight deduct the result of my difficulty I had in getting in touch with Cambridge Soundworks to get the initial issue resolved. Bluetooth promptly connects with my iPhone with a pleasant tone so that I know it's ready to go. The small size fits nicely on the counter next to my bathroom sink, and the rubberized design makes me comfortable that it's not going to be damaged from splashing water.

title: Just Flat Works. Simple, cooperative, GREAT-sounding
body: I am posting this mostly-same review here and over at the Doss Touch BT Speaker page, having tested both side-by-side.The short version is I love both the Doss Touch and the OontZ Angle 3 - you simply can't go wrong with either. They are very different, but both deliver an excellent sound, pair well, easy to use, work every time in whatever situation.My wife and I are both musicians and love listening to music when we have the time. I have bought several Bluetooth speakers and compare this one to the venerable older Jam Plus [the good blue one that people hunt for], and the Oontz Angle 3 which I bought shortly after the Doss. The Jam is great because it works every time, is easy to pair, has good-enough battery life, and makes a lot of noise no matter where you put it as its Omni-radiating design works better than you would imagine. and It has warmth, which a LOT of smallish BT speakers flat don't have. The very good Oontz Angle sounds great and is very simple: much simpler than the DOSS - it has no volume control so nothing to mess with there to confuse you. The Oontz fooled me - when tested by itself you would swear it is the best sounding mini-sized speaker on the market at the price range. But when I A-B'd it side by side with the Doss Touch, the Doss is clearly more capable. It is louder than the Oontz given the same source feeding both. It is almost too loud, too efficient in that if you want it very very quiet, you'll be very glad it has that touch-volume control on the top which the Angle 3 lacks. The Oontz is less loud-sounding given the same signal but IS warm because of the good design with a downward-port as well as the face port, so sound reflects the surface it is placed on. Put the OontZ on a favorable surface and you'll get a very nice low end that is almost impossible at the size. In an Automobile/Truck, as many Oontz reviewers have said, you can plop the Oontz in the windshield-dash "V" and it will make a lot of noise! Perfect for that if you need it. If I had not had the Doss I would have been thrilled with the Oontz - can't go wrong either way. But the Doss starts off clean and crisp and has great "transparency" of fidelity whether at low volume or high. If you are looking for a Boom-Box this isn't it.. the ones that are are expensive and often uni-dimensional, not hi-fi. Buy the OontZ and you'll be happy and uncomplicated.I personally listen to Classical Music when I am working on computer or just busy around a room - the Doss is sublime for that type of music format. If you set it on a table in a spot where there is no under bracing, just a wood top, it is really in its element. Though not 'down-firing' in design, it moves that table like a diaphragm and the rubber feet and mass of the unit keep it well-planted. I sometimes place it in a "hutch" we have,... one of the old fashioned kitchen keeper hutches with a raised top and open area at the middle. The sound from back in that chamber is magic.Like other reviewers, I don't like the audible signals from the Doss - very annoying when someone is sleeping and you flip it on, but Doss has provided firmware switching so if you are even partly competent at downloading and installing the patch to a micro-SD, you can switch off the audible signaling [and more... see their support site]. The Jam, by the way, is also annoying in that respect but we've put up with it 'cause it is a good speaker that "just works".Bottom line: BUY IT.

title: ... Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 bluetooth speaker is a nice little speaker
body: The Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 bluetooth speaker is a nice little speaker. It arrived as requested with 2-day shipping, although my local post office caused a problem by reporting that the item was undeliverable because of an incorrect address. When I did not receive the speaker (or so I thought) on the day promised, I checked the tracking information in the email received from Amazon and there was a notice to contact Amazon. Long story short, the speaker had been delivered but it was placed in my mailbox at the street curb rather than by my front door AND the local post office reported it was undeliverable!! Go figure. In any case, after a phone call to Amazon, during which the rep was most helpful, I thought to check the mailbox anyway in spite of the post office info and the speaker was actually there, delivered in 2 days as promised.I like the compact size of the speaker and it has plenty of sound. I bought it specifically to listen to a single favorite radio station on the Internet that I have previously listened to on FM radio for many years.
