I recently moved to another state and can no longer receive the station on FM radio. I have the speaker linked to my laptop. I did not want to use my smartphone for various reasons.My only complaint is that where I want to use the speaker in some cases is apparently right on the edge of the bluetooth range of 30-33 ft. (even though I have a relatively small house -- 1,470 sq. ft.) so it can be a little picky and start to drop the signal or the signal starts going in and out. That issue would be easily resolved if I used my phone.Also, at least once a day, the speaker loses the signal completely. If I turn bluetooth off and back on again on the laptop, that usually reconnects the speaker although I occasionally need to take other troubleshooting steps. Since I have the speaker on continuously for 6-8 hours a day, losing the signal once or twice is not that bad, although, of course, it would be nice if it didn't happen at all. This may be typical of bluetooth. Since this is my first bluetooth device I don't know. It could also be related to the laptop, but it is a Dell Inspiron and is only 2 years old, running Windows 10.I have not run the battery all the way out and, as I mentioned, I have used the speaker continuously 6-8 hours or longer every day on battery. The intermittent signal loss is worse after the speaker has been on for hours so that could be related to waning battery power. The micro USB charging cord in NOT a charger. There is a micro USB connector at one end of the cable and a regular USB connector at the other, AND it is very short ... maybe 12" long. It can be connected to a charger that plugs into an electrical outlet or to a USB port on a computer. I happened to have an old phone charger that has a detachable electric plug so I was able to connect the regular USB connector to my existing electric plug and I'm good to go. Just a heads up on this.I like the 3.5 m auxiliary plug and accompanying cable which allows direct connection to another device with aux.Overall, the speaker does well for the price.

title: Powerful Little Triangle
body: Ok, well, it technically isnt a triangle, but from a distance you'd be hard pressed to tell me it isnt. Either way, this little speaker packs a lot of punch.I'm generally not someone who is interested in these small, clip-on speakers. I never really have a need for portable sound, and if I do, I have a few larger portables that can play my tunes with acceptable quality and clarity when needed. However, I'm not always the best at remembering to pack these items, so something I can sort of "attach and forget" to my bag, seemed like a good opportunity.After hearing all of the hype around the OontZ Angle 3, but never actually having the need to get my hands on one, I wanted to give this mini speaker a chance. Boy, was I impressed.This speaker packs bass, and a fair amount of clarity into a tiny package. This speaker is truly more "mini" than mini. It's thick. It's heavy. But boy does it sound good for its size. The weight and thickness can be easily forgiven.I havent had much of a chance to test battery longevity, but I will definitely update once I get the chance to use this for an extended period. Winter in my area doesn't present a lot of chances to get outside and give this the use it deserves but I'm sure in the summer this will be a go to.In terms of sound, I would describe this as good quality. This isn't hi-fi, this isn't enthusiast, but this also doesn't sound like a tin can or a hollow, plastic mess. The sound is surprisingly full for such a small system. The bass is HEAVY. The highs can get a bit muted by how heavy the bass is, but can also get a bit shrill on less bass centered tracks. The mids are in there, somewhere. Voice isn't totally lost, but it's also not this speakers strong point. This speaker is also EXTREMELY directional. A lot of speakers in this class leak sound from all angles due to poor construction, but this thing is so solid that listening to it when the driver is faced away from you will significantly obscure and distort the audio.Construction, as mentioned, is VERY good. Nice rubber covering the frame of the device. Solid metal mesh grille on the front. The port covers are built well. For the price, this is standout quality compared to some of the "premium" offerings.This brings me to value. I find mini speakers hard to recommend because there are always compromises. This has the compromise of being on the heavy/bulky side of a "mini" speaker. While it does sound good, it is mono and VERY directional. Good for personal audio, but in any scenario where there may be a lot of background or ambient noise, this could suffer. Sound quality is fine, low lows, high highs, muddy mids. Overall, this speaker is great for certain situations and to have as an everyday carry speaker. However, for entertainment purposes or parties, or anything where you may want to share music with others, there are better options with similar levels of audio, which makes it really difficult to ever recommend mini/clip speakers. This is definitely a niche product, but perhaps you fall into that niche or just have a want for something easy to carry around.

title: Quality, good performance, low price
body: I have the older OontZ Ultra-Portable Wireless speaker. This unit is a replacement. It's a great speaker, with many of the Oontz features, but updated for better performance. The sound quality is better than my previous Oontz with more bass and richer highs and midrange. I use this as a bedroom speaker for relaxation and sleeping sound tracks like ocean and forest sounds. The volume is great and has plenty of range and power for the room. The sound quality doesn't suffer at higher volumes. I listen to ambient sounds as I mentioned above as well as acoustic guitar and blue grass. I've been pleased with the sound quality for that type of music. I've used the speaker outside on my deck at a picnic table and it had plenty of power to fill the area. Many people describe sound quality from these type of speakers as "tinny" or weak, however this speaker is not like that. A great feature of the speaker is the hands free calling feature. This works seamlessly when the speaker is paired with my phone (iPhone 6+) and I'm pleased with the call sound and the microphone. The company claims this speaker is redesigned to be more powerful and have deeper bass. I agree with that claim, however my expectations are aligned with a speaker of this type and size. You should not be expecting sound like you would get from larger stereo speakers. My recommendation for the BEST performance and sound quality is to also download a high quality Equalizer App for your iPhone and use it when playing your music. The app I use is called "Equalizer" from Audioforge. My previous Oontz was difficult to pair, but pairing this speaker is quick and easy. The annoying blue light that was located on the front of the older Oontz has been relocated to the side panel of this speaker. My experience with battery life is about 7 -12 hours between charges. However, I would like a longer charging cord. The included USB cord is quite short (6 inches) and has to located directly near the power source. The speaker does not come with a wall charger. A 3.5 mm, 32 inch audio cable is included. This speaker is designed to be water proof and as such the controls are on the sealed, silicone end panel and have raised ridges to help you locate them. The charging port and 3.5 mm jack port are located on the back panel of the speaker under a tight, silicone flap that's just a bit tricky to open. The sealed power button is directly above the ports. The front panel is a typical metal, perforated grill. The bass radiators are on the bottom panel along with nonskid silicone feet. Overall, I enjoy this speaker, I am pleased with its performance, the sale price was low compared to the quality and features and Cambridge is a known, established, American company. I've contacted Cambridge customer service and received prompt and expert service. The Cambridge website has plenty of resources and information as well. If you need a quality, portable speaker with great performance, give this one a try.

title: Very surprising, especially for its size. (updated)
body: (should now be a 4.5 star rating)I didn't look at all the pics, and wasn't expecting it to be as small it was. I was expecting it to be 3-4 inches longer, although the height and width seem about right.That's being said, I was more than pleasantly surprised by just how loud it was, and even happier with the base it puts out.The only 2 negatives I can find(haven't had a chance to even slightly test battery life) is that a bit of distortion creeps in when right at the max volume and the music has a lot of base to it. This is something all amplifiers do and has nothing to do with quality, or the speakers themselves. The only fix is to program the amp to not go above a certain percentage of its max. This of course lowers sound volume for everything.So just remember to not use it at max volume if playing heavy base.(and lots of distortion can lead to damage over time)And the other is the volume control on my phone has no affect on the speaker volume, even though it shows the Bluetooth symbol when connected. But I can turn down my key press sounds using the phones controls. And you'll need to if they're as loud as mine. The volume controls on the speaker work perfectly though, so not that big of a deal since the phone will be plugged in and the speaker will be moving with me as I work.I bought this to replace the "Anker soundcore" I destroyed (wasn't water resistant at all, and I introduced it to a bunch of water). This only has 2 more watts, but is more than just noticeably louder. It's quite a bit louder and the base is probably double.(Battery life update)Unplugged the speaker at 10:47AM and the battery died at 9:03PM. It played music for all but about 20 minutes of phone talking, which I did through the speaker(other person said they could here me fine but could tell I was on speaker), and about half an hour of websearch(speaker was still connected).Everyone should know that when a company gives battery life estimates, it's under ideal conditions, such as the speaker being close to the phone with clear line of site, and the volume at a level you could easily talk around.I had 2 interior walls between the speaker and phone for just over 5 hours and a single brick wall between them for almost 3 hours, with the volume up around 75%(going by how loud it sounded), and the battery still made it over 10 hours. I'll never be in a situation to test the battery under ideal conditions, but over 10 hours through walls and thumping the volume gets a big thumbs up from me.Wish we still used the 10 point rating system online like in the late 90's, the volume button on my phone not controlling the volume on the speaker most definitely isn't worth losing a whole point(especially since we all know it could just be my phone) But it shouldn't get all 5 like its perfect either.

title: I was going to hold off and buy a better brand (JBL
body: I'm usually a bit hesitant to write reviews, (I don't want my opinion to be the one to dissuade someone from buying something that will provided them with just they're looking for, or encourage them to buy something that they don't truly want), but I had to say something about this speaker.I held off on buying this speaker because at its price point I didn't expect it to offer decent sound. I was going to hold off and buy a better brand (JBL, Harmon Kardon) so that I would have a great sounding speaker.I decided to go cheap and buy this one because it had decent reviews, and I wanted something cheap for the garage. Boy was I wrong!Out of the box, I thought, "it's small and I got what I paid for". It fits into my hand and I'm not a big guy. I figured it would sound pretty much like every small Bluetooth speaker I had seen, tinny and weak in volume. Nope!Here's the thing about small speakers that I have heard; location is everything. I bought the Echo a couple of years ago and read (after the fact) that the sound was not great. That it lacked bass. However, if I placed the Echo on a wooden surface, the bass boomed and the sound was more rounded. Move the Echo to a ceramic or glass surface, and that bass fades. Place the Echo to the side of the room, you lose that "rounded" sound. Same thing with this speaker...I charged it and placed on my wooden desk and paired it with my Dot. Contrary to some reviewers that have had problems with pairing this speaker, it locked on in seconds. It also paired with my cheap phone just as fast. I asked Alexa to play "Black Cow" by Steely Dan. I was floored!!! It sounded incredible for a speaker that is smaller than the satellite speakers for my stereo system. Full, rich, and plenty of bass! I then put it in my truck for the ride to work one day, and it lost most of that bass, but there is no wood in my truck and the plastic or carpet absorbed most of it.The bass is generated from a "radiator" that sends the signal out of the bottom. If that signal is absorbed, or reflected off of a harder surface, it will be diminished.I was going to spend another 50 bucks for a second Dot for the bedroom, but now, by spending 30 bucks I can move my Echo from the dining room to the bedroom and have a great sounding speaker that I can move between the dining room and the garage. With the Fire Voice Remote, I can play or skip songs anywhere I am with the Oontz even though the Dot is in the living room.I only gave out four stars because I have to change the pairing with the Dot from the stereo/theater system to the Oontz through the Alexa app, and because I do not get 12 hours of playback from a full charge. The latter because I play my music loud and proud!I'm impressed enough that I may buy a second for work!

title: The UE stuff is crap unless you really want the "360 degree" muffled sound
body: Update 9/30/16 ** Due to the VTIN company contacting me about their speaker possibly being defective, they sent another one out to me so I decided to have another showdown with the Oontz Angle 3 vs. the VTIN Rocker vs. the JBL Flip 3. The new VTIN unit sounded just like the other one, muted with no highs, decent mid bass, at 75% and up the bass distorts heavily, and overall low volume. I guess it's not TERRIBLE for it's price point, but this Oontz Angle 3 has far higher volume, way better clarity and highs, weaker mid bass but more bass extension, and only distorts at maybe 95% volume on heavy bass songs that my Flip 3 has trouble with as well. It's a good bargain speaker and the best I've heard in this price range. (I've only heard 2 if I don't count the terrible 10$ shower speaker my wife bought me, ugh). Also, have changed my mind when it comes to the Flip 3 vs. the Oontz Angle 3, the Flip has a much warmer and convincing sound to it, especially in the mids where the bass guitar and drums are giving it fuller sound. But it also costs more than 3 times as much.As reference, I own the JBL Flip 3, the JBL Xtreme, and the Aiwa Exos 9 currently. I have owned the Amazon Basics 2 speaker unit and have heard in person the UE Boom and UE Boom 2 speakers against my Xtreme. The UE stuff is crap unless you really want the "360 degree" muffled sound.I was keeping an eye out for cheaper bluetooth speakers to go on sale for Christmas gifts. I ordered the Oontz and also the VTIN Rocker speakers and decided to unbox and have a showdown. Right off the bat the VTIN sounded muffled and boxy while the Oontz sounded open and clear. The VTIN had slightly better mid bass while the Oontz had way deeper bass and more spacious sound. The VTIN would heavily distort above 85% with bass heavy tracks while the Oontz stays listenable and quite good. I put the VTIN up and got the Flip 3 to compare. The JBL Flip 3 costs around 85$ normally and is a rugged speaker and is known for being a good choice in small speakers. Honestly I really was blown away by the Oontz vs. the Flip. The flip has a lot more mid bass but lacked the truly lower notes that the Oontz would pick up, especially if you put it in a corner, it's very impressive.I can't speak to the other features like battery and call quality, but I would NOT hesitate to get this speaker. Hell I may get one to replace the Flip 3 as my ultra portable speaker. If it only it had something to hang it by when you are moving around like the Flip...This speaker will go to my daughter, if we have any trouble with it in the future I will update my review.

title: Great bang for your buck!
body: The Oontz Angle 3 is a very good speaker, especially for the price. The speaker I use for most of my comparisons for speakers in this size is the JBL Flip3. When comparing these two speakers side by side, the Oontz does well, holding it's own. It can play just as loud (maybe a little louder) than the Flip3 but also comes with a little distortion when cranked up to maximum if there is a lot of bass in the music. The bass on this speaker is there, not dominating in any way and actually a little more present if listening to the speaker not directly from the front. I do not consider the Flip3 to be a bass heavy speaker either but it does have a noticeable amount more bass than the Oontz, resulting in a fuller, more serious sound. I would not say the Oontz is tinny but it is a bit thinner sounding in comparison. The Flip3 is extremely well balanced, the Oontz seems to have an emphasis on the high range and offers some really impressive clarity in that area. I did see a lot of reviewers saying this speaker has great bass and I think this can be a bit misleading. If you like a lot of bass, you will not find it here. Bass is present, you can hear it (as I said before, better if listening from the side or back) but not really what I would call punchy or strong. I don't think you will really find that in an inexpensive speaker of this size, you would probably have to go to something like a JBL Charge 2 or Bose Soundlink Mini for something close to this size with boosted bass. One big advantage is that the Angle 3 costs only $28 on Amazon, compared to a retail of $100 for the JBL Flip3.Now to compare this speaker to other speakers in this price range, I would have to put this one at or near the top. I have not found another $30 speaker that really compares with the Oontz Angle 3. Most of them are tinny or hollow sounding and lack any kind of real bass at all. Most of them just have a weird thumpiness where bass is supposed to be. There are a few others out there I would still like to try but it can get expensive buying all of these speakers with my own money so I try to rely on YouTube videos and other reviews to lead me to purchase decent speakers.I have not yet tested the extent to which this is waterproof, I did use it in the shower and it got water sprayed on it and it is just fine. I paid for his with my own money with the intention of using it so I don't want to see how far I can go before it dies from too much water.Overall, if someone came to me and asked which speaker I recommend on a tight budget, I would not hesitate to recommend the Oontz Angle 3.

title: The buttons are easy to see and use
body: DesignI’ll be honest, I thought the speaker would be a bit bigger. This is proof size doesn’t always matter. The speaker is 5.25 inches long and in a triangular prism shape. The buttons are easy to see and use. The ports sit behind a nice little flap under the power button. The mic, which I was surprised to even see, sits at the top the wireless speaker.BatteryI played this speaker for over 8 hours and it just kept going. The battery life is amazing.AudioI love the audio in this nice looking portable Bluetooth speaker. The audio quality is really great and maintains great quality even at high volume. You can hear all of the highs and the lows and they are very well balanced. One thing I’m always afraid of is a speaker with way too much high and not enough mids and low notes. When close to the speaker I can hear the bass although the low bass is not very loud. As you step away from the speaker the low bass fades away. This honestly has to be the only downfall that I see. This isn’t terrible though, because someone can chase after a speaker with a lot of bass and then it sounds muddy. The quality is awesome and people are surprised the speaker is producing the sound as good as it does and for that price.The speaker can easily fill a small room or even a living room. I work in a warehouse with a huge exhaust fan and the fan pretty much drowns out anything that’s not even remotely loud. The Oontz speaker held its own and I could hear from feet away without it being it’s loudest. When the fans are off the Bluetooth speaker just takes over.ConnectivityThe speaker offers two ways to connect. One via a 3.5mm jack or through a compatible Bluetooth device. It comes with a male to male cord that is about 2.5 feet long so you can mess with your phone or mp3 player without disturbing the speaker.The wireless Bluetooth connectivity is solid and I witnessed no interference. The manual says 33 feet of unblocked signal and while I haven’t gone that far away, I have gone pretty far from it with it still playing. The phone was in my hand of course.ChargingThe charging cable has a good length to it. The manual says 12 hours of play for 2/3 volume. The Bluetooth speaker does indeed last a long time. I use it for about 8-9 hours and it last throughout the day.OverallThis speaker is going to get a lot of use from me. The design is nice and people are surprised so much sound comes out of this little speaker. I am very happy with this purchase.

title: so I've used these for almost six months now and I still really like them. TL
body: Okay, so I've used these for almost six months now and I still really like them. TL;DR: A great $30 purchase. If you don't need anything fancy, grab these speakers and they'll hold up just fine for you and sound great.Full review:So, here's why I wanted them, and this will tell you why I voted 4 stars:1. Small, but powerful speakers to fill up the room2. Waterproof or resistant so I can use them outside or on the beach and not worry about rain3. A microphone so I can talk to people while I walk around my room4. Something I can use in a car that doesn't have a 3.5mm input or bluetooth5. Something that's durable and looks coolHere's how it rates1. These things are surprisingly powerful. The audio is good, and the reason I bought these was because I walked into my campus bookstore and was surprised to discover the music came from these speakers, and not from a ceiling-installed sound system. Despite its size, the music was able to be played at a comfortable level that you could hear in almost every corner of the bookstore. As such, they've been great for the house.2. They are indeed water resistant. They haven't broken in the shower or the rain, so they're doing good so far.3. This is where it loses a star: the microphone is acceptable, but the audio playback from my phone is AWFUL. I lose about 60-75% of my volume power. It's barely audible and distinguishable over something as quiet as a fan at full-speed, even at full volume on both my phone and the speakers. Without a fan it's still unacceptable. I'm pretty disappointed in this regard, and would not recommend it to anyone who needs a microphone on their bluetooth speakers. If you don't care about calling capabilities, then forget this point and assume I gave this 5/5 stars.4. They work pretty well in a car, as well. I mean, I'm not expecting them to sound as good as the car sound system (I almost always wish the cars I take rides in would allow me to connect my phone instead of using this)5. So far they have held up well. I accidentally dropped them onto concrete from standing height and they still work just fine. I wouldn't recommend trying this yourself, though. Maybe I was lucky.So there you go! For $27.99 it's an excellent buy, though there are louder speakers out there. I tried using them for a movie night but I was blowing them out trying to make it loud enough to do the movie justice.

title: Functional design, good sound quality with useful little features that simply work.
body: I have been using this little guy for a few months now and all the perks and quirks that came along proved to be more useful than I initially thought they could be.Bluetooth connection ― I have a rather small room with minimal space so I usually let it stands up straight, that's not to say it doesn't do well in spacious environment. It's rather good in that aspect. In addition, it fits in even the smallest corner of my usual busy work station or high up on a shelf chocked full of books and still create crispy sounds with little to no disruptions. Walls may be a problem but that's understandable. The best way is to put the guy in the same room or same space with the source and it could easily go on and on without annoying interference.The 15-minute auto shut-off ― This has helped me multiple times. Being a rather forgetful fellow, I usually don't remember to turn off the little guy after using it for a while. I just turn off the music but often forget to turn the speaker off so the 15-minute limit does the job of saving the battery for longer usage before needing to charge.Sound quality is actually not that great but still better than a lot of products of the same price range that I have tried before. Whenever it connects, you will hear a little "ding" indicating successful connection to the source. That doesn't happen when you turn off, thou. You could also tell how loud the sounds will be from this little "ding" and can adjust your volume before actually press play from the sources.One thing I have noticed a lot of times is that whenever I start the sources (computer, laptops, phones, etc.) right after turning this guy on - around 1 seconds between sequential actions - it usually doesn't connect at all and I need to reset the connection by deleting it from the sources and add it again, making it go back to its original settings when it first connected to the sources. It's not something I worry about too much but I just wish I didn't need to go through the experience and that the speaker should have worked just fine in those situations.Overall, I think the quality is good. Not too bad, but could improve. It's well built and functions as good as any other good speakers that could range way higher than its' price, like the 40-ish and some above. For those of you who just want a simple speaker with minimal design, sits comfortably in your backpacks and does its' job nicely, this is the one to go with.

title: Average sound but bargain price
body: Many years ago, I would receive the Cambridge SoundWorks catalog at my house. We're talking back in the 90s when I was a teenager. It's a name I've always respected but the bad side to that is I have higher expectations on them. This bluetooth speaker has been a very versatile option for us but it's not because of its sound.We have two Bose Mini-Soundlink IIs but as good as they sound, they have a charging base they have to sit on while charging. That's not too frustrating but I kept thinking if I had one that was more movable and versatile, I would be able to leave the Bose where I like them and that's in my office and in the bedroom. I bought this solely on the name and price. It was around thirty dollars when I bought it (2017).First things first, it charges without the need for a base. You just plug that charging cord in and it can be charged via a regular wall outlet or anything with a USB port. The features are minimal and for this price that's about what you should expect. The sound is not bad. It has the same problem most bluetooth speakers have and that is it tries to sound stereo (2 way or more) but it still sounds like all the sound is coming from one speaker. Think about the old clock radios and counter radios that had one speaker in them. I have also noticed that all bluetooth speakers have a warm-in period, meaning, they will sound more full and rich after a few days of using them.If I rated the Bose a 9 out of 10 on sound, I would rate this Oontz a 6 out of 10. That's my way of saying it could be worse or it could be better, so it lands in the average sounding area. I probably would have been tempted to still give it a 5 star since I can take it to the basement, take it outside or wherever I want. By the way, if you're wondering, you do not have to be plugged in to listen to this. I know that seems obvious but there are a lot of people who think they have to keep these speakers plugged in while listening. Charging takes roughly 2 hours. Back to the reason I chose 4 instead of 5. I don't like the control buttons. They are nice and molded into the design but I can't even see them most of the time. It's minor but it's enough when combined with the average sound to just give a 4. Don't let me deter you. I would buy it again. I've heard no less than 5 bluetooth speakers that don't sound as good as this one. For the price, portability and at least average sound it's a very good deal for the price.

title: Inexpensive, handy, decent 🔉 but only lasts 8-10 mo😔..
body: At only $22 it's overall pretty decent.. despite a few small grievances ..until ultimate death🥀☠️... It's small but loud & fits easily on a window ledge or other spot to prop nearby... cuz there's unfortunately no 🚫device to hang. I initially bought a couple Oontz fitted cases sold separately & returned all as they were to simply zip close n carry.. useless for my needs..Connectivity🔗: 😀pretty easy & quick.. however if you need to momentarily disconnect.. I ALWAYS must turn speaker power 📴completely & turn back on to reconnect. But it always does & does so quick n easy.Phone 📱 call/ microphone 🎤 dictation: 👎totally useless. Siri never understands the words I’m saying and phone📞 calls on both ends are rather impossible. Hence having to turn 📴🔛to reconnect Bluetooth. But- that ALWAYS solves issue.Battery🔋 life:🙌 Pretty Dang Long!.. I use it in the restroom.. it's a while before requiring a recharge. They say it's 14 hours life.. I wouldn't disagree.Sound: decent. I use mostly for talk 📻.. I listen often to SXM comedy greats or Howard Stern ..his voice is pretty bassy which can be hard to hear/listen with showe🚿r (or even sink) water🚰💦 running on some 🔈speakers.. not this one!!! The sound cuts right through !🥇The manual buttons on device : 🔵🦷, */-, doesn't appear to include volume😖. So always had to control volume via phone.. not the biggest deal since I keep 📱close as I often have to skip thru annoying commercials or Mary Ann's shrieking or Ronny's... anything😖..Longevity: at roughly 7-8 months, trouble set in. I just skimmed the listing page to re-review deets on cost/reviews/date purchased & was surprised😯 to see that exact 8 mo life statement by another reviewer. For me, the USB female plug stopped accepting the male plug🔌. It's progressively gotten looser to the point & now 13 months to the day- it's no longer chargeable⚰️. I wiggle it in forever😖.. Finally get a 🔴light for a half sec, but never not long enough to try to tape it secure n charge. RIP ⚰️🥀☠️..Bottom line: the price still makes it quite affordable, it’s handy, small, easy to use, transport, place anywhere.. and as long as you know you’ll be replacing it in 8 to 10 months for another 20 or bucks, so be it.. still worth buying..

title: Know why you're shopping for a Bluetooth speaker before purchasing.
body: While my out-of-pocket was substantially lower than the asking price (I only spent $10, thanks to an instant coupon and a prepaid Visa rebate from an unrelated purchase), the Angle 3 is still merely an OK speaker.Sound is veerrrrry mid-range-y, and surprisingly even pales compared to the entry-level--and single-speakered!--Sony SRS-XB01 (I primarily used the XB01 for comparison, as it's the closest competitor I own) and is absolutely blown away by the JBL Flip 3/4 speakers. Now, the asking price for the Angle 3 is about 1/3 to 1/4 of the JBLs, but is only a few bucks more than the Sony. And while sacrificing solid bass and scooped mids, what those few dollars exactly gets you is twofold: Volume and battery life. The Oontz has a great battery (a dozen or more hours, vs the XB01's 5-6hrs)! And it's quite a bit louder to boot.You may be asking, "Why did you give it 4 stars then?" Well, I didn't exactly get it for music listening. My purpose for this speaker is to use at my couch with a DIY synthesizer setup (a Gakken SX-150mkII, hacked with a custom-built 400mm softpot controller, into a Zoom effects pedal), and in that capacity it works amazingly well. It's funny how intended use can influence desired outcome!All in all, had I bought the Oontz Angle 3 to use for listening to music, I likely would have returned it and put that credit toward a better (albeit more expensive) option. But for the reason I bought it, it is good enough if not great, especially considering what I paid.The questions I'd ask you to ask yourself are these: "Am I shopping for something to use with my phone's Prime/Spotify/etc. streaming?" and, "Can I swing forty to fifty bucks more for a speaker that just plain sounds better?" If the answer to either or both of these is "Yes," then I'd recommend looking elsewhere for a compact BT speaker.But I like the Angle 3 nonetheless.[Edit, Oct. 18] So right after my return window closed (of course!), my local big box store put JBL Flip 4s on clearance for $35. Needless to say, I snagged one and am way happier with it than my Oontz. Maybe I'll gift the Angle3 to someone less picky about sound quality than I am.

title: Ok sound, water resistant not water proof. Bluetooth can be sketchy.
body: Ideally I would give this three and a half stars. Its not amazing but its not bad for the price.The main features of the Angle 3 are its bluetooth connectivity, portability and waterproof operation.Bluetooth:Works well. Connects to most devices pretty easily with the exception of the PC. PC it can sometimes "plug(connect?) and play", but on my Windows 10 machine it will start searching and installing a driver when it tries to connect. The first time this happens it will prompt you to restart your computer. One issue I had is when connected to PC I have had an instance where the music I was playing did not play properly with everything above the base (mids and highs) sounding muted and as if they were underwater or under a pillow while the base remained clear. Switching to a phone had the music playing properly.Portability:Small and compact, nothing out of the ordinary here. Nice triangle shape means it can be stood vertically in corners. Battery lasts a fair while too.Waterproof:So I tested this by putting it in the far corner of my shower where it would be repeatedly splashed. Unlike some other devices, this is not waterproof but rather water resistant, with the speaker grill acting as a capillary net to prevent water from reaching the actual drivers. You cant have this submerged at all.With that said, while it is water resistant, once the grill gets covered with water from the splashed shower, the sound becomes muted as it is essentially like covering the speakers with a towel, meaning you will only hear base notes. Not ideal for a shower speaker. This would be much better for a pool speaker where wet hands would be handling it a lot but it wouldnt be splashed all that much.Sound Quality:Meh, its ok. For the price its not bad. Base is a bit heavy and highs are a bit light. If you're gonna use this for a party or something with dance music then it will probably work. Audiophiles: stay faar far away.This speaker works best when used as background music. Almost like a cafe setting, where you want to have some music in the background but not something you can actually listen to.

title: I love this little thing!
body: I was so happy to receive this wonderful piece of gadgetry from Amazon at such a great price. This little sucker impressed me right out of the box. It has great, clear sound and even some nice bass to it! Nothing too fancy, but it'll do in a pinch. It also has an auto-shut off feature, which I need to explore more to be clear on the when timer shuts it off. I believe it's got to be about 10 minutes after going idle. As far as performance, I can only speak of what I hear in my car. I bought it to replace the built-in speakers because they're old and have too much feedback. Once I turn on the OontZ, a first-time passenger would never know that it wasn't coming from the car. In fact, I had a friend riding with me and he thought I had gotten my built-in speakers fixed!But anyway, I've noticed that the further away the speaker is from you, the better the sound. When it's close up, the sound is a little mono; which is to be expected from a portable speaker. I'm no audiophile, but I would say that if you couldn't get your hands on a Bose, this affordable little guy will be your next best bet. This speaker was a steal at $30, considering it retails at $90, so you won't hear too many complaints from me. However, I had to take off a star because of the volume. With the OontZ on max, to get maximum sound on my smartphone, I only need to set it to just two clicks under max (depending on the quality of the mp3). That usually does the trick, but sometimes driving around on the freeway or in bad weather can drown out the sound; even at absolute max on both gadgets. One time, the volume reset itself, which is fine, but I like to put the speaker in the back of my car so that it has better coverage area. I don't want to stop the car somewhere and reach back there just to turn it back up. But other than that, I highly recommend this speaker if you're looking for better sound and don't want to invest $400 to get a new sound system for your car or room.

title: Hard to Beat for the Price
body: This review may change slightly over time, but I'm basing it on my current usage of this product (1-2 weeks at work; few days at home). I work at a paint store with a warehouse attached, and we have an OontZ Angle 3 that we keep back there to listen to while receiving and shipments. If I had to estimate, the warehouse is likely 1250sqft and this little speaker manages to throw its sound from one side to the other. Not perfectly, but enough to hear wherever you stand in the warehouse, so long as the HVAC isn't running. At home in my 700sqft apartment, 50% volume allows you to hear the music in whichever room you're in, and anything higher could be considered too loud even.I haven't tested the Bluetooth range outright, but I can say that my connection has never faltered when carrying my phone between rooms—which is something previous budget speakers couldn't do for me. The battery life seems fairly impressive; we have been able to get two FULL work days of high-volume listening between charges. And the initial pairing process is incredibly simple. I turned on my phone's Bluetooth, turned on the speaker, and within seconds it popped up in my phone's list of discovered devices. No tech savviness required.The biggest downfall of this little speaker (which was to be expected) is the sound quality. Right out of the box, it is obvious the speaker was only 30 bucks. While not tinny like many of its competitors, it's distinctively flat sounding. The saving grace is that a little bit of EQ tweaking can achieve a surprisingly full sound. The highs are bright and clear and the bass is entirely present and doesn't distort, even at higher volumes. If you've got the patience to play around, you'll be delighted to find that this speaker outperforms its $30 price tag.I might not recommend this to devout audiophiles, but for the casual-to-avid listeners you'd be hard-pressed to find a better speaker at this cost.

title: I must say it's a love and dislike feeling I don't hate it at all ...
body: Let me start by saying I purchased this Speaker with my own money and through the many reviews granted by Amazon it helped me on my purchase... Now to the Speaker. I must say it's a love and dislike feeling I don't hate it at all just a view could get better but overall great speaker...the build is pretty solid it's like a silicone soft case on the two ends shape as squares...an the body is like an amazing plasltic or a soft but steardy metal....the points are low key and I must say it took me going on the internet to discover how to charge it...the very flush which is a plus seeing that it's water resistance not water proof... Picture this it's more of a pour you know when it rains slow but not heavy kind of rain and not to dunk in water the underneath seems open wouldn't really take a chance on that....the 10w speakers boom....I test all my speakers on high volume.... It's because you know it's limitations on quality not quantity.... On high volume you get a slight crisp in the Speaker for does who are partying in a small room you would never notice but on the other hand in the same room quiet it would sound a little bit annoying ...but by yourself a nice medium sound makes the Little Giant sound like a Bose....not nearly as good as the bose but not that far away...not like other sized speakers that are bigger but not as good...not to down it really but the venstar Taco which I also bought was less impressive it was about the same price, bigger and straight in terrible... The highs on the venstar sounds like the meds on the oontz....an like I said about the same price....the oontz has little flaws but flaws none the less....I rate it truthfully and at the best of my ability.... It's an amazing Speaker for an amazing price which I bought not gave to review from company's that arebmentioned and no photos are shown because I also don't represent them as an employee to promote neither dictate claims. Thank You!

title: Best Review for the Oontz3 (Just released)9-11-15
body: This review is for the Oontz Angle3. The new speaker from Cambridge SoundWorks.In order of imprtance for me.1. Bass- This speaker has a small subwoofer and 2 speakers built in the case. When at full volume, the bass does not get much louder. So in other words if you want more bass, you will not find it "thumping" harder deeper bass. However, it sounds great. It barely distorts at full volume and the highs and mids sound pretty clean.2. Battery life- Not as good as the other Oontz products but I take it that is because the volume levels reach higher and the speaker is pumping 10watts via chargable battery. I definately would like to see a Oontz4 I would name it (OontzQuad) with double everything-20 watts-14-hr battery. Its a portable speaker so for what you will use it for, patios, pools, showering, it will be more than enough. But who doesnt like specs....3- Style- The design is very functional and you can stand it up or place it on it vertically. It has rubber pads so it will prevent the speaker from moving around on a table or counter.4. Volume- It's loud. Period.5. Portability- It will not fit in a pocket for sure. But it is small to place in a space all to itself and will not intrude or appear like its begging for attention. However the color red is nice and it looks clean. I would not mind displaying this little speaker.6- Bluetooth pairing and distances- it pairs nicely with my Iphone 6plus and I have a 2,000 sq ft home and carried it from one end of the house to the other and was able to change tracks, pause the music, and walk around with out disconnection. Im sure that was more than 30ft.I got it on sale so I can honestly say that I am happy with the purchase and the value to quality ratio. I would of gave 5 stars for a richer deeper sounding bass sound. I will wait for the OontzQuad for that. ;).

title: It Is What It Is For the Price.
body: If you keep the price in mind, then you should not be too disappointed. This is NOT a Bose by any means. But it is pretty good for what it does anyhow. It reminds me of a small to mid-size radio we once would have in the house that we could easily take from room to another or out to the garage when we wanted. It has pretty good bass response, but not exceptionally noteworthy. I am pretty pleased with it, keeping in mind what it actually is and not what I would fantasize it to be. It's sound however, does not compete with the level of my 2012 21" iMac internal speakers, if that helps give a better idea. It's a bit more high-end or tinny in comparison to the iMac.However, it really leaves much to be desired when it comes to that claim of being able to receive blue tooth up to a hundred feet. I've not had that luxury at all. In my apartment this little speaker is never further than twenty to twenty-five feet away with maybe one wall between. Typically, at twenty feet, it drops out completely if I should happen to get up from my chair and move passed it. And if not connected to its plug, it will drop out and in enough to drive me bat crap crazy.Another oddity is that should you restart your computer, or disconnect from blue tooth at some point or wake up your computer and then try to reconnect to it, your results will be mixed. I have found that when trying to do this, the speaker will indeed connect but it will drop its signal in and out. When that happens, I have to disconnect it from the computers signal and reconnect it to get it to play normally.Outside of that, for a very inexpensive and very unintrusive little speaker I am very happy with it. I can place it on an antique table under the foliage of a vase of plants and it is mostly disappearing from view. Try doing that with a computer or a boom box! BTW, does anyone even still use boom boxes?

title: Big sound in a little package
body: I've been in need of a Bluetooth speaker to give me a little more oomph than the speakers on my phone and laptop can provide, and this fits the bill. It's not going to match the quality of some of the larger, $150 speakers, but it's a lot better speaker than I expected for under $30. It's very compact, just slightly larger than a soda can. The controls are easily distinguishable, with large, raised markings, making it easy to control volume and pause and play audio without having to look at them. However, the power button is on the outside edge of the speaker, which could lead to it inadvertently turning on in a bag or backpack. The play/pause button is nice, although I do wish there were a way to skip to the next track like I can do with other Bluetooth devices. It does come with an audio cable for connecting to a non-Bluetooth audio source, and a micro USB cable for charging. It does not include a charging adapter, but I have enough of those around the house, it's not a problem.Some people have said that they don't hear any audio distortion, even at full volume. I have had it turned up all the way, and to my ears, there is some distortion, even if it's just vibration of the case itself. At any rate, I'm never going to be listening to it that loud. It's plenty loud enough to use in the kitchen while cooking dinner, in the bathroom while showering or getting ready, or in a classroom while giving a presentation.Pros: Compact size, great audio quality, long battery life. Water resistant housing makes it great for use indoors or our, in the kitchen, or even in the shower. Built in mic lets this double as a speakerphone.Cons: There is no indicator of battery remaining. Other devices will have some kind of LED indicator, or audio cue. Audio controls include volume and play/pause, but no way to skip to the next track.

title: Still a Great Speaker, But the Water Resistance Feature is Questionable
body: I ordered the OontZ Angle 2-3 years ago and have had absolutely no regrets. When I saw that an Oontz Angle 3 was available I reviewed the improved features and ordered one right away, even though my original is still working perfectly. The 2 new features that attracted my attention were: 1. More Power, and 2. Water resistant. While the original has considerable power, I still regularly run into the annoying Android warning on my phone about too high a volume being possibly bad for you.[personal rant]I have had this phone for months. I use the Oontz in the car. What is more dangerous that the volume that I am choosing, is me regularly having to turn on my phone and key in the code, to give myself permission to turn up the sound louder! After maybe 40 times on a personal item such as a phone, that annoying message should disengage. [rant over]I figured the added volume of this new Oontz Angle 3 would be a benefit. To date I can't say I really notice a difference but I haven't really experimented.The other improvement was the water resistance. The design has changed a little, making it less sharply angled, and the material is more rubbery, which gives it a more rugged look and feel. Personally, I don't like the look as much, but this is minor. What I have found to be a design flaw however, is the rubber cap that goes over 2 plug-in ports. It is a rubber part of the housing that you peel back to plug in a connection. After doing this just once, that cap no longer stays in place. sealing the plug-in ports. I doubt that this at all water resistant after that first day.So the improved volume and water-resistant features are limited at best - definitely a disappointment, but I still have another very good car speaker to listen to my Audible books without being drowned out by road noise.

title: OK Speaker for the Money
body: Have only had this speaker one day so I don't have a lot of run time with it. Here's what I've discovered. I've tried it with my MacBook Air, iPad, iPhone and HP Spectre. The Bluetooth function pairs quickly with all of my devices. I also have a Bose SoundLink and the OontZ connects even quicker. The problem I have is when using it with my MacBook connected to an external monitor. Then the Bluetooth not only sounds awful with a lot of static, it also interferes with the signal and cuts out the video. However, if I use the 3.5mm audio cable, it works just fine. The reason I bought this is that MacBooks cut out the sound when using an external monitor. So you need a speaker like this. I'm OK with the cable, just would have preferred using the Bluetooth.It appears to be well constructed. Haven't run down the battery yet to see how long a charge will last. It's very easy to set up. I like it's small size - doesn't take up much space on my desk. The sound quality is very good for a small speaker - not as good as the Bose, but certainly adequate. And for the price, it's a pretty good deal.I'll update my review after I get more experience with the speaker.Update March 20, 2019I've now been using this speaker for about 3 weeks and have seen no significant changes. It works well in Bluetooth mode with all of my devices except my MacBook Air where it has a lot of static. It does work just fine with the MacBook Air when using the 3.5 mm cable. I've tried it with my wife's MacBook air and my daughter's MacBook air and get the same static in Bluetooth. So, it's not just my computer. Other than that, it's a good value for the money. Seems to be holding up well. After three weeks of constant use, I still have not had to recharge it. So, battery life is great.

title: Read the Review
body: Context: not quite a senior citizen, but close; love tech including small bluetooth speakers: first speaker purchase 46 years ago doubled as end-tables; in the last 2+ years I have purchased 26 small bluetooth speakers for myself and (mostly as) gifts ... (when in doubt why not a mini speaker?); in the last 5 years I have purchased Anker/Soundcore, EWA, JBL, Doss, Altec-Lansing, Tewell, Mifa and Vtin in order of multiple purchases based on satisfaction; always looking for smallest size with best build quality, sound and function at the sale price;That said I purchased the OontZ Angle 3 (3rd Gen) October 2020 on a lightning sale due to being very satisfied and very impressed with the sound of the OontZ Angle Solo (purchased March 2020 on a lightning sale.) Surprisingly, and backed up by my wife as a second opinion, side my side compare of Angle 3 vs Angle Solo the Angle 3 did not impress and was actually disappointing. The Angle Solo with one speaker and 5 watts somehow (?) sounded much fuller and richer and less 'shrill' than the Angle 3 with 2 speakers and 10 watts. I cannot guess how that could be the case and we were surprised but bottom line there are better speakers give or take a few dollars ... and this is our take on a replacement Angle 3 (the first one had random 'pops' when songs changed and was very out of sync with (android) tablet and phone and (windows) pc. The replacement does not appear to have the 'pop' problem.The Angle 3 is a good speaker, but disappoints vs. the cheaper, less watts Angle Solo.And just as an aside, the now discontinued metal 2018 Anker Nano has been eclipsed (IMHO) as best of the smallest speakers by the EWA A106 .. this tiny metal speaker is a freak and a crazy value at the $10 frequent sale price (best stocking stuffer ever!)

title: Great for the price with plenty of versitility.
body: I bought this speaker because I was looking for a relatively inexpensive speaker that I can use in the shower. I was looking at a few speakers but eventually decided on the OobtZ because of its features, price and sheer amount of positive feedback. In this review I will focus on the pros and cons that make this speaker a good choice for a speaker under $30.Starting off the shipping was fast as expected. I opened the box and got a fairly simple unvoxing with a charger, manual and 3.5 chord which I was very happy about because many speakers don't come with one. It came with a mostly full battery which was nice. Initial thoughts on sound were as follows: It has a decent bass for the price and has mids and highs that were clear but a bit crunchy (especially at high volumes). It gets loud enough for a small rom and most certainly a shower or bathroom. Aesthetically it is very slick with a metal grill and rubber endcaps. The size also lends to excellent portability. The controls are easy to use and pairing was very simple. So to sum it all up I will list my personal pros and cons when using the speaker.ProsWater resistance for shower and water useGood portable sizeGood overall soundEasy controls and pairingConsLacks deep bass but this is to be expected with a speaker of this priceCrunchy highs but not so bad that it is hard to listen toNot fully waterproof so don't fully submerge itSo in conclusion this is a very well rounded speaker that is good in all categories but not necessarily amazing in sound which is expected for a $30 speaker. I would say this speaker is great for a shower speaker or something you throw in the bag to a pool, beach or for just casual listening if you don't need top notch sound.

title: The Ins and Outs On Another Bluetooth Speaker.
body: The Pros: - Paired immediately with my Samsung phone...and stayed paired. that means if it's on and bluetooth is on on my phone, it does it's job without anymore input from me. That's a big step over previous bluetooth speakers I've had with this phone.- It's small and it's light..smaller and lighter than you think it's going to be (if you've had other speakers).- It powers up quickly and the controls are large and easily located.The Cons: - Although billed as "more bass", you'll be asking yourself, "more bass than what?". It certainly has less bass than other bluetooth speakers that I have, but more than my phone I guess, so I'll only cut a half star for that.- "Louder volume". Again..than what? Louder than my phone..ok. Not as loud as other (bigger and heavier) bluetooth speakers AND it distorts if you push your phone volume to the top with its volume all the way up. It almost disappears when I turn on my pool slides water, so keep that in mind for volume. Others don't do that. Minus half star.Jury Still Out: - It came with a charge on it and I've run it about 4 hours so far on it without a charge cycle. We'll see how the battery and charging cycles hold up.- This is in use poolside and hasn't been wet yet. We'll see how that part stands up. (other BT speakers have had their batteries corrode in this situation, even though supposedly "waterproof" and not submerged)- I have not used it with voice from the phone yet, just streaming music, so no ideas on how that sounds quality is.Overall, for the price, it's still a winner. I'm most impressed by its bluetooth pairing ability with android devices (also a pair of kindles). Well done to those that worked on that part of this device!

title: Great sound; needs better controls.
body: I was looking for a replacement for the [[ASIN:B00RBIC1IS [Best Outdoor&Shower Bluetooth Speaker Ever] Omaker M4 Portable Bluetooth 4.0 Speaker with 12 Hour Playtime for Outdoors/Shower (Army Green)]], which is a bit too underpowered. (In fact, I'd blown the speaker on that unit.) What I needed was more oomph along with water resistance, and the OontZ Angle 3 seemed to fit the bill.THE GOOD:• The sound from this unit is excellent, considering its size. The stereo speakers put out clear, crisp sound with enough volume to fill a small room, and there's even some bass in there to fill out the audio.• The Oontz is water resistant (great for listening to podcasts in the shower).• It charges using a standard micro USB cable, and will play seemingly forever. (I've run it as long as eight hours.)THE NOT-SO-GOOD:• The main controls are on the right side of the unit under a rubber cover (which is just fine), but the top button is for Bluetooth pairing while the power button is on the edge in a less-convenient spot; considering that powering on and off the device is something you'll do much more often than pairing it, it seems to me that these buttons should be switched.• The playback controls are limited to play/pause and volume up/down. On the Omaker the volume buttons doubled for track forward/back (short press for track; long press for volume); I wish the OontZ would do the same so I could skip or rewind tracks when I don't have immediate access to my phone.• Perhaps this is a personal preference, but I find putting the volume-down button in front and the volume-up button in the rear to be counter intuitive.If you can overlook the unfortunate control design, this is a very good unit for your portable audio needs.

title: Not quite as big as you might think
body: Yeah, I expected a device which I presumed would be about 7 or 8 inches across.... is 5.3 inches across (from the manual). I hadn't looked for dimensions before I placed the order, so be advised. So, from the small size, I don't get much stereo separation.I've powered it on and paired it to Ubuntu 18.04 64 bit, this took about 15 seconds start to finish using Blueman and choosing this as an Audio Sink. Pairing was very quick and easy, the first pairing was the only attempt because it was successful.Listening to DJ Basilisk - Circadian Rhythms and Heliocentric. So, how is the bass response? Low frequency roll-off is substantial, despite designer efforts for good bass (bottom surface is a PASSIVE bass radiator... openings are over half of the bottom surface area). Very strong high frequency output. This device will need substantial EQ work. Total peak sound level is high, but at that level, the distortion is also high.Can't speak to battery life, I'm testing it using the charge which it had from the factory. It is labeled for 5V 1 Amp, comes with a USB to Micro cord of about 18 inches. This Oontz does not come with a charger. Manual claims 6 hours to fully charge and 12 hours play time at 2/3rds volume.Water resistance is IPX5, which the manual says is splash resistance only, no puddles, do not submerge.Manual does not describe any other uses than as a stereo speaker."Crystal Clear Stereo Sound". Uhhh, no. Very little channel separation, left driver is 3/8" from the right driver, my ears are spaced further apart than that. Each ear hears both left AND right.Is it worth the money? Yeah, I think it is, but my only other BT speakers are about the same SOUND quality and 2/3rds the price.

title: You’ll be Hearing Sounds from Every Angle
body: I originally purchased the OontZ Angle 3 because I wanted a good quality portable Bluetooth speaker, but I didn’t want to break the bank. When compared to name brand Bluetooth speakers such as Bose and Beats by Dr. Dre, this is a steal. This is an ideal Bluetooth speaker for anyone who is budget conscious, but wants good sound quality, a cool design, and long battery-life.Impressive Features:• The sound quality is very clear and not muddled. Even while taking a shower at max pressure you will still be able jam to your music since it produces a pretty loud sound for such a small device. And even if your AC is blaring in the background, that won’t interfere with your tunes.• The shape of the design may seem a bit odd at first, but you’ll soon find that it can fit in narrow spaces securely. For example, it fits perfectly in your towel rack which can give you easy access to the controls if needed while in the shower. Even though you won’t be able to skip through songs in that situation because it has no controls for that; you will still be able to blast the volume as loud as you want.• After charging for a couple hours, you will notice that you won’t need to do that again for a several days. Since this device automatically shuts down when no longer in use, you’ll be saving on power and time.• This device also comes with an aux cable, which allows you to connect to anything that has an aux cable plug in; such as your TV, laptop, and cell phone.• Also, you can even take calls on it. So whether you are in the shower or your phone is in another room; you will always have your calls at your fingertips.Overall, this is a great buy for anyone on a budget and on the go.

title: Great speaker and I do recommend it
body: Great speaker and I do recommend it. I own a couple of portable speakers but none being "splash proof". I've really gotten into kayak fishing and wanted one I could take with me that had some kind of water resistance. I bought this speaker knowing is wasn't a waterproof speaker and from the design it's clear it wouldn't survive water submersion. I've only had this speaker a few days and I will say the darn thing is loud! It is much louder than my other two speakers by a noticeable degree. The bass on this little thing is amazing and at full blast, it will literally vibrate itself off a shelf if you aren't careful. I listen to a lot of hip hop music and I love the bass so this is a plus for me. Comparing the speaker to my HMDX Jam speaker which has been my favorite until now, the Oontz appears to lack treble at the lower volume. The music isn't distorted but it seems deeper and not as crisp as the JAM. However on the highest volum setting this seems to not really impact the sound so much as even songs with the highest bass are still clearly heard with no kind of distortion. My conclusion is that this is likely because of the splash proof design. This is the only reason I didn't give it a full 5 stars. I charged the speaker upon arrival and have left it on the shelf turned off when not in use, only having used it for about 30 mins in total since full charge. I haven't had to recharge yet and the battery hasn't died, so I can't really attest to the battery life. I also haven't had the chance to test the splash proof-ness of the speaker as this weather hasn't allowed me to go out kayak fishing. I doubt there will be any issues in this front considering the design. You won't be disappointed with the speaker.

title: Impressive sound for such a small unit
body: This unit sounded great right out of the box but I started noticing a little static and thought I got a defect. I checked the owner's manual and found you have to go in through the headphone input jack with the paper clip and push a little reset button. I did that and the static was gone from the Bluetooth signal and has been Flawless.Oh and just a tip, if you leave it plugged into USB power it will constantly wait for a Bluetooth signal so you don't have to reconnect every time if it's going to remain in one place.📣Update January 2021📣The only flaw I have found is the little rubber cover that was a hassle to seal has finally come off, other than that, the speaker performs great.The speaker does need an EQ on your phone or device to get the full bass capacity out of it from you on board audio files. I'm really surprised at how many phones have such flat response but the usual reason for that is to prevent overdriving earbuds \ headphones that are not dynamically able .I have been driving this speaker with a LG v20, it has a quality digital audio section and works great with an add-on player/ EQ. I use Neutron music player tofor my on board audio files it's $6 on the Play store and well worth it.i tune the low end response (60 and 100 Hertz )parametric eq to my taste and the speaker handles it well.It won't vibrate the pictures off the wall but for what it is it sounds goodHere's the thing about using a streaming service. The audio bandwidth is mp3 quality and compressed quite a bit so by the time it gets from the streaming service to your phone it's quite squashed, if you can't tell it's not a problem.

title: Better than I expected, for not much money
body: My original review of this item wasn't really as positive as it should have been. In essence, this rather diminutive device puts out surprisingly good sound, at a surprisingly high volume. Further, it seems to hold a charge well enough that I can pull it out on a whim, after quite a few months, and plug it in for a few minutes of insurance time, then go to the tub for a few hours to listen to podcasts while I soak. I don't have to worry about dripping all over it when I reach to change the volume, etc., because the buttons are entirely encased in rubber. Part of the reason why I like using this in the tub is that I don't have to worry about damaging it, if it comes to that, because it's inexpensive—yet, that has not been an issue.There's a very secure rubber cap over the inputs, so I don't have to worry about those, either. It has rubber pads on the bottom so I can lay it on the edge of the tub without worrying about it sliding in, when that surface is dry. Once more, I didn't expect much out of this, but I went ahead and got a case for it so I can pack it for travel, if I choose. (I have a more expensive, less water-resistant speaker that I may choose to take with me—it's a real boomer.)My only complaints about it are really quite small. The protective flap that covers the ports is quite difficult to open when tightly sealed. I think that's about it. The speaker is always on high when using Skype, and probably other services, regardless of the phone's volume or pressing the volume buttons (which causes the phone to indicate the volume is being changed. I don't tend to use Skype in the tub, so that's not an issue for me. It did, however, prevent me from giving it five stars.

title: Small but sturdy and good
body: I've used this to listen to music and podcasts in the bathroom for two years. I have also stuffed it into my suitcase for trips when I don't have any other means of listening to tunes. It's mostly good, and has held up well despite being dropped in the shower many times. The only noticeable effect of this is the front grille is loose fitting.The Bluetooth no longer automatically connects. I have to pair it every time I use it. I once had trouble connecting it to a previous phone, and powered it on holding down the pair button. Perhaps this put it into a permanent "pair every time" mode.I appreciate that it has an 1/8" mini jack, so I can connect it to an old iPod and have music just about anywhere. I'll also connect my phone that way if I don't want to take the time to pair Bluetooth. Yes some of us still have headphone jacks on our phones (and use them)!Also it is far smaller than I initially thought. I bought it hastily to play background music for a large gathering, thinking it would be 8 or 10" long based on similar Jabra(?) units I had seen and heard in person. In reality it's about 4" long. That's my fault for not reading the description thoroughly.As far as sound, it is clear and plays plenty loud enough for my uses. There is some bass, particularly if you put it on a surface like a wood desk. That helps the bass through coupling (vibrations transmitted). You can also put it against a wall or into a corner and rely on bass buildup. In any case the mid and high frequencies are well represented.Overall it's a good little unit. That mine has held up after dropping it in water many times says something to me about build quality.

title: Sound Quality Definitely Not The Best
body: First, this is the first "external" speaker I've had in many, many years, but these are my thoughts, after having it for over a month.I've never had it at max volume, and I've always been able to hear it, even with hearing problems. It's good to finally have volume, because my Macbook Pro CERTAINLY DOES NOT. And as good as the built-in iPhone speaker is, it just doesn't cut it if I want to hear it at a distance, or over anything noisy, such as a shower.There are a couple of things I don't like, and anyone purchasing should be aware of.Mainly, the sound is fairly "bass-y," and not in a good way; and to my knowledge, there's nothing I can do to change that. I use it with my iPhone and my Macbook. So, when I say "bass-y," I don't mean that the bass is clear. It's like if you turn the bass all the way up, and the treble down a lot in a normal factory-installed car radio. That's not quality sound. I don't know if I would have noticed this, if not for switching directly from iPhone speakers to this speaker instantaneously during a song. The sheer quality pales in comparison. I want volume, in this case. If you are looking for QUALITY SOUND, this is not the speaker to get. It is ok for what I wanted, but next time, I'll buy one that hopefully has better quality AND volume.The other things are much more minor in my opinion.The volume controls on the speaker seem to be a bit wiggy. It's as if sometimes I must press a button more than once for it to respond.Another thing is the speaker is designed to sit going longways, but controls are on the side. I stand it up on it's end, for easy access to the controls.

title: Great little speaker and can get very loud! Works Perfectly with Echo dot!
body: [[VIDEOID:2bb913cee8875a2514eddb1910bd95f1]] This was a great purchase for the price. The ONLY reason I dinged a star is because the charging cable it comes with is usb only and also EXTREMELY short so if you want to plug it into a wall outlet for continuous use you need to get a usb wall adapter for it which thankfully I had spare of and also a longer usb charging cable as well. Also the 3.5 aux cable it came with is terrible quality and had very bad feedback when plugged in so I threw that out and got a longer higher quality one of those too. These things are important to me because I am using this speaker as a pairing for the echo dot that I use in my kitchen. The great news is after doing those things the speaker itself is pretty amazing and had much higher sound quality than it appears it can push out and it also can get really loud. Enough so that it can easily fill a room at max volume if you so desire. Bottom line is the speaker itself is great quality and built extremely well. The peripherals that it came with however are extremely cheap quality and you should just replace them if you need to use them. I would recommend this purchase however because the price is right and the problems are easily fixable and because i'm using this with my echo the fact that it always stays powered on when plugged in is a MUST HAVE feature for this as you need your speaker to always be on for the echo and not have a forced auto shut off like most other bluetooth speakers do even when plugged in. This one however will always stay on as long as it is plugged into a power source making it invaluable for things like this. So yea great purchase would buy again if need be.

title: Best Cheap Speaker
body: I did a lot of research before choosing this speaker. I was impressed by the large number of 5 star buyer ratings. The price was excellent as well. Speaker came packaged perfectly. Included was a 3 foot Micro-USB charging cable and 3 foot 3.5mm audio cable. Speaker came fully charged. Speaker pairs easily and quickly, but I did experience problems getting it to disconnect from one device to pair to a different source. I contacted the company and they were quick to respond back to me. Their customer service was excellent, fast, relevant and courteous. They even offered to call me to assure that the device was working properly or not. After working with it a few days the device started working as expected and has not had an issue ever since. Maybe it was user error? Not sure. I also ordered the Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 PLUS. The Angle 3 PLUS offers 30 hours of playtime vs the Angle 3 which only offers 7 hours (a big difference). The Angle 3 PLUS is a tad taller at 6 1/2". The Angle 3 is 5 1/4", see attached photos. I didn't notice a sound quality difference but my musician son did. He ordered the 3 PLUS and says it is a better speaker than his Jawbone Bluetooth Speaker for less than half the cost. I use the smaller speaker, which sets on a high shelf in my shower, as it is water resistant & splash proof. The other sets on my office desk. If you are considering purchasing a Bluetooth speaker definitely consider the OontZ. I would go for the Angle 3 PLUS as the price difference is only about $7 unless a smaller speaker is your goal. I have referred several people to this powerful little speaker and all are very happy with their purchase.

title: Got Much more than I Paid For
body: Overall Impressions: I was a little worried when I opened the package and saw in person the size of this product. This is the smallest speaker set, much less Bluetooth speaker, I've ever purchased. After I made sure the unit was charged and paired with my iPhone my concerns were gone. This is a highly portable set of speakers with excellent performance for the origina Tl price much less the sale.Unboxing: As I said before, I was at first concerned about the size. When I removed the speakers from their box I was pleasantly surprised to find the housing is a decent quality metal and not the usual cheap plastic. The charging cable is very short so you'll need to provide your own microSD cable if you want to wall charge. The buttons for using this device are all intuitive without being obtrusive. All in all this product is very well designed with clean lines and a low profile that does not scream "I'm using electronics in the kitchen" or wherever you use it.Pairing: Very easy. There is no code or anything else. As soon as I set the speakers to discover mode they showed up instantly on my phone and I could pair them in under ten seconds. No muss. No fuss.Sound: This one is tricky. Because the sound is so good I ended up expecting better treble and bass response. That is going to be impossible because of the small size. That being said the single mid-range speakers give off an excellent sound with no noticeable vibration or "fuzz" like you'll get with a cheaper unit. The sound is clear even at higher volume levels and much more than adequate for a portable room experience.

title: Compact and powerful speaker
body: My trusty little CA speaker (non Bluetooth) had just died after 11 years of faithful service, so I was on the hunt for a replacement. I searched Amazon extensively and decided on the OontZ Angle 3 (3rd Gen) because of the price and reviews. Had I continued searching within the OontZ line of products, I would have purchased the slightly more expensive (and a bit larger) model with the ability to switch tracks from the speaker and the extended battery life. But, I bought the OontZ Angle 3. My first impression when it arrived was that it was way smaller than expected, but that's a good thing. I use the unit with a 3mm headphone jack cable attached to my iPod, so I'm already way lower tech than the folks who will use this as a Bluetooth speaker. I use this speaker on my motorcycle rides - not on the bike itself, but in the tent or motel when I land. The sound is crisp and clear. The bass could be a little deeper, but overall it sounds great and gets loud. My minor complaint(s) with the unit are as follows: 1) it's all black (by the way this comes in several colors, which I didn't realize when I ordered) Because it's all black, the controls are hard to see 2) the little rubbery flap that lifts up (near the on/off button) which contains the charging port and headphone jack - looks like it will tear off if not handled VERY carefully. I play mine standing on end in order to get the most bass quality, otherwise the bass speaker is facing downward and the sound is somewhat muffled. If I had it to do over, I would buy the larger OontZ Angle and buy it in a color other than black. Otherwise, I love the speaker.

title: Excellent speaker for the price!
body: I rarely write reviews - if ever. So the fact that I'm writing one for a regular ol' speaker like this speaks volumes (pun intended).Sound quality: It is very clear, able to reproduce voices with the upmost clarity. Highs and mids are very pronounced, without being too overpowering. Bass is... somewhat disappointing. Don't let the "Rich Bass", or "passive bass radiator" fool you. While there is enough to notice the low ends, this speaker cannot produce the thump or thundering lows that some people might be used to. If that is important to you, don't get this speaker. Otherwise, if you prefer more attention in the highs and mids, this speaker will please you. Overall, this speaker delivers decent, fairly balanced sound.Battery life: Lasts about 10 hours for me. That's great in my book.Connectivity/reliability: I haven't had any drop-outs, stutters, or other connection issues. This was used between my laptop, phone, and tablet. If you have the speaker within line of sight, you shouldn't have any problems.Water resistance: It's good for bringing to the bathroom when you shower, and can survive a splash of water. But that's it. USE NEAR WATER AT YOUR OWN RISK.TL;DR:Pros:-Excellent clarity in mids and highs.-Great battery life (~10 hours)-Solid Bluetooth connection-Great value for your money.Cons:-Doesn't get loud (enough) to be used outdoors - especially if there is already loud ambient noise.-Doesn't produce "rich bass" as described.-Charges via MicroUSB (at this price though, that's to be expected.).

title: My Ears Approve
body: Good sound from this little speaker.. I use it for listening to music as I take care of work and chores, and when I do my yoga practice. The sound quality is surprisingly good. Doesn't sound tinny and voices sound clear to me on this. I should note I'm a person with a hearing loss and must wear BTE hearing aids, so it's hard for me to find a speaker that actually sounds good to my disabled ears. I was wary about getting a new speaker, but this fit the bill of what I wanted. It's small, has good sounds for its size and isn't bothersome to my ears. So far I've only used it for listening to CD's and as a speaker for my netbook. I use bluetooth for the netbook and setting it up was a cinch.
