Again confusing but once you know you know.It paired super easy to my phone first then later to my laptop. I played a playlist from my phone and was shocked how clean and boomy the sound was. powerful sound from such a small speaker. Very clean and loud. It has a 15 minute idle auto shutoff when on battery. I misunderstood this at first to mean it shut off at 15 minutes no matter what but learned is only if no sound is transmitted so that is a good feature. It is water resistant but not water proof. The bottom is a series of large port holes that prevent it being a totally sealed device.I do plan to buy one of the cases they make for it just for added protection when taking it places. Once you hear this anytime you want a clean quick speaker you will go for this. It is rugged yet quite light weight. The rubber feet keep it well in place on a table so you do not get any walking effects like I hear some do from speaker vibrations at louder volumes.I would advise a quick read through the manual simply to understand the minor differences in how this speaker indicates and pairs compared to other devices. Not complicated just slightly different but quick and easy in spite of it.It does also work as a speakerphone when paired to your phone. I received a call while listening to music and the ring through the speaker was fairly loud but I was unable to turn the ringer down either from the phone or the speaker. Could be simply my inexperience with the speaker but feel it should be noted. I did not answer the call nor do I plan to use the speakerphone function myself. You can also use this speaker on devices without bluetooth via the included aux cable. One end into the aux in on the speaker and other end into earphone out or speaker out on device. Good to have for older electronics or devices without bluetooth ability.Thus far I am extremely pleased and impressed and plan to use it often. I will update if I encounter any major issues.

title: Great little speaker, improves on the Oontz Angle Plus
body: I recently purchased the OontZ Angle 3 from Amazon as a lightning deal in December for $19.99, which was an amazing deal. I also own the OontZ Angle Plus and I have had it for over a year now and paid over twice that price back then. I really love the Plus and use it daily, so I was very excited to get the Angle 3.The Angle 3 is noticeably smaller and lighter than the Plus. The rubberized ends feel very well constructed, and the buttons are very responsive without feeling cheap. There is a rubber flap that conceals the aux input and the charging port. The speaker grill is made of metal and covers two of the 3 sides of the speaker. The "bottom" of this speaker is slotted and contains the passive woofer.Powering up and connecting to the Angle 3 is very simple. There are no annoying beeps or voices that come at you at full blast, just a simple blinking light that lights steady to show that you are connected. It will also flash when you hit maximum volume. Very simple...wish other speakers would do this.The Angle 3 really does pack a punch for its size. It really does get much louder than the Plus, and sounds just as clear (which is very clear). In fact, these Cambridge speakers produce some of the cleanest sound of any Bluetooth speaker I have tried. It sounds as if they have separate tweeters built in (which they don't). The bass response is also very decent for its size. Placement in a corner will enhance the bass response. I have also found that when I put the Angle 3 on a towel or a cloth surface, with the woofer face down, it provides a little more bass. When really cranking the volume, the Angle 3 doesn't break up too much or get distorted. The only thing I really notice is that the bass is less powerful when the volume is really high. This is probably done intentionally in order to achieve the volume levels that this thing does and not sound muddy. If you have tone controls on your source, you can bump up the bass to compensate for this up to a point. That said, the Angle 3 does not produce more bass than the Plus, but it will play louder for sure.The battery life is approx 8 hours at moderate volume levels using Bluetooth. I have found this to be conservative, and I have gotten closer to 9 or 10 with mine. Charging time is roughly 2-3 hours for mine. You can also keep it plugged in and listen to it non-stop if you want, and it will charge while you do so.Overall, I am very impressed with the Angle 3. I love the build quality, the very compact size, and the volume levels that this thing is capable of, especially when compared to the larger Plus. It is also splash and rain resistant. It is a great deal at the current Amazon selling price, and an absolute steal when it was a lightning deal. I would highly recommend this speaker.

title: Best speaker ever for such a small price!!
body: I wanted a speaker that could hold deep bass tones, but also play rock n' roll. I wanted a speaker that could play loud enough to hear regardless of surrounding noise such as at the beach. I wanted a speaker that did not distort when played louder. This speaker accomplishes all these things! I first bought a Braven after hearing it's sample song at Target...it played loud and bass-y (sp?), but once I received this speaker I was very dissapointed. It distorted the bass tones to a "dink, dink" sound rather then keeping with those nice deep tones. It also played loud but only in one direction. This was not for me! On to a search again. At this point I began to be obsessed with researching reviews. I had recently heard someone playing music in line at an amusement park, that played loud and bass-y, theirs was awesome...UE and was small and black. Now don't confuse the UE mini boom, that was my first "rush to the store speaker purchase". This was not the one. The UE miniboom is only okay. To this day I am not sure which one that guy in line had. Anyways, After more reviews, I purchased the JBL Flip 3 Waterproof. This looks pretty! But again, it was not what I was looking for. The bass was better then the Braven, but still didn't hold those deep tones that I was looking for. It played loud but not as loud as I felt like it could for paying $150. I did like that it was waterproof, although I have no plans to put my speaker in or near water. Considering price, and knowing I wasn't getting what was important to me out of a speaker, I returned it. Then the research that kills your brain began! OBSESSED. Everything that sounded like I would get what I wanted was in the $299 price range. I do not have this kind of budget. I wanted to spend no more then $150. As I was researching reviews, I happened to stumble upon the Cambridge SoundWorks Oontz Angle 3. I saw that it had over 8,000 reviews and maintained a 4.5 star. This intrigued me. I mean, it only cost $28, maybe reviewers weren't looking for what I was. So I began reading through review after review. I was beginning to be convinced...especially at a low cost risk. I decided to purchase. And to my surprise, I freaking love this speaker! It has by far been the best speaker not only that I have purchased, but have listened to samples at all the local stores. It plays loud! It plays clear! It maintains those deep bass tones! It may not by earth shaking, but it does not distort the sound! I love it!! I took it outside yesterday (btw, it is small, light, and has a nice grip on underside...case is nice too) my friend synced with her phone (syncs quickly too) and she chose which ever music she wanted to play, and all of our friends were impressed with the sound quality at such an affordable price! Forget those overpriced speakers...this one works for me! Search is over :)

title: Very solid speaker for a very solid price!
body: I got this as a replacement to the original Oontz, and I'm pleased in every aspect so far.I can say I'm a very heavy user of these speakers, and I don't go easy on them. That should stand testament to how solid these are. I use this every day at work at a machine shop, where just the ambient noise is enough to require full volume. I also use it around the house, albeit with lower volumes.The fidelity is great, perhaps more so than the previous model of Oontz I owned. As always, it holds really well at max volume, and doesn't really distort from what I can tell.The volume on this thing is actually above expectations. When it is fully charged, it provides a huge kick for such a little package. I haven't fully drained the battery yet (and this is after 8 hours of use without charging), but when the bit of 'oomph' switches off, I know it's getting low.Usually these things have a bit of trouble reproducing bass simply because of the size. This model does a good job by using what looks like a passive radiator to translate bass, if I'm not mistaken. Somewhat of a mini subwoofer if you will. When I have it at significant volume, I can feel the air being pushed.The battery life is really good if you can remember to charge it prior to use. As a consequence of having a long life, it also has a somewhat long charge time from low to full. It shows on the speaker that it requires 5v1mAh to charge, which would lead me to believe that a charger such as the current standard Samsung 5v2mAh might be limited. I'm not entirely sure about this though.My only gripe is that there are no skip forward or backward buttons on the interface. I'm constantly moving through music and podcasts, and now I have to physically use my phone to do so, whereas the interface volume buttons see little use beyond accidently getting pressed when I want to press pause. I think a lot could be accomplished by staggering the button layout rather than having the 3 buttons in a straight line. I personally don't look at the speaker when I need to press a button because I don't want to waste time. I understand that because of the smaller profile on the angle models, room is a bit more limited than a larger model, but it does look like there is a little bit of room to move things around at least.The Bluetooth connectivity is nothing to be amazed at, and I have had some issues with crackling in the signal, usually fixed by restarting the connection. This might be due to interference from other devices. It's not a big annoyance, but it's something to note.I haven't dropped this one, though it feels sturdy. The original Oontz actually survived multiple drops to a hard ground and still plays to this day no issues. I'm hoping I can expect the same from the angle 3 if it happens.

title: Great speaker for the price. Mids and highs sound great, bass is okay for a compact unit.
body: For what it is, this speaker performs remarkably well. At 10 watts, I think it's perfect for an office or small, enclosed environment. I purchased several of these as gifts and one was a Christmas present for my daughter, who likes to listen to music she has on her iPhone. At 14, it's popular music and an occasional classic by the Red Hot Chili Peppers--whatever her older brother might be liking at the time. I have experience with three other Bluetooth speakers: Jam Classic, Anker Premium, Bose SoundLink Mini. Of the speakers I've heard, the SoundLink Mini has the best balance of clarity and bass, but at 6x the cost. My next choice is the OontZ Angle 3.I ordered speakers in black, red, and one with the pink grill for my daughter, and they came exactly as shown in the photos. I tested it out with a Samsung Galaxy Note 4, a Samsung Galaxy Tab Pro 12.2, a 15" MacBook Pro, and an iPhone 5c. Thus far, two months later, no connection issues with any of those devices. My daughter seems delighted and I can hear the music from her bedroom and bathroom with the doors closed, so it's plenty loud.I think the speaker reproduces mids and highs with surprising crispness and clarity. Bass-wise, it's just okay. Remember that this is an inexpensive compact speaker system. For grins, I streamed Pink Floyd's "Dark Side of the Moon", one of the great classic rock albums of all time, and an album with lots of bass. I barely got any of the thumping lows from it. Again, given the unit's size, nobody should expect bellowing, thumping bass.I like the controls at the end of the speaker--they are easy to find and click when pressed. There is a blue light just above the Bluetooth button, and if you plug the speaker in to a USB power source, a red light appears just above the power button on the back side. It's not as easy to see on my unit, but that's minor. To make the unit water resistant, the company built a flap to cover the USB and AUX ports. It was a little tricky to open the flap, but that's because it formed a pretty decent seal over both ports.This does not come with a case, which is kind of disappointing, but I knew that going in to the purchase, and ordered a separate neoprene case from the company so my girl can tote it without damaging it. She was thrilled with it and can now clip it to her athletic bag to take to her practices or on her backpack.This is a great gift for a teen or for providing general ambience? If you are an audiophile who expects every note to be faithfully reproduced with thumping bass, you need to look for a different Bluetooth speaker system.Friends continue to come to our house to visit and when they hear this little thing put out the music, everyone wants one. Highly recommended for its price-performance ratio!

title: You want portable speakers with great sound? Definetely a great buy.
body: Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ AngleI wanted a portable speaker with blue tooth connectivity so that I may play my phone's music where I go.In short, this portable speaker is really portable, good sound, with a fully functional bluetooth and waterproof feature but beware of some minor dust/dirt issues, overloading volume/bass wiggling, as well as the short, a bit unreliable charger and audio jack cord. Below will be the full details.Pros:Portability - You can easily carry it in your hand. It's length is about 3/4 the size of a tv remote. It is lightweight. I can carry my mouse, laptop, charger, a notebook, my pencil case and this all with ease.Sound - Sound is crisp and clear. It's good enough to be used as speakers for your desktop or laptop. There is definitely a strong Bass to this thing.Volume - The device itself has a volume control of it's own giving you further variability to not just your phone or device volume but you can maximize or dial down the overall volume with this speaker's volume control. With an overall maximum volume, this can fill the whole living room.Bluetooth connectivity - There's a slight delay when trying to connect it but nothing that's more than 2.5 seconds. Sometimes I walk out of the room with my phone and then when I walk back in, my phone would automatically reconnect with ease. It plays a tone when the device is connected, and it plays a different sound when the sound disconnect. After about 10 minutes of inactivity, the Bluetooth will automatically shut off.Battery Life - No problem. A full charge is at least 6 hours.Waterproof - I take it with me sometimes in the shower, No issues there.Cons -The power button - The first few days of having this, the power button wasn't an issue for me. Over time, I did find the button to be small and with some difficulty to push.Dust - The material of this really attracts dust and dirt. Specially the soft coverings at the end. No biggie, just brush it off with your hand or use some Clorox wipes (but quickly use a paper towel after to dry).Little speakers - These are little speakers, at a high volume or with very big bass, the speakers will move and shake. The movement isn't big enough that it moves all over the place. There is also an easy fix. Just put it in an upright, standing position and it will not move or shake.Charger - The charger that comes with it is a tiny cord, less than a foot long. Any micro-usb cable works with it, I use my long kindle fire charger and it works fine.Audio jack cord - It's short as well, a little over a foot long. I use it for my laptop and small twists and turning of the cord will suddenly cause there to be no sound so i would have to re-adjust the cord.

title: Affordable speaker that rocks!
body: First, let me start by stating that I bought this in May of 2016. It is now January of 2017. I wanted to review this product only after I had an opportunity to really use the speaker in different settings and scenarios. So let me begin by saying that I am a big fan of the speaker. Upon arrival, I opened my Amazon shipment to find the speaker packaged in a white box with black writing. Very simple look but after using the speaker for a while, I can tell you that Cambridge SoundWorks does not waste money on flashy marketing or packaging. They put all the money into the quality of the speaker.In my honest opinion, the build quality is nice. Picking it up, the speaker is a very solid piece of hardware. The speaker features rubberized ends with buttons that provide great feedback as you use them. After an initial charge, powering on was a breeze. Immediately after coming online, the speaker searches for either a paired device or is discoverable to be added to a new device. I was able to pair it with my cell phone and laptop with no connection issues. Pairing was fairly quick.So my gripe with similar Bluetooth speakers is usually the bass/treble balance. You either get too much of either one but never both. The Angle 3 does a great job of balancing the two with the design of the housing. The bottom of the unit has a chamber that resonates the bass sounds. I feel that because of this feature, the sound was balanced for the speakers used in the unit without sacrificing any mid to high sounds. In regards to volume, this speaker does a good job of being loud without distorting the sound.As far as the battery, I've never had an issue. I work in an environment where I may be at the desk for an hour or two and would have to get up and return for another hour or two. I can go 3 or 4 days without having to charge the speaker. When I do need to charge it, I like that it uses a standard micro-USB connection.My favorite feature of this speaker has got to be the size. I've heard speakers slightly bigger than this one that would not produce the same volume or sound quality. I like that at the end of each day, I can pick it up and put it in a side pocket of my backpack. Durability is also an A+. I've dropped this thing quite a few times and have yet to see or hear any problems.In conclusion, I feel that the Angle 3 is a great speaker for the money and easily competes with the likes of the Altec Lansing Jacket and Bose Soundlink. Seeing how well made this speaker is, I'm very interested in looking at the Angle 3xl for my home. Do yourself a favor: instead of paying $60+ on big name brand speakers that produce little sound, spend less than $30 on this and give it a try. I'm sure you'll be very pleased.

title: Small size BT Speaker with a TOP NOTCH PERFORMANCE!
body: They say looks are deceptive, correct? It's 100% true for this Oontz Angle-3 Bluetooth (BT) Speaker. It's so SMALL in SIZE, but PERFORMS BIG.I would like to give a 10 out of 10 for this speaker (Amazon has only a max of 5-star rating). I bought one for listening to music during my travel (streaming music from my SmartPhone or Tablet in my hotel). Ended up buying 3 of these BT speakers, one for travel, one for home & one for my office. Indeed this is ultra-portable, super performer!!One of my colleagues was wondering if he would be able to use this for a home party in their finished basement (for streaming music from a Tablet or laptop PC). He has all hi-tech speakers upstairs in their family room, but he didn't want to move them downstairs for the party. I told him, "most definitely, let us try a demo", took him to to one of our auditorium type conference room in our office (it's a smaller auditorium with a 100 people capacity).I made him sit way back in the auditorium and I was on the stage, started streaming music, slowly increased the volume. When I reached about 60%, my colleague said "stop... stop... do not increase the volume further, as folks might think that we are partying here" (of course, we tested this after 6:00 PM). I increased it to 80% and my colleague told me: "OK... we are done testing, this is an awesome speaker, now it's time for me to order this one".The beauty is, it creates a "surround sound effect", I would say. When you increase the volume, it is NOT jarring noise (which happens with some BT speakers), but it offers the increased decibel level in a uniform manner; you can sense the bass & treble getting amplified in a seamless manner without distortion. Truly an amazing performance.You can take it to pool parties, beach parties and stream music - as it's water-proof (from rain, small sprays & splash; don't immerse in water to listen to music under water, while swimming!!).Charging is easy (uses a micro USB, like most SmartPhones & Tablets). One charge lasts for several days. I have played music on this for almost 3 hours continuously in a birthday party. If you keep it idle, it shuts off automatically, saving it's battery power. You can use it for con-calls too, as it has a built in microphone.Last but not the least. 12000+ people have given their reviews, with 5-star rating from 73% of folks and 4-star rating from 15% of folks. Just don't bother about the 5% 1-star and 3% 2-star teams - as you will have some problems/failures with electronics. Just get this little sweet BT speaker and enjoy music.

title: Cambridge Soundworks' newest and smallest OontZ speaker doesn't disappoint - and a great value, too!
body: I've always liked the OontZ Angle line of speakers from Cambridge Soundworks for offering value-priced speakers that maintain performance and quality. When I saw that they're now offering a speaker in the popular "mini" category, I jumped at the opportunity to audition it.True to its heritage, the OontZ Clip delivers a clear and balanced sound. I'd even go so far as to call it reasonably accurate. No, you're not going to get chest-pounding bass out of it. It's a three-inch speaker in a shallow enclosure. But it does have a surprising amount of natural low-frequency response.Like any speaker, placement can make all the difference in performance, especially in the low-frequency range. For the best bass response, you'll want to place the speaker in the upright position, on a hard flat surface like a shelf or table, with the back close to or against a wall, or better yet, in a corner.It offers a pleasant and unfatiguing listen for all but the most bass-heavy genres of music. On top of that, it plays pretty loudly, too. Enough to fill a small room with foreground music, or a larger room with background music. And more than loud enough for personal, near-field listening, which is where the Clip really shines. And two Clips can be paired for true left-right stereo separation.The OontZ Clip's rounded triangle shape is an obvious nod to its Angle ancestry and is also a great form overall. The flat bottom allows it to stand upright and the overall shape is easy and comfortable to hold in one hand. The sides are rubberized, while the front and back faces are sturdy grills. The rounded corners make it easy to pack or pocket, with no sharp edges to snag on things. There's a carabiner on top for clipping to your belt, bag, or elsewhere, and a rubber strap across the back for attaching to things like tree branches, tent poles, bicycle/motorcycle/scooter handlebars, door handles, curtain rods, well, you get the idea. Oh, and did you notice it's IPx7 waterproof? That means you don't have to worry about using it by the pool, in the shower, or on your boat. It's supposed to be able to withstand being submerged for up to 30 minutes, but I don't think I'll test that myself. But accidents happen, so it's good to know.In all, this is a great value and a great-sounding speaker for the size. If you're looking for something small and ultra-portable that actually sounds good too, you can't go wrong with the OontZ Clip. I like it so much that I bought a second one for stereo. If it's anything like my experience with the Angle series, a stereo pair should sound more than twice as good as a single!

title: Sounds much better than it has any right to - amazing speaker
body: This is my first bluetooth speaker purchase, but I've heard loads of different makes and models via friends and acquaintances.I think your experience with this speaker will depend on your expectations for what it will sound like (this is mostly with regards to bass response). First, I've connected this to three different devices via bluetooth, all of various ages and different versions of Android - it generally takes about 3-5 seconds and has been fool proof. The connection distance has never been an issue - on the first day I left my tablet in our kitchen and went upstairs in our small house, and the signal never faltered through several walls. So that's pretty awesome in my book.Now, the important part: the sound. To my ear this speaker has a very even, clear response. It doesn't sound boxy AT ALL, which is a small miracle in itself. It sounds fabulous. With small speakers you worry that they'll sound tinny or boxy or lack entirely in bass response. The bass is well represented here, but it's not "shake the house" bass that you'd get from a larger, more powerful speaker. To test the speaker I listened to some reggae with pronounced bass. The speaker didn't flub out, and the notes are there and present in the music, you just aren't going to get dominant bass out of this thing. It's just not possible. But within that limitation and understanding I have zero complaints about the sound this thing puts out. It's output is clear, distortion-free, enjoyable and simply amazing.I like to use VLC media player for music on my devices and it has a graphic EQ available, which I highly recommend. It's possible to boost the mids to the point of distortion - as midrange will do - with the EQ, but by keeping the EQ curve closer to the center line and making small adjustments - a gentle "smile" curve of sorts - I've gotten excellent results. But even without the EQ the speaker still sounds great.Build quality seems to be fine. The thing gets plenty loud - not hurt-your-ears loud, but loud enough to use at a party. It sounds great outside, as well. One interesting thing - it seems to disperse sound out the back, so when I placed it on a bookshelf with a wall of books close behind it, the sound was suddenly "smaller". I moved it to a place with more space around it and the issue resolved.If we'd had something like this when I was a teenager we would've bowed down to the magic voodoo box. This is a highly compact way to take good-quality music with you. I'm in love.

title: Great Bluetooth Speaker... VERY Pleased!
body: I was very skeptical at first, the pricing seemed like a gimmick. (Paid $30 vs list of $100.) Its performance delivers as promised though. This is a great buy.I had no trouble pairing this with my Android tablet. (I have not tested the built-in microphone, on a phone, etc.)The bass response is pretty amazing. Whatever gimmick they came up with for that, it really does work. I like the way the speakers are angled when you set it on a table, etc. It really helps throw the sound in a good direction. You can stand it on end too (both ends are rubber), and it is pretty stable.The rubber flap that covers the Micro-USB charging port (short cable included) and the 3.5mm audio jack is a bit tricky to open up, but that is OK, it makes a really good seal and is not as hard once you get used to it. It is fairly sturdy too, so with proper care it will likely last a long time. (So often the charging cover is the first thing to break, for being too flimsy.)Compact and light weight. It looks and feels great. Easy to grab and carry around. (Have not given it the "splash" test, but it looks like it can take it. Impressive.) The two rubber strips on the bottom make it nearly impossible to slide around on a smooth surface, so it will not move around easily during use.The power control seems a bit stiff, but you know when you engage it, so I like that. It is extremely unlikely that you will accidentally (while packed for example) turn this speaker on or off. The play/pause and volume +/- controls are conveniently located on one end, and easy to operate (especially if standing on the other end). So is the Bluetooth control. They are all well marked, so no guesswork needed to operate this unit.A charging indicator light is present, as well as an indicator for power-on and Bluetooth connection.I have not tried using this in my (old and noisy) car, but I'm betting it is loud enough, even for that.I am exceptionally pleased with this purchase. It was delivered as promised, and came well packed.I got the suggested case too (even though it was 1/3 the cost of the speaker), and am also pleased with that.I got the suggested retractable audio cable, but you do not really need it... a cable comes with the speaker kit that is about a foot or more long.product link:[[ASIN:B013TLAVG4 [New] OontZ Angle 3 Bluetooth Portable Speaker : Louder Volume with 10W+ Power, More Bass, Weatherproof IPX5 Wireless Shower Speaker (Red - The Perfect Valentines Gift), by Cambridge SoundWorks]]

title: Size does not matter with the Oontz Angle 3
body: Upon receiving the package my initial thought was this thing is massive, but after opening the Amazon box, and getting past the air sacs I pulled out a smaller box. "What the hell is this small thing?" I did not think that I ordered a mini speaker I was hoping for something with more substance, something that could fill an entire room with my music. I proceeded to open the package and this bizarre prism shaped thing was in my hands, throwing aside the instruction manual, I proceeded to turn on the speaker, paired it easily with my iPhone, and was disappointed with how quiet my music was playing. I did a few things wrong, first my iPhone's volume was low, I turnt it up and now we were in business, but the bass was still disappointing. I had the speaker standing vertically, I placed it so the base of the triangle was down, after noticing the rubber guards. The bass improved, but still not quite what I was hoping, not enough bass, Meghan Trainor was sounding a little flat, I noticed the plus sign on the side of the Oontz and the volume started going up again and the bass was starting to rumble. We are in business! Over the last week of owning this device I've been very impressed, it is most definitely able to fill any room you want, depending on acoustics. I use this daily while showering, it is waterproof, but I noticed the bass was not as strong on porcelain surfaces, so I placed it on the counter where there is a wood surface and it seems to improve it. Friends and family members are asking about it, and when I tell them the price 4 of them have already ordered one. Battery life has been great, I still have not needed to charge the device out of the box and it has had an active time of ~6 hours. Connecting is a breeze, it has auxiliary input, charged with a micro usb cable which are hidden behind a rubber guard, I don't think aux can be used while near water though. I was really surprised at the amount of sound this thing can generate. Granted it's not going to be full range, I think the midline is diminished and upper ends are a little higher than they should be, I'm too lazy to try to adjust the equalizer in my phone. But if you are just wanting something to play music from time to time, and are going to be near water, you could drop $200 easily on a Bose, or drop less than $40 and slightly compromise sound quality. I don't use the built in microphone so I can't really tell you how well it works, but Siri does not respond from the speaker. Definitely would recommend, unless you are a seriously anal audiophile.

title: Can't find any flaws so far
body: Received the product about an hour ago. First impression, a little smaller than what I expected. You get a USB charging cable about 5 inches long, and an 3.5mm stereo audio cable about foot and half in length. Most of the speaker is covered in a rubber-like coating (two end caps and the speaker grill), which feels good and secure in hand. You press the power button to turn on and press and hold to turn off. Sound quality is as described, good and clear, with enough bass that won't distort when playing at max volume. And, it's pretty loud for its size. As a comparison, my previous Bluetooth speaker is [[ASIN:B00ZJ7C0YI Mengo AquaCube, Waterproof Speaker [3W Ultra Clear Sound] Waterproof Portable Bluetooth (4.1) Speaker - Green - Retail Packaging]]. My ears are about two to two and half feet from the speaker. I constantly need to turn both the volume on the Mengo AND my laptop to the max in order to have adequate loudness. With this OontZ Angle 3, I set the speaker to max volume then I only need to put my laptop at 25% volume. The sound is already louder than the Mengo (of course, the OontZ Angle 3 is a much bigger speaker than the Mengo). To turn it up any louder it hurts my ears. It's that loud.A few things to be noted: The charging indicator doubles as a power indicator, glows a few second when the power is turned on. When turned on the Bluetooth light will flash to search for a device to connect, and when connected it will make a chirp sound and becomes solid blue. To connect to another Bluetooth device (since it will always try to connect to the last successfully connected device), you press and hold the Bluetooth button until it makes another chirp sound, and it'll enter the search mode again. For the volume up and down button, you press and hold or press multiple times to change volume. The power/charging indicator doubles as the max/min volume indicator. When the volume is at max/min point, and you continue to hold the volume +/- buttons down, the power indicator will flash. The volume adjusts separately from your Bluetooth host's volume control, so in order to reach the absolute loudest volume you need to make sure both the host and the speaker have turned their volume to the max. The Play button doubles as a Pause button, but there are no Skip Forward/Backward buttons, so you need get back to your Bluetooth host to use those functions.Overall, solid 5 stars...

title: Detailed review.
body: Let me first start of in saying I've had quite a few bt speakers larger and small, and have had much experience in speakers themselves be it home audio and car audio. This little speaker is made by Cambridge Soundworks which is a legitimate company that has been making quality home audio speakers for many years, so needless to say they know what they are doing. This little speaker EXCEEDS expectations for it's size and price trust me, keep in mind this IS a speaker and like any other speaker it should be broken in before cranking it max volume right out of the box. Listen to it on about three quarters of max volume for around an hour or so with multiple types of frequencies before you crank it up all the to max volume. After that any time you use it you should let it "warm up" for about a song or two before cranking it again, this is and should be the case for any and all speakers you have/use home or car audio in mind. Where this speaker really shines is on top of a large wooden surface without much but the speaker itself upon it like a table or dresser, you can hear massive difference in the bass department if you compare putting on a bed or holding it in your hand while it plays and then putting in on the wooden surface. You can hear a massive difference in the mids and lows when doing so, that being said this is NOT a subwoofer and it will not shake your house or knock pictures off your walls.. and you shouldn't expect it to. However, you can definitely hear and in some cases even slightly FEEL the bass from this thing depending on the placement of the speaker and where you're sitting, play around with surfaces and positions on that surface and you'll notice the differences trust me. As for the highs and overall clarity, it shines there also. At max volume it is in fact distortion free, and it is very loud for something it's size for sure. Overall nevermind the "bang for your buck" statements, this thing outshines almost ALL the 100+ dollar "top name brand" crap in the stores that are in it's size bracket. There is a reason this thing is the number one best seller on Amazon with such good reviews compared to all the other crap "name brand" models that have poor reviews. Battery life is solid too, been getting 7-8+ hours straight. Buy it, it's less than 30 bucks and it is worth every single cent.. PERIOD.

title: *UPDATED* Excellent product! Very happy with this little guy
body: *UPDATED late November 2022* - I originally purchased this in January 2018, and nearly 5 years later (five!!) this is still going strong. This remains my single favorite purchase on Amazon and maybe my favorite purchase ever, particularly when factoring in the price and OUTSTANDING value. For its size, price, build quality, sound, and (I can now say) sheer longevity, this is a product that has lived up to its cost many times over. I've brought it on everything from ski-trips to my friends Bachelor party, and daily I use it as a small (but mighty) speaker to plug into my computer monitor for whenever I'm not using my headphones. I can't say enough good things about this product. Pick one up if you haven't already. These are still an absolute steal--especially these days when inflation has made the price of just about everything else go sky-high. You won't regret the purchase, that much I can promise!Original Review:For what you pay and how compact this awesome little speaker is, the sound and value are amazing.I bought this to replace my portable iHome speakers that finally bit the dust. It's worth noting that those died in part because of on/off switches that would often accidentally slip on in luggage, ruining the battery; they also had rather cumbersome wires.These address those issues and more, including adding a number of great little touches:-Rubberized buttons that are water resistant-The speaker automatically shuts off if no sound is coming through them to preserve battery--unless it's already plugged in; great design feature!-Great battery life, partly because of the above point, and partly because it's just a high quality, high capacity battery-Did I mention that the sound is amazing for its size and price?! Brought this to my friend's bachelor party in Mexico and it was plenty loud for the big house we rented out. No, the speaker is not a Bose 7.1 Dolby Surround home theater set up, but it shouldn't be. It's only about 5 inches wide and costs the same as a trip to the convenience store.-Bluetooth reduces the need for cords, but also works with a standard headphone jack (and comes with the cord!), which you can use to plug into a computer, for exampleAltogether it's one of the best purchases I've made in a long time.

title: Very impressed! Small but surprisingly powerful
body: I got this little Bluetooth speaker to listen to my podcasts at audio books when I’m home cooking, cleaning, working, etc. when I don’t feel like wearing a Bluetooth ear piece (I wear it all day at work, don’t want it on my head more while home) and my iPhone isn’t quite loud enough at home while I walk around doing things. It’s about the size I expected based on the customer photos (which is an awesome thing amazon allows). It’s small but has a solid quality feel. It doesn’t feel like a plastic cheap kids toy.It connected very very quickly to my phone and reconnects to it quickly as well, which is a big deal. I switch between a lot of Bluetooth devices (two Bluetooth ear pieces, one Bluetooth headphones, my cars blue tooth) so it’s a big plus for me. Some devices get fussy and you have to go into settings and totally reconnect. Just turn it on an it’s good to go.Sound. Woah. I was surprised how loud it gets. I expected it to be 20% louder than my phone and is put it up somewhere high so the sound would travel more. Pff. No need. This little thing gets surprisingly loud! I can be two rooms away and hear it just fine and it’s not on full volume.I like that it’s water resistant because I do sometimes leave it on near the shower. My phone was never loud enough on full blast to hear the radio or podcasts - but this is. It does say not to submerge, which wasn’t my intention, but it’s nice to know that if a random drop or three hits it, it won’t die.The battery life seems to last quite awhile and it does show the battery life bar on your phone (not battery life indicator on the unit beyond a blinking light when it gets to 15%). Charges quickly and uses a mini usb cord I have like 15 of for other tablets and such.Also - I have used this for a few conference calls for work. It worked really well. Everyone heard me as well as they do on the small Bluetooth earpiece. Sometimes it’s just nice not to have to have it plugged into your ear. Also - while I write reports and listen to podcasts I get random calls and this does just fine talking through them without having to switch devices or use my speakerphone, which always seems to be somewhat poor quality.Overall, really great little unit.

title: Powerful unit
body: I’m straight up telling you this thing is powerful.Forget the jbl flip; my friend has one, and forget the Bass Nyne; I own one.This cheap dinky little thing outputs quality and volume, no distortion maxed out, crisp balanenced sound stage, and fair bass.It does not reach the 90 decibels of the Bass Nyne, but at peak 85 decibels, it comes pretty dang close.The treble is piercing, stuff goes through walls and can fill two entire separate rooms with ear shattering sound without loss of clarity, the mids are also like butter.The Bass, now here’s the thing, it does not deliver as hard of Bass as the Bass Nyne or bigger units; my Bass Nyne sport was a little over $79.00, keep that in mind.But with the right phone equalizer setup, and all things maxed, this thing viberates things off my shelf with the Bass, it has a mild thump, you can legit feel the bass if you’re within 4 feet, further than that, and it’s simply present in the sound stage.I would discribe this Speaker the size of a soda can, as balanced sound stage, and a frickin bazooka tank killer the size of a soda can.How did I not get this sooner?No idea, but it’s true; no hype.I went from a Sony xplod boom box, to the Bass Nyne, and from the Nyne to the Oontz.How they can make something this small, and deliver such power; I have no idea, but they did it.Cheap, powerful, and small.This right here replaces all my prior speakers.Along with my new iPhone 8 Plus, I’m really starting to feel like the future has arrived.Also don’t even bother with the jbl flip, charge etc.This thing out preforms it, is smaller, and costs less.This thing is a hidden gem.If you must have 90 decibels, and extreme bass though so fierce it will penetrate an entire house; the Bass Nyne will do that at the best price point.For annoying people on one entire floor spanning 2 rooms and through a wall, while still feeling Bass at 4-5 feet, while still capable of ear shattering volumes, the Oontz is a straight shock trooper.This little baby is my new weapon of choice.

title: Well Built, Sounds Great, Reliable In Every Way!
body: I literally spent a few months trying to figure out the best Bluetooth speaker option for myself. I have a few Bluetooth speaker in the house that I had bought quite a while ago that actually work well, but I wanted something a tiny bit more powerful so that I could practice guitar along with it and use an amplifier and not have it sound thin or compressed or weird or whatever. The other Bluetooth speakers that I have I basically use them at the bedside streaming some ambient noises just for sleeping at night, so they're not particularly powerhouses or meant the blow my mind with epic sound.Just came quick, and was actually charged when I got to me, which was very cool. Connecting it to my phone was effortless, and every time I connected it takes mere seconds and is not a big ordeal. Extremely user-friendly, the controls on the side are easy to find even if in total darkness probably. In one of the pictures you can see the controls easily laid out, and under the power button there is a little flap that allows you to connect an external speaker with 1/8 inch cable as well as a port to power the speaker up with a USB cable that is supplied. The item is small, but has some girth to it. It's extremely well-built , and I don't have any worries about the quality or durability at all.The best part? This thing sounds absolutely amazing. You can see in the other picture the dual speakers. The primary reason my other speakers don't sound as well as this does is because they have a single speaker. This one disperses a bit more sound, and covers the spectrum of audio and gives it a really sweet, rich sound. I'm not using this at full volume or trying to have an outdoor party with it, so for my needs at a lower volume and just wanting a crystal clear sound that doesn't sound bizarre, this thing does the trick really, really well.All in all, this is an extremely well built and affordable option in the world of Bluetooth speakers. There are also cases for these items Amazon to protect your investment, I'm looking at those right now. This is such a great little device, I'm very glad that I narrowed it down to this item!

title: Fantastic bluetooth speaker for the money! Highly recommended!
body: I recently bought this bluetooth speaker for use around the house, mainly in the bedroom and bathroom. This speaker is very good for the price. I bought this for $20 with a coupon from eBay, but wanted to write a review on Amazon as it's just that good of a wireless speaker.First off, the small size makes it very portable to move around the household. I like the overall minimalistic design it matte black. The build quality seems better than its price range. The sides are a rubber material and I like the fact that I can stand the speaker on its side so that it can fit in tight spaces vertically. The volume, play/pause, and bluetooth pairing button are on one side of the speaker. The power button is on the back plus the aux and micro usb ports are under a rubber flap. The speaker is rated IPX5 water resistant, however I’ve not tested this out. It pairs quickly and reliably with multiple devices that I’ve tried, so no issues there.The sound quality is pretty good for the price. The sound volume can get pretty loud without distortion which is amazing for such a small and inexpensive speaker. Of course, the sound quality can’t really compete with other bluetooth speakers that are much more expensive, but for $20 its fairly good and I’m satisfied. I’m using it in the bedroom as an alarm clock/music player paired to an old smartphone. I liked the fact that I can set a sleep timer on my smartphone and 15 minutes after the music stops, the speaker automatically shuts off to save battery power. Since the volume can get quite loud, its good in the bathroom while showering.The manual states up to 7 hours of use and that seems to be about right depending on volume level. The speaker can be used also while it’s charging as well. The user manual is actually comprehensive and lists all of the various features and how-to’s which is nice and unexpected in this price range. Overall, I’m very pleased with this speaker, especially considering that it’s inexpensive, sounds good and loud, and very portable/versatile. This is a speaker that I can highly recommend and I feel that it’s a flat-out a bargain for $20.

title: UPDATE: 2nd device, catastrophic failure this time
body: FINAL UPDATE: I must say, the Oontz customer service is phenomenal. They have resolved the issue and I am now satisified with this great speaker. They promptly sent a third speaker and this one had zero defects and has been working great for over a week now. I've used it multiple times a day, and every time I turn it on the bluetooth automatically connects with my phone. Good job Oontz, both on a great speaker at a very reasonable price and on your customer service team. Thank you.UPDATE: I initially reviewed this speaker at 2 stars because the bluetooth was not functional. I received a replacement and the bluetooth so far has worked flawlessly. However, I noticed the charging LED did not illuminate. I maneuvered the speaker a bit and could see the light illuminate briefly and I assumed the LED was not soldered correctly. I have the soldering iron and skills so I figured I would rate it at 4 stars due to poor QC but it is a good speaker and I could reattach the LED. WRONG. Catastrophic failure. The LED was soldered just fine. It was the micro USB charging port. The solder joint had seperated and the speaker would only charge if I put pressure on one side of the plug (forcing the micro USB connecter onto the PCB). I have spent far too much time tinkering with this speaker. It sounds great, but I wish I could get one that functioned as advertised. I'm very disappointed and Cambridge SoundWorks needs to closely examine their manufacturer in China.OLD: I received a replacement speaker because of my previously mentioned Bluetooth troubles. I've used the new speaker 3 times now and the Bluetooth has not skipped at all. With that said, the second speaker's power LED was not soldered correctly and will not illuminate. I was able to confirm it does at least charge though. I've upgraded my review to 4 stars because the new speaker is working as intended other than the cosmetic LED problem. At the price point, it is deserving of 5 stars but because I've received 2 defective units I will not give it a perfect score. Fix the QC at the plant and this device is a great speaker for the price.

title: It came right on time and sounded amazing right out of the box
body: I bought this December of '15 because I got a new job being a bus driver for my university and we can use Bluetooth speakers to play music. It came right on time and sounded amazing right out of the box. It has great sound with plenty of bass, and the little rubber feet on the bottom are essential for putting it on any flat surface. I used the speaker every day for about 2 months, I used it in my car because that radio had gone out and also on the bus, so at least 4 hrs every week day. Then it stopped charging, I was rather frustrated because I had only had it for about a month and a half or two months and there was no reason for it to have stopped. So I went to the Cambridge SoundWorks website and EMAILED them with my story of what happened and how I really liked it but was disappointing that it stopped working. WITHIN THE HOUR I had a response from an actual person, not an automated message confirming that my email had been received, the polite lady asked me for my shipping address and with no questions asked, except for my amazon invoice number, she sent me a brand spanking new speaker!! I got the new one a few days later, it was rather quiet on my bus for those few days, and it worked perfectly just like the other one I did, now 5 months later I am still satisfied with my purchase and with the customer service of Cambridge SoundWorks. I will definitively recommend them to all of my friends who are looking for a speaker and will continue to buy from them because they have great products and amazing customer service. This speaker also has amazing volume without any distortion, I use it on my johnboat with a small outboard and I can still hear it perfectly. I also used it at a party I had in my backyard, which is pretty big for a back yard probably 30 yards squared. I hooked it up to my computer with the aux cord that came with it and with the charger then plugged my computer in to charge and it played music at just about full blast all night. The volume was perfect for about 30 people in the back yard and it didn't disturb the neighbors. All in all a great little speaker and well worth the money.

title: 4.5★★★★Stars
body: Revised review 3/2/17:After my initial review I received a comment from SoundWorks. I got in touch with them and they assisted me right away. I found out the original item I purchased "Cambridge SoundWorks OontZ Angle 3 Speaker" was damaged during delivery and that's why it didn't work. They offered me a replacement and included a case for it.When I received my item I tested it with all my devices as for that was the reason why I had returned it in the first place my original item only sink with one device. I have at least six devices; 2 Iphones, 1 android, 1 tablet, 2 kindle fire devices. I was extremely happy to find out that this speaker's Bluetooth sinking option worked just perfectly fine with all of these devices.Not only did this Cambridge Soundworks OontZ Angle 3 Speaker worked but also I noticed the sound on this item was much better than the first one I had originally purchase. The second thing I also noticed was that this speaker comes with a cord that you can also connect to your computer or laptop if they do not have Bluetooth capability. In my case both my laptop and my work computer sound are sadly low and do not have Bluetooth and when I tried this I about cried. I was so thrilled that I was also able to use it on these two devices and I feel like I am in heaven since music is my life having a speaker that brings your music loud and back to life is everything to me.After this incredible experience with this company I can tell you I will be purchasing additional ones so I can keep one at my office, one to keep in my vehicle and one to keep at home. You can't beat this quality for the price you pay. Thank you SoundWorks for your amazing customer service that speaks volumes about your company.If you are looking for a good, affordable Bluetooth speaker and a great customer service do yourself a favor and purchase this item. I can honestly tell you that my experience has been great and I know that if this device messes up I know is just an email away and they will make sure that I am satisfy with their products.

title: Perfect for what I need!
body: I just purchased this if I find any issues I will add them later. I love to fish and I take my iPod to the dock with me and while I wade and fish I listen to music. I had a pair of cheap speakers that plugged into my iPod they sounded okay, but you really couldn't hear them over the water. I thought Okay I'll give this a try, I read a couple of the bad reviews and figured well they didn't do something right. I plugged it in and let it charge It took the Bluetooth instantly. so no real setup needed the first thing I noted in the directions and I am going on a limb here it is not windows friendly. It is however Apple friendly and works with all my devices. Now I can't say for sure, about windows since I avoid them like the plague but that's me. The directions even try to explain what do do for window users. I read some of the better reviews and figures for 25 bucks I can't go to wrong.I am glad I spent it, first they are nice and loud, for tiny speakers I was impressed by the quality of the audio output they had. I listen to a wide variety of rock, and these speakers handled everything I played even the bass was incredible! Now I'm a sound nut, I like true stereo sound not the crap they push as sound these days. so I was pleasantly surprised when these lived up to the reviews I had read. Now don't think these are waterproof they are far from it so I don't suggest showering since the bottom is full of holes. They are splash resistant which means if you have them by the pool and your wee one splashes some water on them they won't fall apart.For as small as it is it is heavy so it won't move around a lot, I tested them the other day when I went fishing on my lake and loved the fact I could walk most of the water and still hear them. so the audio is plenty loud. These will be perfect and well used when we go camping this winter and next summer.My iPod is one of the second generations and at the top it even has a battery bar showing you the charge of the speakers battery, since it works with that I think any good Bluetooth will work with these speakers.

title: WOW!!! What a rich sounding, full bass, affordable speaker. Buy it!
body: WOW!!! I love this speaker. I was looking for a small Bluetooth speaker to place on my headboard to pair with my older flatscreen TV. It is a Sony and has pretty good built in speakers but it is across the room and when at volume to hear in the bed, the audio can be clearly heard in the hall and adjoining room when the doors are open. I wanted a more immersive sound experience while watching TV in bed and boy did I get it. The sound that comes from this small, unobtrusive speaker knocks my expectations out of the park. There is an incredible depth and balance of tones. I love the clarity especially when I turn the volume down at night as I sleep with the TV on. This speaker is plugged in and used essentially 24 hours a day. I did test the speaker at a volume comfortable when listening approximately 2 foot away to see what kind of battery life it had and it was over 24 hours. The manual states that at higher volume there is shorter battery life. This speaker can fill the 120 sq/ft room easily at higher volumes. We thought that we would I initially want to pair another and have one on each side of the bed but it is totally unnecessary. One of the features I enjoy the best is the that the volume buttons are a raised “+” and “-“ symbol that allows for ease of volume control in the dark. I have not used this speaker with a phone or ipad so I cannot speak to the features that allow the speaker to be used to answer calls or to play and search through music from a portable device so I cannot speak to its performance of these tasks. I had had previous experience with Cambridge Soundworks products and I believe you cannot go wrong with their products. I did have to use an optical output to Bluetooth adapter on my TV since it was not Bluetooth enabled. I was a little concerned that it might be too technical for me but the instructions were clear and the speaker immediately paired with no problem. The wireless adapter that I used was Taotronics model TT-BA09. This adapter does come with a male to male optical audio cable. It was not clear whether it was included.

title: A No-Brainer If You Are Looking for a Mini Bluetooth Speaker
body: I needed to replace my mini bluetooth speaker that was probably a good dozen years old. It was dirt cheap but served me well aside from the grill kept following off even after several super glue repairs. I took it with me everywhere along with my Kindle Fire HDX 8.9. I always knew it wasn't the greatest but kept telling myself it was good enough.When the time came that I was positively going to replace my old one, I turned to the Amazon review system to help me research and decide on a new one. The Oontz Angle 3 (3rd Gen) was one of the first to come up in a search. The sleek-looking triangular shape caught my attention and, though it is twice as big as my previous one, this seemed to be more than small enough to be portable.The first thing that caught my attention out of the box was how cool it looked. Then I had to remind myself that looking cool wasn't the most important thing. I thoroughly read the user's manual that came with it and had no issue pairing it up to my Kindle Fire by following said instructions in a matter of seconds.So hearing this speaker for the first time after listening with my old speaker for so long was a noticeable improvement in overall clarity. The sound was so crisp and clear and seemingly 10 times louder if I turned it up high enough.It comes with a USB connection for charging on any USB charger that will connect to it. I use an Amazon fast charger and it's fine. It seems like it will get about 12 hours on a full charge though I haven't run the battery all the way down. The volume control is on the left hand side. You can also change tracks with the same arrows by holding them down longer.One feature I didn't even realize until I bought it is the fact that it has a built-in microphone so you can pair it with your cell phone (if bluetooth compatible) or the Amazon Alexa.I highly recommend this bluetooth mini speaker as I think it is a bargain for the price I bought it. There's probably better but you likely will pay at least double.

title: small but very powerful and convenient
body: I haven't had a good portable speaker like this for my lap top in a while.. on my previous 3 speaker sets, the wires always eventually come loose and the speakers end up breaking form all the moving around i do, because i like to move my lap top to listen to tunes in my shop.. WIRES NO MORE!!!.. i can Bluetooth this speaker to my phone or my lap top and just carry it anywhere in my house.. i have a three story house and the reach distance was amazing.. it never cut out going up to the attic rooms steaming from the basement.. the pause button works for you tube videos. i have a slight hassle switching from my lap top to the phone. i usually end up clicking the remove button from my laptop because i cant get it to just disconnect.. its faster to just remove it and then add it again when im back on my lap top. im going to buy another one especially for the lap top since its hard to disconnect. its very small and both speakers are built into the one unit which makes it sooo convenient for me.. you can see i brought it into my sew shop. the sound is amazing.. the bass is nice and i love my R & B old school.. im usually turning it down now instead of wishing they would be louder.. my old wires connected speakers don't compare to this.. the VOontZ Angle 3 is really nice and clean design and the tri-design makes it easy to carry from place to place- it doesn't take up hardly any space and i can prop my cell phone up against it as I watch videos in bed or listen to my meditations- i can move the speaker next to my pillow and turn it down low so Im the only one that can hear.. The speaker phone/mic does work well when its connected to my smart phone. the embossed controls are easy to braille/touch/find without looking. The volume control can be controlled by either the speaker or connected device. The charge lasts longer than the manual says. the charge is quick as well.. id say its a great all around portable speaker with a booming sound for working out or dancing in the living room... or sewing in the shop!!! nice product.. very pleased.

title: One of the very best portable BT speakers – 4.95 stars
body: Much has already been written about the OontZ Angle 3 already, so my review will be fairly short.I bought this speaker in August 2018 and have used it regularly since. It's very well made, has very good battery life, and very good volume with *very* little distortion. It also has incredible range, playing perfectly clear at 100'. It has 3/5mm jack for AUX input, and a micro-USB port for charging. (I've only seen one speaker with USB-C charging.) It also has an Auto-Shut off when it's signal source times-out.But where the Angle 3 truly excels is in it's audio quality. So many BT speakers I've tried – the Brava Mini, SoundCore 2, JBL Clip 2, and a few more – have a poor aural balance. They're bass heavy, which is supposed to impart a "warmth", a "richer" sound quality. But this is almost always done at the expense of midrange clarity, muddying it as the boosted bass creeps too far into the midrange.That bass heaviness of other speakers generally acceptable for more casual listening of music, but it adversely impacts voice intelligibility, especially at low volume. This can be extremely annoying when listening to audiobooks, giving an unnatural timbre to the voice and inducing fatigue with extended listening. It can also be a hinderance when trying to discern lyrics of some music genres or watching video.I'm not an audiophile by any means and don't expect that level of sound quality from a portable BT speaker. The aforementioned speakers are all quite serviceable in a number of circumstances and may have features preferred over the Angle 3 that make them a better choice for many listeners.But none of them come close to the OontZ Angle 3 in audio performance. It's a clear standout in pleasing audio whether it be music or spoken word. If clear natural pleasant audio is your main consideration in a portable BT speaker, you can't do any better than the Angle 3 unless you get the Angle 3 Ultra, or spend a *lot* more money in the genre.

title: Great Bang for your Buck...
body: I have the Oontz Angle 3 Raindance. Be realistic about your expectations. It's a sub $30 speaker. It doesn't sound nearly as good as my JBL Charge 3, but that's a $120+ speaker. But it does, in my opinion, sound better than the Anker Soundcore 2, a $40 speaker, which I also owned for a short time before returning it. The Anker just sounded.....small and bright. Not much bass that the Soundcore 2 advertises with its "Bass Up" technology. I also didn't hear what other reviewers were raving about with the Soundcore 2's sound. I did A LOT of research on these 2 speakers. I needed something way more portable than my JBL, something I wouldn't stress out about if I lost or broke it. For a small, cheap speaker, it's well built, very portable, and the battery & connectivity are outstanding. The sound can fill a small to medium sized room in a typical house, maybe not a mansion, but it does pretty well. Most importantly, it handles most music pretty good: Well balanced, and the bass is really, not bad at all, even at around 70-80% volume. I'd say it sounds best at 70% or lower. It even sounds decent outside while doing yard work or if you have a small gathering. If you want something super loud, pay more or get some bluetooth headphones.Is it something your going to blast Snoop Dogg, Tech 9, or even Sade (who is suprisingly bassy), expecting the windows in your room to shake? Nope. But it also doesn't sound like it's straining either. I personally think NO speaker sounds good cranked up to 100%. The Oontz mixes those sounds accordingly to accomodate for it's small size. You can also find and download free equalizer apps and tweak the sound a little more. It advertises the ability to "daisy chain" 2 speakers at once, but that's not really my bag.If you're expecting something to blow your hair back while feeling the music thump in your chest, you have to spend significantly more. But like I said, you'll get the most bang for your buck with this little speaker.

title: Hands down the best sounding bluetooth speaker for $30!!!
body: This is hands down the best sounding bluetooth speaker for $30 out there on the market today. I have never heard another portable speaker of this size (other than the original jambox, which cost WAY more) sound as good & have the bass extension & accuracy that this one does. No, the bass will certainly not blow you out of the water in terms of volume, but it does give you a small amount of NATURAL sounding bass that goes surprisingly deep for a unit of this size (try putting your ear to the subwoofer on the bottom, you'd be surprised at the quality of bass coming out!). It's also surprisingly loud if you use it with your iPhone Music EQ set to "flat" — it's amazing how much volume this little unit can handle!Next thing I love about this unit is the aesthetics. First off, it comes in RED!!! (my favorite color) — five different colors actually if you count black. It's a little too thick to comfortably fit in your pants pocket, but it's still definitely small enough to throw in a purse, or jacket for that matter. The triangle design also gives you the perfect listening angle when oriented as pictured and the placement of the subwoofer on the bottom really helps the bass response when you place it on a flat surface that can resonate.I should mention the first unit I got was defective — for some reason it wouldn't even turn on unless I had it plugged into the wall. Even then, the volume was substantially softer than it should be and would distort very easily. However, Amazon made it was a breeze to return and I had a replacement unit in no time, so hopefully that was just a bad apple.One more thing — try setting your iPhone Music EQ to "R&B" & then turning up the volume a little to compensate. While this will decrease your overall headroom, I found this setting to substantially improve the sound of the Angle 3 by muting the harsh upper midrange frequencies & leaving you with a nice "hi-fi" bass & treble boost.

title: Such good. Much water resistant. Very audio.
body: I've never heard of this brand before and don't even know how to pronounce OontZ, but let me tell you, this little thing does not disappoint. I have only positive things to say about it:1) It's super compact. Smaller than I though it would be. You can easily carry it in one hand.2) It's surprisingly and sufficiently loud for its size. It will put out enough volume to listen to in a large room, and even more so in a smaller room with good acoustics. This thing packs some power for such a portable size. As far as sound quality goes, if you're an audiophile like me, this speaker obviously isn't going to sound like your high-end Bose system, but it's perfect for spoken word audio.3) The bass is surprisingly great for its size. I was expecting somewhat of a tinny sound, like most small speakers are limited to, but this speaker has a very decent low end.4) It's water resistant! I use this speaker in the shower every day. You can easily pair your Bluetooth device to it, set the volume, take it in the shower for your listening pleasure, then easily take it out, dry it off a bit, and move it to your next location seamlessly.5) A 3.5mm aux input is just icing on the cake in case you can't or don't want to use Bluetooth, e.g., when I'm at my computer, which has no speakers, I can plug in the 3.5mm cord and listen to audio from my computer without wasting battery on my phone using Bluetooth to the speaker.6) It actually comes with clear English instructions, not some broken instructions that look like they were written by a Mongolian dyslexic.There is ONE dislike I have, albeit very minor: This speaker does not have forward/backward buttons for skipping tracks and fast forward/rewind function. It would be nice, but it does have the other necessary buttons, i.e., volume, pause, and Bluetooth pairing.Overall, I am extremely satisfied with this speaker. Cambridge Soundworks has a winning product right here. Get it now.

title: The Caseling OontZ Angle 3 Hard Case is the perfect carrying case for the Cambridge OontZ Angle 3 speaker
body: The Caseling OontZ Angle 3 Hard Case is the perfect carrying case for the Cambridge OontZ Angle 3 speaker. There is enough room in the bottom of the case to store a dual port USB charger and a high quality 3' charging cable. BUT ... you have to buy the right charger and micro USB cable to fit them in correctly. Size is the determining factor. There is not much room for both a charger and a quality cable. The iKits Dual Port 3.4A USB Charger AC Wall Charger/Travel Charger is the ONLY one that is thin enough to fit in the bottom compartment of the Caseling OontZ Angle 3 Hard Case. It's a perfect fit. The Anker PowerLine Micro USB (3 ft) - Durable Charging Cable, with Kevlar Fiber and 10000+ Bend Lifespan is the perfect choice for the cable because you will have to wind it up for it to fit perfectly inside. This cable can handle heavy duty use and bending. The Caseling OontZ Angle 3 Hard Case, the iKits charger and the Anker PowerLine 3' cable are all available on Amazon. If you're looking for an "everything in one case" solution ... the Caseling OontZ Angle 3 Hard Case is the one to buy. The iKits Dual Port 3.4A USB charger is more than capable of charging BOTH the Oontz Angle 3 speaker and your Android or Apple device at the same time. All you need is a 2nd charging cable.This is an unbiased review. I did NOT receive any of these items free or at a discount. I paid full price on Amazon just like you will. Here are the Amazon links for the case, the charger and the cable shown in the photos.https://www.amazon.com/OontZ-Portable-Wireless-Bluetooth-Speakers/dp/B00ZVICXVU/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1469278918&sr=8-1&keywords=Caseling+OontZ+Angle+3+Hard+Casehttps://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B019ERG3QO/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1https://www.amazon.com/Anker-PowerLine-Micro-USB-3ft/dp/B012VWH6PQ/ref=sr_1_3?s=wireless&ie=UTF8&qid=1469195688&sr=1-3&keywords=anker+powerline+micro+usb

body: Hola everyone...Happy 4th of July. GBA.I received this product on time, in fact on the early date promissed which was great. The box to start made me get a bit concernd duento the size. title: For what you get for what you pay, this is fantastic.
body: For what you get for what you pay, this is fantastic.I have tried to get good, quality sound for my outside events in my backyard and my workshop. I hooked up some older speakers to my player, and I got good sound, but there were tangled cords everywhere, and getting everything back inside from the yard was a chore.This little device cut the cord, so to speak, on all that.It is loud enough for the patio or firepit, and can be adjusted to taste using the controls.Set-up was absurdly simple. I turned on my device, turned on Bluetooth, then powered up the OontZ. Within a few seconds the two had paired, and the music began playing through the speaker.I've switched around to different devices in the garage, the patio, and my office, and in each instance the method for pairing / re-pairing work perfectly.The volume controls are easy to use, as is the on/off button. The device can be recharged using a micro-USB connection (one is provided) and it can be paired with a non-Bluetooth device using the provided micro RCA connector (same as connecting a device to a typical stereo or computer). [ETA: Not sure on the name of the RCA jack, whether it's "micro" or "mini", but it's the one you'd use to connect headphones on your smartphone.]Sound quality is great for this small speaker, and it can be laid down on a table to enhance the bass, or stood upright for a brighter sound. There are no other controls for adjusting sound quality, but for parties and outdoor social events, it's perfectly fine.Yes, I was skeptical when I ordered it and thought it would be tinny or too quiet, but it is good-quality and loud enough for my uses.I have not tried larger devices, such as boombox-size Bluetooth speakers, so I imagine that you can get even louder sound, but for this price, this is a steal.
